010727.english: And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell: and great was the fall of it.
010727.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏧᎦᎿᎭᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏚᏃᎱᎦᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏃᎸᏁᎢ, ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᏥᏚᏱᎶᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏲᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᎦᏎᏗᏳ ᏥᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎤᏲᏨ.
010727.phonetic: Hna-quo-no tsu-ga-hna-ne-i, a-le tsi-du-no-hu-ga-ne-i, a-le tsu-no-lv-ne-i, na-hna a-da-ne-lv tsi-du-yi-lo-le-i, a-le tsu-yo-tse-i, a-le u-s-ga-se-di-yu tsi-nu-li-s-ta-ne u-yo-tsv.

010728.english: And it came to pass, when Jesus had ended these sayings, the people were astonished at his doctrine:
010728.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏍᏆᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎ ᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬᎢ.
010728.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, na-s-gi Tsi-sa u-s-qua-dv na-s-gi ni-ga-we-s-gv-i, u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se da-de-yo-hv-s-gv-i.

010729.english: For he taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
010729.cherokee: ᎤᏤᎵᎦᏰᏃ ᏥᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎪ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎥᏝᏃ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩᏉ ᏥᏓᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎪ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ.
010729.phonetic: U-tse-li-ga-ye-no tsi-da-de-yo-hv-s-go i-yu-s-di ge-se-i, v-tla-no di-no-we-li-s-gi-quo tsi-da-na-de-yo-hv-s-go i-yu-s-di yi-ge-se-i.

010801.english: When he was come down from the mountain, great multitudes followed him.

010802.english: And, behold, there came a leper and worshipped him, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
010802.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎠᏓᏰᏍᎩ ᎤᏢᎩ ᎤᎷᏤ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏱᏣᏓᏅᏖᎸ ᏰᎵᏉ ᏱᏍᎩᏅᎦᎸ.
010802.phonetic: A-le gv-ni-yu-quo a-da-ye-s-gi u-tlv-gi u-lu-tse u-da-do-li-s-da-ne-le-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, i-yu-no yi-tsa-da-nv-te-lv ye-li-quo yi-s-gi-nv-ga-lv.

010803.english: And Jesus put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, I will; be thou clean. And immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
010803.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏙᏯᏅᎯᏛ ᎤᏒᏂᎴᎢ, ᎦᏓᏅᏛᎵ, ᏣᏓᏅᎦᎸᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎤᏬᏎᎴᎢ; ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᏓᏰᏍᎩ ᎤᏢᎬ ᎤᏓᏅᎦᎸᎮᎢ.
010803.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-do-ya-nv-hi-dv u-sv-ni-le-i, ga-da-nv-dv-li, tsa-da-nv-ga-lv-dv ge-se-s-di, u-wo-se-le-i; gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv a-da-ye-s-gi u-tlv-gv u-da-nv-ga-lv-he-i.

010804.english: And Jesus saith unto him, See thou tell no man; but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
010804.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏥᏌ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; Ꭷ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᏥᎯᏃᏁᎵ, ᎮᎾᏉᏍᎩᏂ, ᏫᏣᎪᏩᏛ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗ ᏫᎲᎦ ᎼᏏ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎠᎵᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏙᏗ.
010804.phonetic: Hi-a-no Tsi-sa nu-we-se-le-i; Ka tle-s-di gi-lo tsi-hi-no-ne-li, he-na-quo-s-gi-ni, wi-tsa-go-wa-dv a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi, a-le a-li-s-go-lv-do-di wi-hv-ga Mo-si u-ne-tsv a-li-s-go-lv-do-di na-s-gi u-na-do-le-ho-hi-s-do-di.

010805.english: And when Jesus was entered into Capernaum, there came unto him a centurion, beseeching him,
010805.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎨᏆᏂ ᏫᎤᎷᏨ, ᎤᎷᏤᎴ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᎫᏈ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᎤᏍᏗᏰᏔᏁᎢ,
010805.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa Ge-qua-ni wi-u-lu-tsv, u-lu-tse-le a-s-go-hi-gu-qui a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi di-ti-ni-do-hi, u-s-di-ye-ta-ne-i,

010806.english: And saying, Lord, my servant lieth at home sick of the palsy, grievously tormented.
010806.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᎦᏅᎦ ᏗᏇᏅᏒ ᎤᏢᎦ ᎤᎸᏓᎸᎥᏍᎦ ᎤᏣᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᎩᎵᏲᎦ.
010806.phonetic: Hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, tsi-nv-si-da-s-di ga-nv-ga di-que-nv-sv u-tlv-ga u-lv-da-lv-v-s-ga u-tsa-ta-nv-hi a-gi-li-yo-ga.

010807.english: And Jesus saith unto him, I will come and heal him.

010808.english: The centurion answered and said, Lord, I am not worthy that thou shouldest come under my roof: but speak the word only, and my servant shall be healed.
010808.cherokee: ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈᏃ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᏗᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏝᏰᎵ ᏱᏂᎪᎢ ᏥᏁᎸ ᏣᏴᏍᏗᏱ; ᎯᏁᎩᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏩᏒ, ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗᏃ ᏓᏳᏗᏩᏏ.
010808.phonetic: A-s-go-hi-tsu-qui-no di-ti-ni-do-hi di-u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-i; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, tla-ye-li yi-ni-go-i tsi-ne-lv tsa-yv-s-di-yi; hi-ne-gi-quo-s-gi-ni u-wa-sv, tsi-nv-si-da-s-di-no da-yu-di-wa-si.

010809.english: For I am a man under authority, having soldiers under me: and I say to this man, Go, and he goeth; and to another, Come, and he cometh; and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth it.
010809.cherokee: ᎠᏴᏰᏃ ᏗᏆᏓᏁᎶᏗᏉ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎩᎧᎭ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎬᏆᏁᎶᏙᎢ, ᎩᎶᏃ ᎮᎾ ᏥᏲᏏ, ᎡᎪᎢ; ᏅᏩᏓᎴᏃ ᎡᎮᎾ ᏥᏲᏏ, ᏗᎡᎪᎢ; ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗᏃ, ᎯᎠ ᎿᎭᏛᎦ ᏥᏲᏏ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏛᏁᎰᎢ.
010809.phonetic: A-yv-ye-no di-qua-da-ne-lo-di-quo, a-le da-gi-ka-ha a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi na-s-gi de-gv-qua-ne-lo-do-i, gi-lo-no He-na tsi-yo-si, e-go-i; nv-wa-da-le-no E-he-na tsi-yo-si, di-e-go-i; tsi-nv-si-da-s-di-no, hi-a hna-dv-ga tsi-yo-si, na-s-gi na-dv-ne-ho-i.

010810.english: When Jesus heard it, he marvelled, and said to them that followed, Verily I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.
010810.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏛ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎥᏝ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎢᏏᎵ ᏚᏙᎥ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏯᎩᏩᏛᎲ.
010810.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-dv-ga-nv u-s-qua-ni-go-se-i, a-le hi-a ni-du-we-se-le Nv-da-gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-dv, u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha, v-tla na-s-quo I-si-li du-do-v na-s-gi i-ga-i go-hi-yu-di ge-sv ya-gi-wa-dv-hv.

010811.english: And I say unto you, That many shall come from the east and west, and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, in the kingdom of heaven.
010811.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏗᎧᎸᎬ ᎠᎴ ᏭᏕᎵᎬ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏗᏂᎶᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎠᎾᏅᎥᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎡᏆᎭᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᏏᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏤᎦᏈ, ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ,
010811.phonetic: A-le hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; U-ni-tsa-ti di-ka-lv-gv a-le wu-de-li-gv i-di-tlv di-ni-lo-s-ge-s-di, a-le i-tsu-la-ha a-na-nv-v-s-ge-s-di E-qua-ha-mi, a-le E-si-gi, a-le Tse-ga-qui, ga-lv-la-di e-hi na-hna u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i,

010812.english: But the children of the kingdom shall be cast out into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
010812.cherokee: ᎦᎸᎳᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᎡᎯ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᎡ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏓᎨᏥᏄᎪᏫᏏ ᏫᏙᏓᎨᎦᏓᎡᏏ ᏧᎵᏏᎬ ᏙᏱᏗᏢ, ᎾᎿᎭᏓᏂᏴᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏂᎸᏓᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏓᏂᏄᏙᎬᎢ.
010812.phonetic: Ga-lv-la-di-s-gi-ni e-hi na-hna u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-e a-ne-hi da-ge-tsi-nu-go-wi-si wi-do-da-ge-ga-da-e-si tsu-li-si-gv do-yi-di-tlv, na-hna da-ni-yv-ge-s-di, a-le da-ni-lv-da-gi-s-ge-s-di da-ni-nu-do-gv-i.

010813.english: And Jesus said unto the centurion, Go thy way; and as thou hast believed, so be it done unto thee. And his servant was healed in the selfsame hour.
010813.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ; ᎮᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏦᎯᏳᏅ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏫᏂᏣᎵᏍᏓᏏ.  ᎤᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗᏃ ᎾᎯᏳᏉ ᎤᏗᏩᏎᎢ.
010813.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a nu-we-se-le a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi; He-na, a-le nu-s-dv tso-hi-yu-nv na-s-gi wi-ni-tsa-li-s-da-si.  U-nv-si-da-s-di-no na-hi-yu-quo u-di-wa-se-i.

010814.english: And when Jesus was come into Peter's house, he saw his wife's mother laid, and sick of a fever.
010814.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎦᏁᎸ ᎤᎷᏨ, ᎤᎪᎮ ᏈᏓ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ ᎤᏥ ᎦᏅᎨ ᎤᏗᎴᎲᏍᎨᎢ.
010814.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no Qui-da ga-ne-lv u-lu-tsv, u-go-he Qui-da u-da-li-i u-tsi ga-nv-ge u-di-le-hv-s-ge-i.

010815.english: And he touched her hand, and the fever left her: and she arose, and ministered unto them.
010815.cherokee: ᎤᏒᏂᎴᏃ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ, ᎤᏗᎴᎲᏍᎬᏃ ᎤᏗᏩᎭᎢ.  ᏚᎴᏁᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏍᏕᎸᎯᏙᎴᎢ.
010815.phonetic: U-sv-ni-le-no u-wo-ye-ni, u-di-le-hv-s-gv-no u-di-wa-ha-i.  Du-le-ne-no a-le du-s-de-lv-hi-do-le-i.

010816.english: When the even was come, they brought unto him many that were possessed with devils: and he cast out the spirits with his word, and healed all that were sick:
010816.cherokee: ᎤᏒᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ, ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏯᎢ ᏕᎬᏩᏘᏃᎮᎴᎢ; ᏚᏄᎪᏫᏎᏃ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎧᏁᎬᏉ ᎬᏗᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ ᏚᏅᏩᏁᎢ.
010816.phonetic: U-sv-no nu-li-s-ta-nv, u-ni-tsa-te a-ni-s-gi-na di-gv-wa-ni-ya-i de-gv-wa-ti-no-he-le-i; du-nu-go-wi-se-no a-ni-s-gi-na ka-ne-gv-quo gv-di-s-ge-i, a-le ni-ga-dv tsu-ni-tlv-gi du-nv-wa-ne-i.

010817.english: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying, Himself took our infirmities, and bare our sicknesses.
010817.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏗᏱ ᎠᏰᎸᏒᎩ ᎢᏌᏯ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᏧᏁᏤᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎤᏩᏒ ᎢᎩᎩᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ ᏗᏗᏩᎾᎦᎳᎯᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏕᎩᏢᎬᏃ ᏧᏤᎵ ᏂᏚᏩᏁᎸᎩ.
010817.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-di-yi a-ye-lv-sv-gi I-sa-ya a-do-le-ho-s-gi tsu-ne-tse-i, hi-a tsi-nu-we-se-i; U-wa-sv i-gi-gi-s-da-ne-lv-gi di-di-wa-na-ga-la-hi-yu ge-sv-i, de-gi-tlv-gv-no tsu-tse-li ni-du-wa-ne-lv-gi.

010818.english: Now when Jesus saw great multitudes about him, he gave commandment to depart unto the other side.
010818.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏚᎪᎲ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏴᏫ ᎬᏩᏜᏓᏫᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᏁᏤ ᎢᏍᎪᏅ ᏭᏂᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
010818.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no du-go-hv u-ni-tsa-ti yv-wi gv-wa-dla-wi-s-di-s-gv-i, u-ne-tse i-s-go-nv wu-ni-lo-hi-s-di-yi.

010819.english: And a certain scribe came, and said unto him, Master, I will follow thee whithersoever thou goest.
010819.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏗᎪᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᎤᎷᏤᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᏓᎬᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎵ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎮᏙᎲᎢ.
010819.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di di-go-we-li-s-gi u-lu-tse-i, hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi da-gv-s-da-wa-di-do-li ni-ga-v he-do-hv-i.

010820.english: And Jesus saith unto him, The foxes have holes, and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.
010820.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏧᎳ ᏚᏂᏔᎴᏐᎢ, ᏥᏍᏆᏃ ᎦᎸᎶ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏚᎾᏁᏍᏓᏝᎰᎢ, ᏴᏫᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎥᏝ ᎢᎸᎯᏢ ᎠᏍᎪᎵ ᎤᏗᏱ ᏱᎩ.
010820.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Tsu-la du-ni-ta-le-so-i, tsi-s-qua-no ga-lv-lo a-ne-hi du-na-ne-s-da-tla-ho-i, yv-wi-s-gi-ni U-we-tsi v-tla i-lv-hi-tlv a-s-go-li u-di-yi yi-gi.

010821.english: And another of his disciples said unto him, Lord, suffer me first to go and bury my father.
010821.cherokee: ᏅᏩᏓᎴᏃ ᎤᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎢᎬᏱ ᎨᎾ ᏥᏂᏌᏄᎦ ᎡᏙᏓ.
010821.phonetic: Nv-wa-da-le-no u-s-da-wa-di-do-hi hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, i-gv-yi ge-na tsi-ni-sa-nu-ga e-do-da.

010822.english: But Jesus said unto him, Follow me; and let the dead bury their dead.
010822.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᎭᎴᎢ; ᏍᎩᏍᏓᏩᏚᎦ, ᎤᎾᏁᎳᎩᏃ ᏧᏂᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᏓᏂᏂᏏᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏧᎾᏤᎵ ᏧᏂᏲᎱᏒᎯ.
010822.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a nu-we-ha-le-i; S-gi-s-da-wa-du-ga, u-na-ne-la-gi-no tsu-ni-yo-hu-sv-hi da-ni-ni-si-s-ge-s-di tsu-na-tse-li tsu-ni-yo-hu-sv-hi.

010823.english: And when he was entered into a ship, his disciples followed him.

010824.english: And, behold, there arose a great tempest in the sea, insomuch that the ship was covered with the waves: but he was asleep.
010824.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏱᎶᎴ ᎤᏃᎴ ᎢᏓᎵ; ᏥᏳᏰᏃ ᎠᎹ ᏓᎵᏍᏗᎳᏁᎬ ᎤᏭᏢᎥᏍᎨᎢ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᎦᎵᎮᏉ.
010824.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo-no u-tsa-ti u-yi-lo-le u-no-le i-da-li; tsi-yu-ye-no a-ma da-li-s-di-la-ne-gv u-wu-tlv-v-s-ge-i; a-se-no i-ga-li-he-quo.

010825.english: And his disciples came to him, and awoke him, saying, Lord, save us: we perish.
010825.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴ ᎬᏩᏰᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏍᎩᏍᏕᎸ, ᏓᏲᏣᏗᏒᏂᏉ.
010825.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no gv-wa-lu-tse-le gv-wa-ye-s-ta-ne-i, hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, s-gi-s-de-lv, da-yo-tsa-di-sv-ni-quo.

010826.english: And he saith unto them, Why are ye fearful, O ye of little faith? Then he arose, and rebuked the winds and the sea; and there was a great calm.
010826.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᏥᏍᎦᎢᎭ?  ᎤᏍᏗ ᎢᏦᎯᏳᎯ!  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏚᎴᏅ ᏚᏍᎦᏤ ᎤᏃᎴ ᎠᎴ ᎥᏓᎵ, ᎤᏣᏘᏃ ᎤᏓᏥᎾᏍᏛᎯ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
010826.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, Ga-do-no i-tsi-s-ga-i-ha?  U-s-di i-tso-hi-yu-hi!  Hna-quo-no du-le-nv du-s-ga-tse u-no-le a-le v-da-li, u-tsa-ti-no u-da-tsi-na-s-dv-hi nu-li-s-ta-ne-i.

010827.english: But the men marvelled, saying, What manner of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him!
010827.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯᏃ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎾᏍᏉᏰᏃ ᎤᏃᎴ ᎠᎴ ᎥᏓᎵ ᎢᎬᏬᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ.
010827.phonetic: A-ni-s-ga-ya-no u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Ga-do u-s-di hi-a a-s-ga-ya, na-s-quo-ye-no u-no-le a-le v-da-li i-gv-wo-hi-yu-hv-s-ga.

010828.english: And when he was come to the other side into the country of the Gergesenes, there met him two possessed with devils, coming out of the tombs, exceeding fierce, so that no man might pass by that way.
010828.cherokee: ᎢᏍᎪᏂᏗᏢᏃ ᏭᎷᏨ ᎦᎩᏏᏂ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ, ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏯᎢ ᏕᎬᏩᏠᏎᎢ, ᏓᏓᏂᏌᎲ ᏧᏂᏄᎪᏤᎢ, ᎤᏣᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᎿᎭᎸᎯᏳ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᎾᎿ ᎭᎬᏩᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ.
010828.phonetic: I-s-go-ni-di-tlv-no wu-lu-tsv Ga-gi-si-ni u-na-tse-li-go-hi, a-ni-ta-li a-ni-s-gi-na di-gv-wa-ni-ya-i de-gv-wa-tlo-se-i, da-da-ni-sa-hv tsu-ni-nu-go-tse-i, u-tsa-ta-nv-hi u-ni-hna-lv-hi-yu ge-se-i, na-s-gi v-tla gi-lo na-hna gv-wa-lo-hi-s-di yi-ge-se-i.

010829.english: And, behold, they cried out, saying, What have we to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of God? art thou come hither to torment us before the time?
010829.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎤᏁᎷᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏗᎦᏓᏛᏙᏗ ᏱᎩ ᏂᎯ, ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᏥᎪ ᎠᏂ ᏣᎷᏥᎸ ᏍᎩᏂᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏙᏗᏱᏉ ᎠᏏᏉ ᎾᏍᏆᎵᏍᎬᎾ?
010829.phonetic: A-le gv-ni-yu-quo u-ne-lu-ne-i, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; v-tla go-hu-s-di di-ga-da-dv-do-di yi-gi ni-hi, Tsi-sa U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi, tsi-go a-ni tsa-lu-tsi-lv s-gi-ni-gi-li-yo-i-s-do-di-yi-quo a-si-quo na-s-qua-li-s-gv-na?

010830.english: And there was a good way off from them an herd of many swine feeding.
010830.cherokee: ᎨᎯᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏗᏁᏙᎮ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᏏᏆ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏂᏙᎮᎢ.
010830.phonetic: Ge-hi-no i-yv-dv di-ne-do-he u-ni-tsa-ti su-na-da-tlu-gi si-qua a-na-li-s-da-yv-ni-do-he-i.

010831.english: So the devils besought him, saying, If thou cast us out, suffer us to go away into the herd of swine.
010831.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾᏃ ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏍᎩᏂᏄᎪᏫᏒᎭ, ᎤᎾᏁᎳᎩ ᏍᎩᏁᎵᏎᎸᎭ ᏏᏆ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᏫᎪᏍᏗᏴᏍᏗᏱ.
010831.phonetic: A-ni-s-gi-na-no gv-wa-ta-yo-se-le-i, hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i; i-yu-no s-gi-ni-nu-go-wi-sv-ha, u-na-ne-la-gi s-gi-ne-li-se-lv-ha si-qua su-na-da-tlu-gi wi-go-s-di-yv-s-di-yi.

010832.english: And he said unto them, Go. And when they were come out, they went into the herd of swine: and, behold, the whole herd of swine ran violently down a steep place into the sea, and perished in the waters.
010832.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏍᏕᎾ.  ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏨᏃ, ᏏᏆ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᏫᏚᏂᏴᎴᎢ.  ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᏏᏆ ᎠᏯᏄᎵᏳ ᎤᎾᎵᏎᎢ, ᏭᏂᎦᏐᎠᏎ ᎦᏁᏡᎩ ᎬᏒᎢ, ᎥᏓᎵ ᏭᎾᎵᏔᏗᏅᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎹᏱ ᏚᏂᎵᏬᏤᎢ.
010832.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i; S-de-na.  U-ni-nu-go-tsv-no, si-qua su-na-da-tlu-gi wi-du-ni-yv-le-i.  A-le gv-ni-yu-quo ni-ga-dv su-na-da-tlu-gi si-qua a-ya-nu-li-yu u-na-li-se-i, wu-ni-ga-so-a-se ga-ne-tlu-gi gv-sv-i, v-da-li wu-na-li-ta-di-nv-se-i, a-le a-ma-yi du-ni-li-wo-tse-i.

010833.english: And they that kept them fled, and went their ways into the city, and told every thing, and what was befallen to the possessed of the devils.
010833.cherokee: ᏗᏂᎦᏘᏯᏃ ᎤᎾᎵᏎᎢ, ᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᏭᏂᎶᏎᎢ, ᏂᎦᏛ ᏭᏂᏃᎮᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎬᏩᏂᏯᎢ ᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ.
010833.phonetic: Di-ni-ga-ti-ya-no u-na-li-se-i, di-ga-du-hv wu-ni-lo-se-i, ni-ga-dv wu-ni-no-he-le-i, a-le a-ni-s-gi-na gv-wa-ni-ya-i nu-na-li-s-da-ne-lv-i.

010834.english: And, behold, the whole city came out to meet Jesus: and when they saw him, they besought him that he would depart out of their coasts.
010834.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᏂᎦᏗᏳ ᏍᎦᏚᎩ ᏧᏂᏄᎪᏤᎢ, ᏥᏌ ᏕᎬᏩᏠᏏᎴᎢ; ᎬᏩᎪᎲᏃ ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᎤᏓᏅᏍᏗᏱᏉ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
010834.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo-no ni-ga-di-yu s-ga-du-gi tsu-ni-nu-go-tse-i, tsi-sa de-gv-wa-tlo-si-le-i; gv-wa-go-hv-no gv-wa-ta-yo-se-le u-da-nv-s-di-yi-quo u-na-tse-li-go-hi ge-sv-i.

010901.english: And he entered into a ship, and passed over, and came into his own city.
010901.cherokee: ᏥᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᏣᏅ, ᏚᏪᏐᏤᎢ, ᎤᏩᏒᏃ ᎤᏪᏚᎲ ᏭᎷᏤᎢ.
010901.phonetic: Tsi-yu-hi-no u-tsa-nv, du-we-so-tse-i, u-wa-sv-no u-we-du-hv wu-lu-tse-i.

010902.english: And, behold, they brought to him a man sick of the palsy, lying on a bed: and Jesus seeing their faith said unto the sick of the palsy; Son, be of good cheer; thy sins be forgiven thee.
010902.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎬᏩᏘᏃᎮᎴ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏢᎩ ᎤᎸᏓᎸᎥᏍᎩ ᎦᏅᎨ ᎠᏤᏍᏙᎩᎯ; ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎬᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎤᎸᏓᎸᎥᏍᎩ; ᎠᏇᏥ, ᎤᎦᎵᏍᏗᏳᏉ ᎭᏓᏅᏓᏓ, ᏣᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎡᏣᏙᎵᎩ.
010902.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo-no gv-wa-ti-no-he-le a-s-ga-ya u-tlv-gi u-lv-da-lv-v-s-gi ga-nv-ge a-tse-s-do-gi-hi; Tsi-sa-no u-do-le-ho-sv a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gv-i, hi-a nu-we-se-le u-lv-da-lv-v-s-gi; A-que-tsi, u-ga-li-s-di-yu-quo ha-da-nv-da-da, tsa-s-ga-nv-tsv e-tsa-do-li-gi.

010903.english: And, behold, certain of the scribes said within themselves, This man blasphemeth.
010903.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎢᎦᏛ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ; ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎠᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᎭ.
010903.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo-no i-ga-dv di-no-we-li-s-gi hi-a nu-ni-we-se du-na-da-nv-dv-i; hi-a a-s-ga-ya Ga-lv-la-di e-hi a-so-tlv-i-s-di-ha.

010904.english: And Jesus knowing their thoughts said, Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts?
010904.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎠᎦᏔᎮ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬᎢ, ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏖᎭ ᏗᏥᎾᏫᏱ?
010904.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no a-ga-ta-he nu-s-dv a-na-da-nv-te-s-gv-i, hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i; Ga-do-no u-yo i-tsa-da-nv-te-ha di-tsi-na-wi-yi?

010905.english: For whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and walk?
010905.cherokee: ᎦᏙᎨ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎤᏟ ᎠᎯᏗᏳ ᎧᏁᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᏥᎪ ᎯᎠ ᏱᎾᎦᏪᎠ, ᏣᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎡᏣᏙᎵᎩ?  ᎯᎠᎨ ᏱᎾᎦᏪᎠ, ᏔᎴᎲᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎨᏓ?
010905.phonetic: Ga-do-ge u-s-di u-tli a-hi-di-yu ka-ne-i-s-di-yi, tsi-go hi-a yi-na-ga-we-a, tsa-s-ga-nv-tsv e-tsa-do-li-gi?  Hi-a-ge yi-na-ga-we-a, ta-le-hv-ga a-le ge-da?

010906.english: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (then saith he to the sick of the palsy,) Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.
010906.cherokee: ᎠᏗᎾ ᎢᏣᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎬᏩᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎤᎸᏓᎸᎥᏍᎩ; ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᏣᏤᏍᏙ ᎯᎾᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏤᏅᏒ ᎮᎾ.
010906.phonetic: A-di-na i-tsa-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi yv-wi U-we-tsi a-s-ga-nv-tsv gv-wa-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv a-ni e-lo-hi, hna-quo hi-a nu-we-se-le u-lv-da-lv-v-s-gi; Ta-le-hv-ga, tsa-tse-s-do hi-na-gi, a-le di-tse-nv-sv he-na.

010907.english: And he arose, and departed to his house.

010908.english: But when the multitudes saw it, they marvelled, and glorified God, which had given such power unto men.
010908.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎤᏂᎪᎲ, ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎸᏉᏔᏁ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᏴᏫ.
010908.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no u-ni-go-hv, u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i, a-le u-ni-lv-quo-ta-ne U-ne-la-nv-hi na-s-gi tsu-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-lv-hi na-s-gi i-yu-s-di tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi yv-wi.

010909.english: And as Jesus passed forth from thence, he saw a man, named Matthew, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he saith unto him, Follow me. And he arose, and followed him.
010909.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏓᏅᏒ, ᎤᎪᎲᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎹᏚ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎤᏬᎸᎩ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎠᎫᏴᏗᏱ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏍᎩᏍᏓᏩᏚᎦ.  ᏚᎴᏅᏃ ᎤᏍᏓᏩᏛᏒᎩ.
010909.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no na-hna u-da-nv-sv, u-go-hv-gi a-s-ga-ya Ma-du tsu-do-i-dv, u-wo-lv-gi a-ye-li a-gu-yv-di-yi.  Hi-a-no nu-we-se-lv-gi; S-gi-s-da-wa-du-ga.  Du-le-nv-no u-s-da-wa-dv-sv-gi.

010910.english: And it came to pass, as Jesus sat at meat in the house, behold, many publicans and sinners came and sat down with him and his disciples.
010910.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎦᏅᎬ [ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬ] ᎦᎵᏦᏕ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎩ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏂᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾ ᎤᏂᎷᏨ ᎤᎾᏂᏢᏅᎩ ᎠᏂᏅᏜᏓᎥ ᏥᏌ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ.
010910.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi, ga-nv-gv [a-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv] ga-li-tso-de, gv-ni-yu-quo u-ni-tsa-dv-gi a-de-lv a-ni-gi-si-do-hi a-le a-ni-s-ga-na u-ni-lu-tsv u-na-ni-tlv-nv-gi a-ni-nv-dla-v Tsi-sa a-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi.

010911.english: And when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto his disciples, Why eateth your Master with publicans and sinners?
010911.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏᏃ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚ ᏂᏪᏎᎸ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᏗᏤᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏂᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎢᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎦ?
010911.phonetic: A-ni-qua-li-si-no u-na-do-le-ho-sv, hi-a ni-du ni-we-se-lv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi; Ga-do-no Di-tse-yo-hv-s-gi a-de-lv a-ni-gi-si-do-hi a-le a-ni-s-ga-na i-tsu-la-ha i-a-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-ga?

010912.english: But when Jesus heard that, he said unto them, They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick.
010912.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏂᏚᏂᏢᎬᎾ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏚᎸᏗ ᏱᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎰ ᏗᏓᏅᏫᏍᎩ, ᏧᏂᏢᎩᏍᎩᏂ.
010912.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-dv-ga-nv hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Ni-du-ni-tlv-gv-na v-tla u-du-lv-di yi-nu-na-li-s-da-ne-ho di-da-nv-wi-s-gi, tsu-ni-tlv-gi-s-gi-ni.

010913.english: But go ye and learn what that meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice: for I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
010913.cherokee: ᎢᏤᎾᏍᎩᏂ, ᏫᏣᏕᎶᏆ ᎦᏛᎬ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏂᎦᏪᎭ; ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏆᏚᎵᎭ, ᎥᏝᏃ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎳᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎾᏂᏍᎦᏅᎾ ᏱᏗᏥᏯᏅᎯᎸ, ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾᏍᎩᏂ ᏧᏂᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗᏱ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ.
010913.phonetic: I-tse-na-s-gi-ni, wi-tsa-de-lo-qua ga-dv-gv hi-a na-s-gi tsi-ni-ga-we-ha; a-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv a-qua-du-li-ha, v-tla-no a-tsi-lv--ge-la-s-di-yi ge-sv-i.  V-tla-ye-no na-ni-s-ga-nv-na yi-di-tsi-ya-nv-hi-lv, a-ni-s-ga-na-s-gi-ni tsu-ni-ne-tli-yv-s-di-yi du-na-da-nv-dv-i.

010914.english: Then came to him the disciples of John, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees fast oft, but thy disciples fast not?
010914.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏣᏂ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ [ᏥᏌ] ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᎠᏴ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏᏃ ᏯᏃ ᎠᎹᏟ ᏦᏨᏍᎦ, ᏂᎯᏃ ᎨᏣᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᎹᏟ ᎾᏅᏍᎬᎾ ᏥᎩ?
010914.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsa-ni gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi [Tsi-sa] gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-gi, hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; Ga-do-no a-yv A-ni-qua-li-si-no ya-no a-ma-tli tso-tsv-s-ga, ni-hi-no ge-tsa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi a-ma-tli na-nv-s-gv-na tsi-gi?

010915.english: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? but the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and then shall they fast.
010915.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏥᎪ ᏰᎵ ᏕᎨᎦᏨᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎯ ᏱᏓᎾᏠᏱᎦ ᎠᏏᏉ ᎤᏕᏒᏂᎸᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏗᏙᎲᎢ?  ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᎦ ᏛᎵᏱᎶᎵ ᏓᎨᏥᏯᏅᎡᎵ ᎤᏕᏒᏂᎸᎯ, ᎩᎳ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᎹᏟ ᏛᏅᏂ.
010915.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Tsi-go ye-li de-ge-ga-tsv-s-di-s-gv a-ni-ga-ta-hi yi-da-na-tlo-yi-ga a-si-quo u-de-sv-ni-lv-hi u-ne-la-di-do-hv-i?  A-se-no i-ga dv-li-yi-lo-li da-ge-tsi-ya-nv-e-li u-de-sv-ni-lv-hi, gi-la hna-quo a-ma-tli dv-nv-ni.

010916.english: No man putteth a piece of new cloth unto an old garment, for that which is put in to fill it up taketh from the garment, and the rent is made worse.
010916.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏤ ᏱᎦᎳᏍᏢᏗᏍᎪ ᎤᏪᏘ ᎠᏄᏬᎩᎯ, ᎦᎳᏍᏢᏛᏰᏃ ᏴᎦᏣᎦᎸᏓ ᎠᏄᏬ, ᎤᏓᏣᎦᎸᎲᏃ ᎤᏟ ᎡᏉᎯᏳ ᏱᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓ.
010916.phonetic: V-tla a-le gi-lo i-tse yi-ga-la-s-tlv-di-s-go u-we-ti a-nu-wo-gi-hi, ga-la-s-tlv-dv-ye-no yv-ga-tsa-ga-lv-da a-nu-wo, u-da-tsa-ga-lv-hv-no u-tli e-quo-hi-yu yi-ni-ga-li-s-da.

010917.english: Neither do men put new wine into old bottles: else the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish: but they put new wine into new bottles, and both are preserved.
010917.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏤ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᏧᏪᏘ ᏗᏑᏢᏛ ᏱᏓᏂᏟᏍᏗᏍᎪᎢ; ᏗᏑᏢᏛᏰᏃ ᏱᏓᏓᏣᎦᎸ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᏯᏤᏬᎩ, ᏗᏑᏢᏛᏃ ᏱᏓᏲᎩ.  ᎢᏤᏍᎩᏂ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᏗᏤ ᏗᏑᏢᏛ ᏓᏂᏟᏍᏗᏍᎪᎢ, ᎢᏧᎳᏃ ᏚᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᎢ.
010917.phonetic: V-tla a-le i-tse gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di tsu-we-ti di-su-tlv-dv yi-da-ni-tli-s-di-s-go-i; di-su-tlv-dv-ye-no yi-da-da-tsa-ga-lv, a-le gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di ya-tse-wo-gi, di-su-tlv-dv-no yi-da-yo-gi.  I-tse-s-gi-ni gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di di-tse di-su-tlv-dv da-ni-tli-s-di-s-go-i, i-tsu-la-no du-ni-s-qua-ni-go-do-i.

010918.english: While he spake these things unto them, behold, there came a certain ruler, and worshipped him, saying, My daughter is even now dead: but come and lay thy hand upon her, and she shall live.
010918.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᏕᎦᏪᏎᎲᎩ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᎤᎷᏨ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎠᏇᏥ ᎠᎨᏳᏣ ᎦᏳᎳ ᎤᏲᎱᏏᏕᎾ.  ᎠᏗᎾ ᎮᎾ, ᎯᏯᏒᏂᎸᎭ, ᎠᎭ ᏛᎠᏛᏂᏗᏉ.
010918.phonetic: A-si-quo na-s-gi ni-de-ga-we-se-hv-gi, gv-ni-yu-quo gi-lo i-yu-s-di nu-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi u-lu-tsv u-da-do-li-s-da-ne-lv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; A-que-tsi a-ge-yu-tsa ga-yu-la u-yo-hu-si-de-na.  A-di-na he-na, hi-ya-sv-ni-lv-ha, a-ha dv-a-dv-ni-di-quo.

010919.english: And Jesus arose, and followed him, and so did his disciples.
010919.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏚᎴᏅ ᎤᏍᏓᏩᏛᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ.
010919.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no du-le-nv u-s-da-wa-dv-sv-gi, a-le na-s-quo gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi.

010920.english: And, behold, a woman, which was diseased with an issue of blood twelve years, came behind him, and touched the hem of his garment:
010920.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎤᏢᎩ ᎩᎬ ᎤᏪᏅᎡᎯ ᏔᎳᏚ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏓ ᎢᎬᏩᎵᏍᏔᏅᎯ, ᎣᏂᏗᏢ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ, ᎦᏓᎷᏯᏛ ᎤᏄᏩᎥ ᎤᏒᏁᎸᎩ.
010920.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo-no a-ge-yv u-tlv-gi gi-gv u-we-nv-e-hi ta-la-du tsu-de-ti-yv-da i-gv-wa-li-s-ta-nv-hi, o-ni-di-tlv u-lu-hi-s-da-ne-lv-gi, ga-da-lu-ya-dv u-nu-wa-v u-sv-ne-lv-gi.

010921.english: For she said within herself, If I may but touch his garment, I shall be whole.
010921.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏄᏪᏎ ᎤᏓᏅᏛᎢ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᏄᏩᎥᏉ ᏯᏆᏒᏂᎸ, ᎠᏎ ᏯᏆᏗᏫ.
010921.phonetic: Hi-a-ye-no nu-we-se u-da-nv-dv-i; i-yu-no u-nu-wa-v-quo ya-qua-sv-ni-lv, a-se ya-qua-di-wi.

010922.english: But Jesus turned him about, and when he saw her, he said, Daughter, be of good comfort; thy faith hath made thee whole. And the woman was made whole from that hour.
010922.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᎦᏔᎲᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎪᎲ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎠᏇᏥ, ᎤᎦᎵᏍᏗᏉ ᎭᏓᏅᏓᏓ.  ᏦᎯᏳᏒ ᏣᏗᏫᏍᏓ.  ᎠᎨᏴᏃ ᎾᎯᏳᏉ ᎤᏗᏩᏒᎩ.
010922.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-ga-ta-hv-sv a-le u-go-hv, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; A-que-tsi, u-ga-li-s-di-quo ha-da-nv-da-da.  Tso-hi-yu-sv tsa-di-wi-s-da.  A-ge-yv-no na-hi-yu-quo u-di-wa-sv-gi.

010923.english: And when Jesus came into the ruler's house, and saw the minstrels and the people making a noise,
010923.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᎦᏁᎸ ᎤᎷᏨ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎪᎲ ᏗᏂᏤᎷᎯᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏴᏫ ᏓᏂᏴᎬᎢ,
010923.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no nu-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi ga-ne-lv u-lu-tsv, a-le du-go-hv di-ni-tse-lu-hi-s-gi, a-le yv-wi da-ni-yv-gv-i,

010924.english: He said unto them, Give place: for the maid is not dead, but sleepeth. And they laughed him to scorn.
010924.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏣᏓᏅᎾ, ᎾᎪᏃ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᏱᎩ ᎠᎨᏳᏣ; ᎦᎵᎭᏉᏍᎩᏂ.  ᎥᎬᏩᏰᏣᏍᏔᏅᏉᏃ.
010924.phonetic: Hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; I-tsa-da-nv-na, na-go-no u-yo-hu-sv-hi yi-gi a-ge-yu-tsa; ga-li-ha-quo-s-gi-ni.  V-gv-wa-ye-tsa-s-ta-nv-quo-no.

010925.english: But when the people were put forth, he went in, and took her by the hand, and the maid arose.
010925.cherokee: ᎨᏥᏄᎪᏫᏒᏃ ᏴᏫ, ᎤᏴᎸᎩ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ ᎤᏂᏴᎲᎩ, ᎠᎨᏳᏣᏃ ᏚᎴᏅᎩ.
010925.phonetic: Ge-tsi-nu-go-wi-sv-no yv-wi, u-yv-lv-gi u-wo-ye-ni u-ni-yv-hv-gi, a-ge-yu-tsa-no du-le-nv-gi.

010926.english: And the fame hereof went abroad into all that land.
010926.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a du-no-tsa-lo-tsi-do-lv-i na-hna ni-gv-na-dv du-ye-li-si-do-lv-gi.

010927.english: And when Jesus departed thence, two blind men followed him, crying, and saying, Thou Son of David, have mercy on us.
010927.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏓᏅᏒ, ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᏗᏂᎨᏫ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏒᎩ, ᎤᏁᎷᎬᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎬᎩ, ᏍᎩᏂᏙᎵᎩ ᏂᎯ ᏕᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ.
010927.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no na-hna u-da-nv-sv, a-ni-ta-li di-ni-ge-wi a-ni-s-ga-ya gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-sv-gi, u-ne-lu-gv-gi hi-a na-ni-we-s-gv-gi, s-gi-ni-do-li-gi ni-hi De-wi U-we-tsi.

010928.english: And when he was come into the house, the blind men came to him: and Jesus saith unto them, Believe ye that I am able to do this? They said unto him, Yea, Lord.
010928.cherokee: ᎦᎵᏦᏕᏃ ᎤᏴᎸ, ᏗᏂᎨᏫ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᎩ, ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏍᏙᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦᏍᎪ ᏰᎵ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏆᏛᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ?  ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎥᎥ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ.
010928.phonetic: Ga-li-tso-de-no u-yv-lv, di-ni-ge-wi gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-gi, tsi-sa-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; I-s-do-hi-yu-hv-s-ga-s-go ye-li na-s-gi i-gv-qua-dv-ne-di ge-sv-i?  Hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; V-v, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi.

010929.english: Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your faith be it unto you.
010929.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏚᏒᏁᎸ ᏗᏂᎦᏙᎵ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏄᏍᏛ ᎢᏍᏙᎯᏳᎲᏍᎬ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᏍᏓᎵᏍᏓᏏ.
010929.phonetic: Hna-quo-no du-sv-ne-lv di-ni-ga-do-li, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Nu-s-dv i-s-do-hi-yu-hv-s-gv na-s-gi-ya ni-s-da-li-s-da-si.

010930.english: And their eyes were opened; and Jesus straitly charged them, saying, See that no man know it.
010930.cherokee: ᏗᏂᎦᏙᎵᏃ ᏚᎵᏍᏚᎩᏒᎩ.  ᏥᏌᏃ ᏚᏅᏍᏓᏕᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒᎩ.
010930.phonetic: Di-ni-ga-do-li-no du-li-s-du-gi-sv-gi.  Tsi-sa-no du-nv-s-da-de-lv-gi, hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Tle-s-di gi-lo u-do-le-ho-sv-gi.

010931.english: But they, when they were departed, spread abroad his fame in all that country.

010932.english: As they went out, behold, they brought to him a dumb man possessed with a devil.
010932.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏂᏄᎪᎬᎢ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎩᎶ ᎬᏩᏘᏃᎮᎸᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏩᎨᏫ, ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᏯᎢ.
010932.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-ni-nu-go-gv-i gv-ni-yu-quo gi-lo gv-wa-ti-no-he-lv-gi a-s-ga-ya u-wa-ge-wi, a-s-gi-na u-ya-i.

010933.english: And when the devil was cast out, the dumb spake: and the multitudes marvelled, saying, It was never so seen in Israel.
010933.cherokee: ᎠᏍᎩᎾᏃ ᎠᏥᏄᎪᏫᏒ ᎤᏩᎨᏫ ᎤᏬᏂᏒᎩ.  ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏒᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᎪᎲᎯ ᏱᎩ ᎢᏏᎵᏱ.
010933.phonetic: A-s-gi-na-no a-tsi-nu-go-wi-sv u-wa-ge-wi u-wo-ni-sv-gi.  U-ni-tsa-ti-no u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-sv-gi, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; v-tla i-lv-hi-yu na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-go-hv-hi yi-gi i-si-li-yi.

010934.english: But the Pharisees said, He casteth out devils through the prince of the devils.
010934.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎬᏗᎭ ᏥᏕᎦᏄᎪᏫᏍᎦ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ.
010934.phonetic: A-se-no A-ni-qua-li-si hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; A-ni-s-gi-na u-na-tse-li u-gv-wi-yu-hi gv-di-ha tsi-de-ga-nu-go-wi-s-ga a-ni-s-gi-na.

010935.english: And Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every sickness and every disease among the people.
010935.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏧᏛᎾ ᏕᎦᏚᎲ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏍᏗ ᏕᎦᏚᎲ ᎤᏪᏙᎸᎩ, ᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬᎩ ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎬᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎬᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎧᏅᏫᏍᎬ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏚᏂᏢᎬ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎥᏳᎩ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏂᏱᎵᏕᎲᎢ.
010935.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no ni-ga-v tsu-dv-na de-ga-du-hv a-le tsu-s-di de-ga-du-hv u-we-do-lv-gi, da-de-yo-hv-s-gv-gi tsu-ni-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, a-le a-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gv-gi o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv ka-no-he-s-gv-gi, a-le de-ka-nv-wi-s-gv ni-ga-v du-ni-tlv-gv a-le ni-ga-v v-yu-gi yv-wi u-ni-yi-li-de-hv-i.

010936.english: But when he saw the multitudes, he was moved with compassion on them, because they fainted, and were scattered abroad, as sheep having no shepherd.
010936.cherokee: ᏚᎪᎲᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏴᏫ, ᏚᏪᏙᎵᏨᎩ, ᎤᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᏩᎾᏉ ᏓᏂᎶᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏗᎦᎴᏲᏨᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎠᏫ ᎤᏂᏃᏕᎾ ᏁᎲᎾ ᏥᎨᏐ ᎤᏂᎦᏘᏯ.
010936.phonetic: Du-go-hv-no u-ni-tsa-ti yv-wi, du-we-do-li-tsv-gi, u-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi wa-na-quo da-ni-lo-gv-i, a-le u-na-di-ga-le-yo-tsv-hi ge-sv-i, na-s-gi-ya a-wi u-ni-no-de-na ne-hv-na tsi-ge-so u-ni-ga-ti-ya.

010937.english: Then saith he unto his disciples, The harvest truly is plenteous, but the labourers are few;
010937.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᎭᎸ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ; ᎡᏉᎯᏳ ᏠᎨᏏ ᎤᎦᏛᎾᏨᎯ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏂᎦᏲᎵᏳ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ.
010937.phonetic: Hna-quo hi-a ni-du-we-ha-lv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi; E-quo-hi-yu tlo-ge-si u-ga-dv-na-tsv-hi, a-se-no a-ni-ga-yo-li-yu tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi.

010938.english: Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he will send forth labourers into his harvest.
010938.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎡᏥᏔᏲᏏ ᎤᎶᎨᏛ ᏧᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᎤᎦᏛᎾᏤᎸᎢ.
010938.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di e-tsi-ta-yo-si u-lo-ge-dv tsu-nv-s-di-yi tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi u-ga-dv-na-tse-lv-i.

011001.english: And when he had called unto him his twelve disciples, he gave them power against unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal all manner of sickness and all manner of disease.
011001.cherokee: ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎲᏃ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᏚᏁᎸ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏧᏂᏄᎪᏫᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎾᏓᏅᏬᏗᏱ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏚᏂᏢᎬ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎥᏳᎩ.
011001.phonetic: Wi-du-ya-nv-hv-no ta-la-du i-ya-ni-dv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, du-ne-lv a-ni-s-gi-na tsu-ni-nu-go-wi-s-di-yi, a-le tsu-na-da-nv-wo-di-yi ni-ga-v du-ni-tlv-gv, a-le ni-ga-v v-yu-gi.

011002.english: Now the names of the twelve apostles are these; The first, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother;
011002.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏗ ᏚᎾᏙᎥ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ.  ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᏣᏃᏎᎰᎢ, ᎡᏂᏗᏃ ᎾᏍᎩᏉ ᎤᏅᏟ, ᏥᎻᏃ ᏤᏈᏗ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᏣᏂᏃ ᎾᏍᎩᏉ ᎤᏅᏟ.
011002.phonetic: Hi-a-no na-s-gi nu-s-di du-na-do-v ta-la-du i-ya-ni-dv ge-tsi-nv-si-dv.  I-gv-yi-yi Sa-wa-ni Qui-da tsa-no-se-ho-i, E-ni-di-no na-s-gi-quo u-nv-tli, Tsi-mi-no Tse-qui-di u-we-tsi, Tsa-ni-no na-s-gi-quo u-nv-tli.

011003.english: Philip, and Bartholomew; Thomas, and Matthew the publican; James the son of Alphaeus, and Lebbaeus, whose surname was Thaddaeus;
011003.cherokee: ᏈᎵᎩᏃ, ᏩᏓᎳᎻᏃ, ᏓᎻᏃ, ᎹᏚᏃ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᎩᏏᏙᎯ, ᏥᎻᏃ ᎡᎵᏈ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᎴᏈᏯᏃ ᏓᏗᏯ ᏣᏃᏎᎰᎢ,
011003.phonetic: Qui-li-gi-no, Wa-da-la-mi-no, Da-mi-no, Ma-du-no a-de-lv a-gi-si-do-hi, Tsi-mi-no E-li-qui u-we-tsi, Le-qui-ya-no Da-di-ya tsa-no-se-ho-i,

011004.english: Simon the Canaanite, and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.
011004.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂᏃ ᎠᎨᎾᏂᏗ, ᏧᏓᏏᏃ ᎢᏍᎦᎳᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏌ ᏧᎶᏄᎮᎸᎩ.
011004.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni-no A-ge-na-ni-di, Tsu-da-si-no I-s-ga-la-di na-s-gi Tsi-sa tsu-lo-nu-he-lv-gi.

011005.english: These twelve Jesus sent forth, and commanded them, saying, Go not into the way of the Gentiles, and into any city of the Samaritans enter ye not:
011005.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᏥᏌ ᏚᏅᏒᎩ ᏚᏁᏤᎸᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏗᏁᎲ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏫᏥᎶᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏌᎺᎵ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏚᏂᏚᎲ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏴᎸᎩ
011005.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a ta-la-du i-ya-ni-dv Tsi-sa du-nv-sv-gi du-ne-tse-lv-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi di-ne-hv i-di-tlv tle-s-di wi-tsi-lo-sv-gi, a-le Sa-me-li a-ne-hi du-ni-du-hv tle-s-di i-tsi-yv-lv-gi

011006.english: But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
011006.cherokee: ᎠᏫᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᎾᎴᎾᎯᏛ ᎢᏏᎵ ᏏᏓᏁᎸ ᎨᏒ ᏗᏥᏩᏛᎱᎦ.
011006.phonetic: A-wi-s-gi-ni u-na-le-na-hi-dv I-si-li si-da-ne-lv ge-sv di-tsi-wa-dv-hu-ga.

011007.english: And as ye go, preach, saying, The kingdom of heaven is at hand.
011007.cherokee: ᎢᏣᎢᏒᏃ ᎢᏣᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏥᏪᏍᎨᏍᏗ; ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎡᏍᎦᏂᏳ ᏓᏯᎢ.
011007.phonetic: I-tsa-i-sv-no i-tsa-li-tsi-do-hv-s-ge-s-di, hi-a ni-tsi-we-s-ge-s-di; Ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv e-s-ga-ni-yu da-ya-i.

011008.english: Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, cast out devils: freely ye have received, freely give.
011008.cherokee: ᏧᏂᏢᎩ ᏕᏥᏅᏫᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏓᏰᏍᎩ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ ᏕᏥᏅᎦᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᏧᏂᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᏕᏣᎴᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏕᏥᏄᎪᏫᏍᎨᏍᏗ.  ᎠᏎᏉ ᎡᏥᏁᎸᎯ, ᎠᏎᏉ ᎢᏣᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ.
011008.phonetic: Tsu-ni-tlv-gi de-tsi-nv-wi-s-ge-s-di, a-da-ye-s-gi tsu-ni-tlv-gi de-tsi-nv-ga-li-s-ge-s-di, tsu-ni-yo-hu-sv-hi de-tsa-le-di-s-ge-s-di, a-ni-s-gi-na de-tsi-nu-go-wi-s-ge-s-di.  A-se-quo e-tsi-ne-lv-hi, a-se-quo i-tsa-da-ne-he-s-di.

011009.english: Provide neither gold, nor silver, nor brass in your purses,
011009.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᏕᏣᏓᏠᎲ ᏱᏣᏓᏁᎴᏍᏗ ᏓᎶᏂᎨ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎬ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᎴ ᎥᏣᏱ,
011009.phonetic: Tle-s-di de-tsa-da-tlo-hv yi-tsa-da-ne-le-s-di da-lo-ni-ge a-de-lv a-le u-ne-gv a-de-lv a-le v-tsa-yi,

011010.english: Nor scrip for your journey, neither two coats, neither shoes, nor yet staves: for the workman is worthy of his meat.
011010.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏕᎦᎶᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏔᎵ ᏗᏣᏄᏬ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎳᏑᎶ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏙᎳᏅᏍᏗ; ᏧᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯᏰᏃ ᎤᏩᏛᏗ ᎨᏐ ᎤᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ.
011010.phonetic: A-le de-ga-lo-di, a-le ta-li di-tsa-nu-wo, a-le di-la-su-lo, a-le di-do-la-nv-s-di; tsu-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi-ye-no u-wa-dv-di ge-so u-li-s-da-yv-di.

011011.english: And into whatsoever city or town ye shall enter, inquire who in it is worthy; and there abide till ye go thence.
011011.cherokee: ᎢᎸᎯᏢᏃ ᎤᏛᎾ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎢᏥᎷᎨᏍᏗ, ᎢᏣᏓᏛᏛᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎡᎲ ᎤᏓᏅᏘ, ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎢᏥᏁᏍᏗ ᎬᏂ ᎥᏣᏂᎩ.
011011.phonetic: I-lv-hi-tlv-no u-dv-na ga-du-hv a-le u-s-di ga-du-hv i-tsi-lu-ge-s-di, i-tsa-da-dv-dv-hv-s-ge-s-di gi-lo e-hv u-da-nv-ti, na-hna-no i-tsi-ne-s-di gv-ni v-tsa-ni-gi.

011012.english: And when ye come into an house, salute it.

011013.english: And if the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it: but if it be not worthy, let your peace return to you.
011013.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏂᏁᎳ ᎤᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᏗᎬᏩᎾᏓᏂᎸᎢᏍᏗ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎢᏣᏤᎵ ᎤᏂᎷᏤᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏗᎬᏩᎾᏓᏂᎸᎢᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎢᏨᏒᏉ ᎢᏥᎷᏤᏗ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011013.phonetic: I-yu-no na-hna a-ni-ne-la u-wa-do-hi-ya-dv di-gv-wa-na-da-ni-lv-i-s-di i-ge-se-s-di, na-s-gi u-wa-do-hi-ya-dv i-tsa-tse-li u-ni-lu-tse-di ge-se-s-di; i-yu-no di-gv-wa-na-da-ni-lv-i-s-di ni-ge-sv-na i-ge-se-s-di, na-s-gi nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv i-tsv-sv-quo i-tsi-lu-tse-di i-ge-se-s-di.

011014.english: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, when ye depart out of that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet.
011014.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏂᏗᏣᏓᏂᎸᎬᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏥᏁᎬ ᎾᏛᎩᏍᎬᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎾᎿᎭᎦᎵᏦᏕ ᎠᎴ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎢᏥᏄᎪᎩ, ᏕᏥᏅᎪᎥᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎦᏓ ᏗᏣᎳᏏᏕᏂ.
011014.phonetic: I-yu-no gi-lo ni-di-tsa-da-ni-lv-gv-na i-ge-se-s-di, a-le i-tsi-ne-gv na-dv-gi-s-gv-na i-ge-se-s-di, na-hna ga-li-tso-de a-le ga-du-hv i-tsi-nu-go-gi, de-tsi-nv-go-v-di-s-ge-s-di ga-da di-tsa-la-si-de-ni.

011015.english: Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.
011015.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏳᏪᏎᎭ, ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎤᏂᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏐᏓᎻ ᎠᎴ ᎪᎹᎵ, ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎤᏟᎯᏳ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ.
011015.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-yu-we-se-ha, e-s-ga-quo i-ga-i u-ni-gi-li-yo-i-s-di ge-se-s-di So-da-mi a-le Go-ma-li, di-gu-go-do-di-yi i-ga ge-se-s-di, u-tli-hi-yu na-s-gi na ga-du-hv-i.

011016.english: Behold, I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves: be ye therefore wise as serpents, and harmless as doves.
011016.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎢᏨᏅᎵ ᎠᏫ ᎤᏂᏃᏕᎾ ᏣᏁᎳᏗᏙᎰ ᏩᏲᎯ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᎾᏛ ᎢᎨᏥᏏᎾᏌᏂ ᎨᏌᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎫᎴ-ᏗᏂᏍᎪᏂᎯ ᎢᎨᏣᏓᏅᏘ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011016.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo i-tsv-nv-li a-wi u-ni-no-de-na tsa-ne-la-di-do-ho wa-yo-hi na-s-gi-ya-i; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-na-dv i-ge-tsi-si-na-sa-ni ge-sa-s-di, a-le gu-le--di-ni-s-go-ni-hi i-ge-tsa-da-nv-ti ge-se-s-di.

011017.english: But beware of men: for they will deliver you up to the councils, and they will scourge you in their synagogues;
011017.cherokee: ᏕᏤᏯᏙᏤᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᏗᎾ ᏴᏫ, ᏕᎦᎳᏫᎥᏰᏃ ᏙᏓᎨᏣᏘᏃᎵ, ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱᏃ ᏙᏓᎨᏥᎵᎥᏂᎵ.
011017.phonetic: De-tse-ya-do-tse-he-s-di a-di-na yv-wi, de-ga-la-wi-v-ye-no do-da-ge-tsa-ti-no-li, tsu-ni-la-wi-i-s-di-yi-no do-da-ge-tsi-li-v-ni-li.

011018.english: And ye shall be brought before governors and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them and the Gentiles.
011018.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎠᏴ ᎨᏒ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏚᏃᎸ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ ᏕᏣᏘᏃᎯᎮᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏙᏗ.
011018.phonetic: A-le a-yv ge-sv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi a-le u-ni-gv-wi-yu-hi du-no-lv i-gv-yi-di-tlv de-tsa-ti-no-hi-he-s-di, na-s-gi u-nv-sv a-le Tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi u-na-do-le-ho-hi-s-do-di.

011019.english: But when they deliver you up, take no thought how or what ye shall speak: for it shall be given you in that same hour what ye shall speak.
011019.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏕᎨᏣᏘᏃᎯᎮᏍᏗ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏤᎵᎯᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏁᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏥᏪᏍᏗᏱ; ᎾᎯᏳᏉᏰᏃ ᎡᏥᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏪᏍᏗᏱ.
011019.phonetic: A-se-no hna-quo de-ge-tsa-ti-no-hi-he-s-di tle-s-di yi-tse-li-hi-s-ge-s-di i-yu-s-di i-tsi-ne-i-s-di-yi a-le i-tsi-we-s-di-yi; na-hi-yu-quo-ye-no e-tsi-ne-di ge-se-s-di i-tsi-we-s-di-yi.

011020.english: For it is not ye that speak, but the Spirit of your Father which speaketh in you.
011020.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᏂᎯ ᏱᏥᏁᎪᎢ, ᎢᏥᏙᏓᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏓᏅᏙ ᎧᏁᎪᎢ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏨᏗᏍᎪᎢ.
011020.phonetic: V-tla-ye-no ni-hi yi-tsi-ne-go-i, I-tsi-do-da-s-gi-ni U-da-nv-do ka-ne-go-i ni-hi i-tsv-di-s-go-i.

011021.english: And the brother shall deliver up the brother to death, and the father the child: and the children shall rise up against their parents, and cause them to be put to death.
011021.cherokee: ᎠᏍᎦᏯᏃ ᏙᏓᏳᏲᏏ ᎤᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ, ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᏃ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏙᏓᏳᏲᏏ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏂ ᏧᎾᏛᏒᎯ ᏙᏛᎾᏡᏔᏂ ᏧᏂᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏛᏅᏂᏌᏂ ᏗᎨᏥᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011021.phonetic: A-s-ga-ya-no do-da-yu-yo-si u-yo-hu-hi-s-di-yi di-na-da-nv-tli, a-ga-yv-li-ge-no u-we-tsi do-da-yu-yo-si, a-le o-ni tsu-na-dv-sv-hi do-dv-na-tlu-ta-ni tsu-ni-ga-yv-li-ge-i, a-le na-s-gi nv-dv-nv-ni-sa-ni di-ge-tsi-i-s-di ge-se-s-di.

011022.english: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name's sake: but he that endureth to the end shall be saved.
011022.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏂᎥᏉ ᏴᏫ ᎨᏥᏍᎦᎩ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᏴ ᏓᏆᏙᎥ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᎩ ᎠᏟᏂᎬᏁᎯ ᎠᏥᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011022.phonetic: A-le na-ni-v-quo yv-wi ge-tsi-s-ga-gi ge-se-s-di a-yv da-qua-do-v nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di; a-se-no na-s-gi gv-li-s-qua-di-s-gi a-tli-ni-gv-ne-hi a-tsi-s-de-lv-di ge-se-s-di.

011023.english: But when they persecute you in this city, flee ye into another: for verily I say unto you, Ye shall not have gone over the cities of Israel, till the Son of man be come.
011023.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏲ ᏂᎨᏨᎿᎭᏕᎨᏍᏗ ᎢᎸᎯᏢ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᎢᎶᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏅᏩᎵᎴ ᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᏫᏥᎶᏍᎨᏍᏗ.  ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎥᏝ ᎢᏏᎵᏱ ᏕᎦᏚᎲᎢ ᏱᎨᏤᏙᏁᏍᏗ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏓᎦᎷᏥ.
011023.phonetic: A-le hna-quo u-yo ni-ge-tsv-hna-de-ge-s-di i-lv-hi-tlv ga-du-hv-i, i-lo-li-s-ge-s-di nv-wa-li-le di-ga-du-hv wi-tsi-lo-s-ge-s-di.  U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya-ye-no hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha, V-tla I-si-li-yi de-ga-du-hv-i yi-ge-tse-do-ne-s-di hna-quo yv-wi U-we-tsi da-ga-lu-tsi.

011024.english: The disciple is not above his master, nor the servant above his lord.
011024.cherokee: ᏗᏕᎶᏆᏍᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏟ ᏱᎾᏥᎸᏉᏙᎢ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏧᏪᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏟ ᏱᎾᏥᎸᏉᏙᎢ ᏒᏍᎦᏉ ᎤᏅᏏᏙᎯ.
011024.phonetic: Di-de-lo-qua-s-gi v-tla u-tli yi-na-tsi-lv-quo-do-i e-s-ga-quo tsu-we-yo-hv-s-gi, a-le a-tsi-nv-si-da-s-di v-tla u-tli yi-na-tsi-lv-quo-do-i sv-s-ga-quo u-nv-si-do-hi.

011025.english: It is enough for the disciple that he be as his master, and the servant as his lord. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebub, how much more shall they call them of his household?
011025.cherokee: ᏰᎵᏉᏰᏃ ᏗᏕᎶᏆᏍᎩ ᏧᏪᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᎢᏧᎳᎭᏉ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᎤᏅᏏᏙᎯ ᎢᏧᎳᎭᏉ ᏱᎩ.  ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᏧᏓᏘᎿᎭᎢ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᏇᎵᏥᏆ ᏳᏃᏎᎸ, ᎤᏟ ᎠᎯᏗᏳ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏧᏂᏪᏎᏗᏱ ᏚᏓᏘᎿᎭᎥᎢ.
011025.phonetic: Ye-li-quo-ye-no di-de-lo-qua-s-gi tsu-we-yo-hv-s-gi i-tsu-la-ha-quo yi-gi, a-le a-tsi-nv-si-da-s-di u-nv-si-do-hi i-tsu-la-ha-quo yi-gi.  I-yu-ye-no tsu-da-ti-hna-i a-da-ne-lv Que-li-tsi-qua yu-no-se-lv, u-tli a-hi-di-yu na-s-gi i-tsu-ni-we-se-di-yi du-da-ti-hna-v-i.

011026.english: Fear them not therefore: for there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; and hid, that shall not be known.
011026.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏗᏥᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ.  ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏱᎬᏍᎦᎳ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᏱᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
011026.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di tle-s-di yi-di-tsi-s-ga-i-he-s-di.  V-tla-ye-no go-hu-s-di yi-gv-s-ga-la na-s-gi gv-ni-ge-sv i-yu-li-s-do-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i, v-tla a-le go-hu-s-di u-de-li-dv yi-gi na-s-gi a-do-le-ho-hi-s-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i.

011027.english: What I tell you in darkness, that speak ye in light: and what ye hear in the ear, that preach ye upon the housetops.
011027.cherokee: ᎤᎵᏏᎬ ᎢᏨᏁᏤᎸᎢ ᎢᎦᎦᏛ ᎢᏥ ᏁᎨᏍᏗ; ᎡᏣᏙᏅᎡᎸᏃ ᎨᏒ ᎦᏌᎾᎵ ᎢᏣᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
011027.phonetic: U-li-si-gv i-tsv-ne-tse-lv-i i-ga-ga-dv i-tsi ne-ge-s-di; e-tsa-do-nv-e-lv-no ge-sv ga-sa-na-li i-tsa-li-tsi-do-hv-s-ge-s-di.

011028.english: And fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.
011028.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏗᏥᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᏰᎸᎢ ᎠᏂᎯᎯ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎬᏩᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏥᎩ.  ᎤᎬᏫᏳᏎᏍᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᎡᏥᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎬᏩᎯᏍᏗ ᏥᎩ ᎠᏰᎸᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎢᏧᎳ ᏨᏍᎩᏃᎢ.
011028.phonetic: A-le tle-s-di yi-di-tsi-s-ga-i-he-s-di a-ye-lv-i a-ni-hi-hi, a-se-no a-da-nv-do gv-wa-ni-hi-s-di ni-ge-sv-na tsi-gi.  U-gv-wi-yu-se-s-di-s-gi-ni e-tsi-s-ga-i-he-s-di na-s-gi di-gv-wa-hi-s-di tsi-gi a-ye-lv-i a-le a-da-nv-do i-tsu-la tsv-s-gi-no-i.

011029.english: Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing? and one of them shall not fall on the ground without your Father.
011029.cherokee: ᏝᏍᎪ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᏥᏍᏆᏯ ᏌᏉ ᎢᏯᏓᏅᏖᏗ ᏧᎾᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏌᏉ ᎡᎳᏗ ᎤᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ ᎢᏥᏙᏓ ᎤᏓᏅᏖᎸᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
011029.phonetic: Tla-s-go a-ni-ta-li tsi-s-qua-ya sa-quo i-ya-da-nv-te-di tsu-na-gv-wa-lo-di yi-gi, a-se-no v-tla sa-quo e-la-di u-nv-i-s-di yi-gi I-tsi-do-da u-da-nv-te-lv-hi ni-ge-sv-na.

011030.english: But the very hairs of your head are all numbered.

011031.english: Fear ye not therefore, ye are of more value than many sparrows.
011031.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏥᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ; ᎤᏟᏰᏃ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏗᏣᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᏂᎯ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏥᏍᏆᏯ.
011031.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di tle-s-di yi-tsi-s-ga-i-he-s-di; u-tli-ye-no ni-ga-v di-tsa-gv-wa-lo-di ni-hi e-s-ga-quo u-ni-tsa-ti tsi-s-qua-ya.

011032.english: Whosoever therefore shall confess me before men, him will I confess also before my Father which is in heaven.
011032.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎾᏋᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎤᏓᏥᏴᏁᎵ ᎡᏙᏓ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᎢ.
011032.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no gv-ni-ge-sv na-quv-ne-he-s-di yv-wi a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i, na-s-quo a-yv na-s-gi gv-ni-ge-sv u-da-tsi-yv-ne-li E-do-da ga-lv-la-di e-hi a-ga-ta-hv-i.

011033.english: But whosoever shall deny me before men, him will I also deny before my Father which is in heaven.
011033.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᎠᏆᏓᏱᎮᏍᏗ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏥᏯᏓᏱᎵ ᎡᏙᏓ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᎢ.
011033.phonetic: Gi-lo-s-gi-ni i-a-qua-da-yi-he-s-di yv-wi a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i, na-s-quo a-yv na-s-gi da-tsi-ya-da-yi-li E-do-da ga-lv-la-di e-hi a-ga-ta-hv-i.

011034.english: Think not that I am come to send peace on earth: I came not to send peace, but a sword.
011034.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᏙᎯᏱ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏫᏏᎸ ᎡᎶᎯ, ᏱᏍᎩᏰᎵᏎᎮᏍᏗ; ᎥᏝᏍᎩᏂ ᏙᎯᏱ ᏯᎩᎾᏄᎪᏫᏏᎸ, ᎠᏰᎳᏍᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᏅᎯᏛ.
011034.phonetic: Tle-s-di do-hi-yi u-na-nu-go-wi-si-lv e-lo-hi, yi-s-gi-ye-li-se-he-s-di; v-tla-s-gi-ni do-hi-yi ya-gi-na-nu-go-wi-si-lv, a-ye-la-s-di-s-gi-ni ga-nv-hi-dv.

011035.english: For I am come to set a man at variance against his father, and the daughter against her mother, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.
011035.cherokee: ᎠᏴᏰᏃ ᎠᎩᎷᏥᎸ, ᏗᎾᏓᏍᎦᎩ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏙᏓᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎦᏅᎩ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎤᏥᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏦᎯᏯ ᎤᏦᎯᏃ;
011035.phonetic: A-yv-ye-no a-gi-lu-tsi-lv, di-na-da-s-ga-gi i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi a-s-ga-ya u-do-da-no, a-le a-ga-nv-gi a-ge-yv u-tsi-no, a-le u-da-tso-hi-ya u-tso-hi-no;

011036.english: And a man's foes shall be they of his own household.

011037.english: He that loveth father or mother more than me is not worthy of me: and he that loveth son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.
011037.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎤᏟ ᏄᎨᏳᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎠᏴ ᎾᎩᎨᏳᏎᏍᏗ, ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᎠᏆᏤᎵ ᎢᎬᏩᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ.  ᎩᎶᏃ ᎤᏟ ᏄᎨᏳᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎠᏴ ᎾᎩᎨᏳᏎᏍᏗ, ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᎠᏆᏤᎵ ᎢᎬᏩᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011037.phonetic: Gi-lo-no u-tli nu-ge-yu-se-s-di u-do-da a-le u-tsi e-s-ga-quo a-yv na-gi-ge-yu-se-s-di, v-tla ye-li na-s-gi a-yv a-qua-tse-li i-gv-wa-li-s-do-di yi-ge-se-s-di.  Gi-lo-no u-tli nu-ge-yu-se-s-di u-we-tsi a-s-ga-ya a-le u-we-tsi a-ge-yv e-s-ga-quo a-yv na-gi-ge-yu-se-s-di, v-tla ye-li na-s-gi a-yv a-qua-tse-li i-gv-wa-li-s-do-di yi-ge-se-s-di.

011038.english: And he that taketh not his cross, and followeth after me, is not worthy of me.
011038.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎾᏱᏍᎬᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏧᏓᎿᎭᏩᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎾᎩᏍᏓᏩᏕᎬᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᎠᏴ ᎠᏆᏤᎵ ᎢᎬᏩᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011038.phonetic: Gi-lo na-yi-s-gv-na i-ge-se-s-di u-tse-li tsu-da-hna-wa-dv a-le na-gi-s-da-wa-de-gv-na i-ge-se-s-di, v-tla ye-li a-yv a-qua-tse-li i-gv-wa-li-s-do-di yi-ge-se-s-di.

011039.english: He that findeth his life shall lose it: and he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it.
011039.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎬᏅ ᎢᎠᏩᏘᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲᎱᏎᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᎤᏲᎱᏎᎮᏍᏗ ᎬᏅ ᎠᏴ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏩᏛᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011039.phonetic: Gi-lo-no gv-nv i-a-wa-ti-s-ge-s-di, na-s-gi u-yo-hu-se-di ge-se-s-di; gi-lo-no i-u-yo-hu-se-he-s-di gv-nv a-yv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di, na-s-gi u-wa-dv-di ge-se-s-di.

011040.english: He that receiveth you receiveth me, and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.
011040.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᏕᏣᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᏓᏆᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏃ ᎠᏴ ᏓᏆᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ ᏅᏛᎩᏅᏒᎯ
011040.phonetic: Gi-lo-no de-tsa-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di, na-s-gi a-yv da-qua-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di; gi-lo-no a-yv da-qua-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di, na-s-gi da-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di nv-dv-gi-nv-sv-hi

011041.english: He that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet's reward; and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man's reward.
011041.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᏓᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎠᎦᎫᏴᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎦᎫᏴᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏃ ᏓᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏓᏅᏘ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏓᏅᏘ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙ ᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎤᏓᏅᏘ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎦᎫᏴᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎦᎫᏴᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ.
011041.phonetic: Gi-lo-no da-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di a-do-le-ho-s-gi a-do-le-ho-s-gi ge-sv u-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ge-s-di, a-do-le-ho-s-gi a-ga-gu-yv-da-ne-di ge-sv na-s-gi a-ga-gu-yv-da-ne-he-s-di; gi-lo-no da-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di u-da-nv-ti a-s-ga-ya u-da-nv-ti ge-sv u-di-ga-li-s-do di-s-ge-s-di, u-da-nv-ti a-s-ga-ya a-ga-gu-yv-da-ne-di ge-sv na-s-gi a-ga-gu-yv-da-ne-he-s-di.

011042.english: And whosoever shall give to drink unto one of these little ones a cup of cold water only in the name of a disciple, verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise lose his reward.
011042.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᏌᏉ ᎯᎠ ᏧᎾᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎤᎵᏍᏈᏗᏉ ᎠᎧᎵᎢ ᎤᏴᏜᏓᎠᎹ ᎠᎱᎥᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎥᏝ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲᎱᏎᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᎦᎫᏴᎡᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
011042.phonetic: Gi-lo-no sa-quo hi-a tsu-na-s-di ge-sv-i u-li-s-qui-di-quo a-ka-li-i u-yv-dla a-ma a-hu-v-s-ge-s-di a-gi-s-da-wa-di-do-hi ge-sv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di, u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya ni-tsv-we-se-ha, v-tla na-s-gi u-yo-hu-se-di yi-ge-se-s-di a-ga-gu-yv-e-di ge-sv-i.

011101.english: And it came to pass, when Jesus had made an end of commanding his twelve disciples, he departed thence to teach and to preach in their cities.
011101.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ; ᏥᏌ ᎤᏍᏆᏛ ᏕᎧᏁᏤᎲ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎤᏂᎩᏒᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᏂ, ᏚᏕᏲᎤᏒᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏥᏙᏅᏒᎩ ᏚᏂᏚᎿᎭᎥᎢ.
011101.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi; Tsi-sa u-s-qua-dv de-ka-ne-tse-hv ta-la-du i-ya-ni-dv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, u-ni-gi-sv-gi na-hna-ni, du-de-yo-u-sv-gi a-le u-li-tsi-do-nv-sv-gi du-ni-du-hna-v-i.

011102.english: Now when John had heard in the prison the works of Christ, he sent two of his disciples,
011102.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏣᏂ ᏣᏥᏍᏚᎲ ᏧᏛᎦᏅ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ, ᏙᏧᏅᏎ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ,
011102.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsa-ni tsa-tsi-s-du-hv tsu-dv-ga-nv nu-s-dv du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv Ga-lo-ne-dv, do-tsu-nv-se a-ni-ta-li gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi,

011103.english: And said unto him, Art thou he that should come, or do we look for another?
011103.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏅᏓᏳᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏂᎯᏍᎪ Ꮎ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗ ᏥᎩ, ᏥᎪᎨ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᎣᏥᎦᏖᏃᎮᏍᏗ?
011103.phonetic: Hi-a nv-da-yu-we-se-lv-gi; Ni-hi-s-go na u-lu-hi-s-di tsi-gi, tsi-go-ge nv-wa-da-le o-tsi-ga-te-no-he-s-di?

011104.english: Jesus answered and said unto them, Go and shew John again those things which ye do hear and see:
011104.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏥᏍᏕᎾ ᏣᏂ ᏫᎡᏍᏗᏃᎲᏏ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎢᏍᏓᏛᎩᏍᎬᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏍᏗᎪᏩᏘᏍᎬᎢ:
011104.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-ne-tsv hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; tsi-s-de-na Tsa-ni wi-e-s-di-no-hv-si ni-ga-v i-s-da-dv-gi-s-gv-i a-le i-s-di-go-wa-ti-s-gv-i:

011105.english: The blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have the gospel preached to them.
011105.cherokee: ᏗᏂᎨᏫ ᎠᏂᎪᏩᏘᎭ, ᏗᏂᏲᎤᎵᏃ ᎠᏁᏙᎭ, ᎠᏓᏰᏍᎩ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ ᏚᎾᏗᏩᏍᎦ, ᏧᏂᎵᎡᎾᏃ ᎠᎾᏛᎩᎭ, ᏧᏂᏲᎱᏒᎯᏃ ᏕᎨᎦᎴᏗᎭ ᎤᏲᏃ ᎢᏳᎾᏛᎿᎭᏕᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎢᎨᎦᎵᏥᏙᏁᎭ.
011105.phonetic: Di-ni-ge-wi a-ni-go-wa-ti-ha, di-ni-yo-u-li-no a-ne-do-ha, a-da-ye-s-gi tsu-ni-tlv-gi du-na-di-wa-s-ga, tsu-ni-li-e-na-no a-na-dv-gi-ha, tsu-ni-yo-hu-sv-hi-no de-ge-ga-le-di-ha u-yo-no i-yu-na-dv-hna-de-gi o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv i-ge-ga-li-tsi-do-ne-ha.

011106.english: And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.

011107.english: And as they departed, Jesus began to say unto the multitudes concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken with the wind?
011107.cherokee: ᎢᎤᏁᏅᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎴᏅᎲᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏣᏂ ᏚᏃᎮᏓᏁᎸᎩ; ᎦᏙ ᎢᏣᎦᏔᏅᏎ ᎢᎾᎨᎢ?  ᏩᏐᎾᏍᎪ ᎤᏃᎴ ᎤᏖᎸᎮᏍᎬᎢ?
011107.phonetic: I-u-ne-nv-no Tsi-sa u-le-nv-hv-gi hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi u-ni-tsa-ti Tsa-ni du-no-he-da-ne-lv-gi; Ga-do i-tsa-ga-ta-nv-se i-na-ge-i?  Wa-so-na-s-go u-no-le u-te-lv-he-s-gv-i?

011108.english: But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? behold, they that wear soft clothing are in kings' houses.
011108.cherokee: ᎦᏙᎨ ᎢᏣᎦᏔᏅᏎᎢ?  ᎠᏍᎦᏯᏍᎪ ᏗᏩᎾᎨ ᏧᏄᏩᎢ?  ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏗᏩᎾᎨ ᏧᎾᏄᏩᎢ ᎤᏂᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏓᏂᏁᎸ ᎠᏁᎰᎢ.
011108.phonetic: Ga-do-ge i-tsa-ga-ta-nv-se-i?  A-s-ga-ya-s-go di-wa-na-ge tsu-nu-wa-i?  Gv-ni-yu-quo di-wa-na-ge tsu-na-nu-wa-i u-ni-gv-wi-yu-hi da-ni-ne-lv a-ne-ho-i.

011109.english: But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? yea, I say unto you, and more than a prophet.
011109.cherokee: ᎦᏙᎨ ᎢᏣᎦᏔᏅᏎᎢ?  ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩᏍᎪ?  ᎥᎥ, ᎢᏨᏲᏎᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏟᎯᏳ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ.
011109.phonetic: Ga-do-ge i-tsa-ga-ta-nv-se-i?  A-do-le-ho-s-gi-s-go?  V-v, i-tsv-yo-se-ha, a-le u-tli-hi-yu e-s-ga-quo a-do-le-ho-s-gi.

011110.english: For this is he, of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
011110.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᎯᎠ Ꮎ ᎨᎪᏪᎸ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᎸᏍᎬ ᏥᏂᎬᏅ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ, ᏥᏅᎵ ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᏣᎧᏛ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏣᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓᏁᎵ ᏣᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ.
011110.phonetic: Na-s-gi-s-gi-ni hi-a na ge-go-we-lv hi-a a-tsi-no-he-lv-s-gv tsi-ni-gv-nv; Gv-ni-yu-quo, tsi-nv-li tsi-nv-si-da-s-di tsa-ka-dv i-gv-yi-di-tlv, na-s-gi da-tsa-dv-nv-i-s-da-ne-li tsa-lo-hi-s-di-yi i-gv-yi-di-tlv.

011111.english: Verily I say unto you, Among them that are born of women there hath not risen a greater than John the Baptist: notwithstanding he that is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
011111.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᎬᏩᏂᎾᏄᎪᏫᏒᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏳᎾᏄᎪᏨ ᎬᏩᏓᎵᏁᎯᏕᏗ ᏣᏂ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏍᏗᎧᏂ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏟ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏣᏂ.
011111.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha, A-ni-ge-yv gv-wa-ni-na-nu-go-wi-sv-hi tsi-gi v-tla gi-lo yu-na-nu-go-tsv gv-wa-da-li-ne-hi-de-di Tsa-ni di-da-wo-s-gi; a-se-no u-s-di-ka-ni ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv u-tli a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu e-s-ga-quo tsa-ni.

011112.english: And from the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and the violent take it by force.
011112.cherokee: ᏣᏂᏃ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏨ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎪᎯ ᎢᏯᏍᏘ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎾᏁᎷᎩᎡᎭ, ᎠᎾᏁᎷᎩᏍᎩᏃ ᎬᏍᎦᎢᏍᏓᎩ ᏓᏂᏂᏱᎭ.
011112.phonetic: Tsa-ni-no di-da-wo-s-gi u-na-nu-go-tsv nv-da-gv-wa-da-le-nv-dv go-hi i-ya-s-ti ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv a-na-ne-lu-gi-e-ha, a-na-ne-lu-gi-s-gi-no gv-s-ga-i-s-da-gi da-ni-ni-yi-ha.

011113.english: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.
011113.cherokee: ᎾᏂᎥᏰᏃ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎬᎩ ᏣᏂ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏥᎸ ᎢᏯᏍᏘ.
011113.phonetic: Na-ni-v-ye-no a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi a-le di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di a-na-do-le-ho-s-gv-gi Tsa-ni u-na-nu-go-tsi-lv i-ya-s-ti.

011114.english: And if ye will receive it, this is Elias, which was for to come.
011114.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᏗᎨᏣᏓᏂᎸᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎢᎳᏯ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ.
011114.phonetic: I-yu-no di-ge-tsa-da-ni-lv-i-s-di ge-se-s-di, na-s-gi hi-a I-la-ya u-lu-hi-s-di tsi-ge-sv-gi.

011115.english: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

011116.english: But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like unto children sitting in the markets, and calling unto their fellows,
011116.cherokee: ᎦᏙᎨ ᏙᏓᎦᏥᏯᏟᎶᏍᏔᏂ ᎯᎠ ᎪᎯ ᏣᏁᎭ?  ᏗᏂᏲᎵᏉ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎦᏃᏙᏗᏱ ᏣᏂᏃᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏥᏫᏓᏂᏯᏂᏍᎪ ᎤᏁᏓᏍᏗ;
011116.phonetic: Ga-do-ge do-da-ga-tsi-ya-tli-lo-s-ta-ni hi-a go-hi tsa-ne-ha?  Di-ni-yo-li-quo na-s-gi-ya ga-no-do-di-yi tsa-ni-no-i a-le tsi-wi-da-ni-ya-ni-s-go u-ne-da-s-di;

011117.english: And saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned unto you, and ye have not lamented.
011117.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᏓᏂᏪᏎᎰᎢ; ᎠᏤᎷᎩ ᎣᎬᏔᏅ ᏕᏨᏃᎩᎡᎸ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏣᎵᏍᎩᏒ; ᏙᏥᏴᎬᎩ ᎢᏣᏛᎪᏗ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏗᏣᏠᏱᎸ.
011117.phonetic: A-le hi-a tsi-ni-da-ni-we-se-ho-i; a-tse-lu-gi o-gv-ta-nv de-tsv-no-gi-e-lv, a-se-no v-tla yi-tsa-li-s-gi-sv; do-tsi-yv-gv-gi i-tsa-dv-go-di, a-se-no v-tla yi-di-tsa-tlo-yi-lv.

011118.english: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He hath a devil.
011118.cherokee: ᏣᏂᏰᏃ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ ᎥᏝ ᏯᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎨᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏯᏗᏔᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᏂᎠᏂᏪᎭ; ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᏯᎠ.
011118.phonetic: Tsa-ni-ye-no u-lu-tsv-gi v-tla ya-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-ge-i a-le v-tla ya-di-ta-s-ge-i, a-se-no ni-a-ni-we-ha; A-s-gi-na u-ya-a.

011119.english: The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold a man gluttonous, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners. But wisdom is justified of her children.
011119.cherokee: ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᎷᏨ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏗᏔᏍᎦ, ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᎠᏂᏪᎭ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᎬᎥᏍᎩ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ, ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏂᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᏕᎾ ᎤᎾᎵᎢ.  ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᎦᏔᎿᎭᎢ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᎬᏩᏰᎸᎭ.
011119.phonetic: Yv-wi u-we-tsi u-lu-tsv a-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-ga a-le a-di-ta-s-ga, hi-a-no ni-a-ni-we-ha; Gv-ni-yu-quo a-s-ga-ya u-gv-v-s-gi a-li-s-da-yv-di a-le gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di, a-de-lv a-ni-gi-si-do-hi a-le a-ni-s-de-na u-na-li-i.  A-se-no a-ga-ta-hna-i tsu-we-tsi o-si-yu i-gv-wa-ye-lv-ha.

011120.english: Then began he to upbraid the cities wherein most of his mighty works were done, because they repented not:
011120.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᎴᏅᎲᎩ ᏚᎬᏍᎪᎸᏅᎩ ᏚᏂᏚᎲᎢ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ, ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᏂᏚᏂᏁᏟᏴᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
011120.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-le-nv-hv-gi du-gv-s-go-lv-nv-gi du-ni-du-hv-i na-hna u-tli i-ga-i u-s-qua-ni-go-di du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-i, nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi du-na-da-nv-dv ni-du-ni-ne-tli-yv-sv-na ge-sv-i.

011121.english: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works, which were done in you, had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
011121.cherokee: ᎤᏲᎢᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎪᎴᏏᏂ, ᎤᏲᎢᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏇᏣᏱᏗ; ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏕᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏤᎲ ᏱᏙᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎴ ᏔᏯ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏙᏂ, ᎪᎯᎩ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏁᏟᏴᏛ ᏱᎨᏎ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ, ᏧᏏᏕᎾ ᏱᏚᎾᏄᏬᎡ ᎠᎴ ᎪᏍᏚ ᏳᎾᏓᏅᎵᏰᎡᎢ.
011121.phonetic: U-yo-i-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di Go-le-si-ni, u-yo-i-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di Que-tsa-yi-di; i-yu-ye-no u-s-qua-ni-go-di de-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv ni-hi i-tse-hv yi-do-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-le Ta-ya a-le Sa-do-ni, go-hi-gi di-gv-wa-ni-ne-tli-yv-dv yi-ge-se du-na-da-nv-dv-i, tsu-si-de-na yi-du-na-nu-wo-e a-le go-s-du yu-na-da-nv-li-ye-e-i.

011122.english: But I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the day of judgment, than for you.
011122.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎤᏂᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏔᏯ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏙᏂ, ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎤᏟᎯᏳ ᏂᎯ.
011122.phonetic: A-se-no hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; e-s-ga-quo i-ga-i u-ni-gi-li-yo-i-s-di ge-se-s-di Ta-ya a-le Sa-do-ni, di-gu-go-do-di-yi i-ga ge-se-s-di, u-tli-hi-yu ni-hi.

011123.english: And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted unto heaven, shalt be brought down to hell: for if the mighty works, which have been done in thee, had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
011123.cherokee: ᏂᎯᏃ ᎨᏆᏂ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏤᏣᏌᎳᏓ, ᏨᏍᎩᏃᎢ ᏮᏓᏰᎶᎥᏔᏂ; ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏕᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏤᎲ ᏱᏙᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎴ ᏐᏓᎻᏱ, ᎠᏏ ᎪᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏱᏂᎬᏩᏍᏕᏉ.
011123.phonetic: Ni-hi-no Ge-qua-ni ga-lv-la-di i-yv-dv tse-tsa-sa-la-da, tsv-s-gi-no-i wv-da-ye-lo-v-ta-ni; i-yu-ye-no u-s-qua-ni-go-di de-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv ni-hi i-tse-hv yi-do-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-le So-da-mi-yi, a-si go-hi ge-sv yi-ni-gv-wa-s-de-quo.

011124.english: But I say unto you, That it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgment, than for thee.
011124.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎤᏂᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏐᎵᎻ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎤᏟᎯᏳ ᏂᎯ.
011124.phonetic: A-se-no hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; E-s-ga-quo i-ga-i u-ni-gi-li-yo-i-s-di ge-se-s-di So-li-mi di-gu-go-do-di-yi i-ga ge-se-s-di, u-tli-hi-yu ni-hi.

011125.english: At that time Jesus answered and said, I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.
011125.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎬᏯᎵᎡᎵᏤᎭ ᎡᏙᏓ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎡᎶᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏕᎲᏍᎦᎳᏁᎸ ᎠᏂᏏᎾᏌᏂ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏁᏯᏔᎯ, ᏗᏂᏲᎵᏃ ᏥᏕᎯᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᎸ.
011125.phonetic: Na-hi-yu Tsi-sa u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Gv-ya-li-e-li-tse-ha E-do-da, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi ga-lv-la-di a-le e-lo-hi, na-s-gi hi-a tsi-de-hv-s-ga-la-ne-lv a-ni-si-na-sa-ni a-le a-ne-ya-ta-hi, di-ni-yo-li-no tsi-de-hi-na-nu-go-wi-se-lv.

011126.english: Even so, Father: for so it seemed good in thy sight.

011127.english: All things are delivered unto me of my Father: and no man knoweth the Son, but the Father; neither knoweth any man the Father, save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son will reveal him.
011127.cherokee: ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏓᎩᏲᎯᏎᎸ ᎡᏙᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏯᎦᏔᎭ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᏙᏓᏉ ᎤᏩᏒ; ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᏯᎦᏔᎭ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᏩᏒ, ᎩᎶᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᎾᏄᎪᏫᏏ ᎡᎳ ᎤᏪᏥ.
011127.phonetic: Ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di da-gi-yo-hi-se-lv E-do-da, a-le v-tla gi-lo ya-ga-ta-ha U-we-tsi U-do-da-quo u-wa-sv; v-tla a-le gi-lo ya-ga-ta-ha a-ga-yv-li-ge-i U-we-tsi u-wa-sv, gi-lo-no na-s-gi tsi-na-nu-go-wi-si e-la U-we-tsi.

011128.english: Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
011128.cherokee: ᎡᏤᎾ, ᎠᏴ ᏍᎩᎷᏥ, ᏂᎦᏗᏳ ᎠᎩᎵᏯ ᏗᏥᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎨᏛ ᏗᏣᎵᏎᎯ, ᎠᏴᏃ ᏓᏨᏯᏪᏐᎸᏍᏔᏂ.
011128.phonetic: E-tse-na, a-yv s-gi-lu-tsi, ni-ga-di-yu a-gi-li-ya di-tsi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi, a-le ga-ge-dv di-tsa-li-se-hi, a-yv-no da-tsv-ya-we-so-lv-s-ta-ni.

011129.english: Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls.
011129.cherokee: ᎢᏥᏴ ᎠᏆᎩᎳᎾᎶ ᎠᎴ ᏍᎩᏯᏕᎶᏆᏏ, ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏘᏳᏰᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏆᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏳ ᎠᎩᎾᏫᏱ, ᏓᏥᏩᏛᎯᏃ ᏗᏣᏓᏅᏙ ᏧᏯᏪᏐᎸᏍᏗᏱ.
011129.phonetic: I-tsi-yv a-qua-gi-la-na-lo a-le s-gi-ya-de-lo-qua-si, a-qua-da-nv-ti-yu-ye-no a-le a-qua-da-do-li-s-di-yu a-gi-na-wi-yi, da-tsi-wa-dv-hi-no di-tsa-da-nv-do tsu-ya-we-so-lv-s-di-yi.

011130.english: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.

011201.english: At that time Jesus went on the sabbath day through the corn; and his disciples were an hungred, and began to pluck the ears of corn, and to eat.
011201.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳ ᏥᏌ ᎠᎢᏒᎩ ᎠᏫᏒ ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ; ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎠᎪᏄ ᏚᏂᏲᏏᏍᎬᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᎴᏅᎲᎩ ᎤᏂᏍᎫᏕᏒᎩ ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᏗᎬᎩ.
011201.phonetic: Na-hi-yu Tsi-sa a-i-sv-gi a-wi-sv u-tsa-le-s-di u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-ga; gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no a-go-nu du-ni-yo-si-s-gv-gi, a-le u-na-le-nv-hv-gi u-ni-s-gu-de-sv-gi u-tsa-le-s-di a-le u-ni-s-di-gv-gi.

011202.english: But when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto him, Behold, thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do upon the sabbath day.
011202.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎯᎠ ᏅᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎢᎬᏛᏁᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ ᎾᎾᏛᏁ ᎨᏣᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ.
011202.phonetic: A-se-no A-ni-qua-li-si u-na-do-le-ho-sv hi-a nv-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; Gv-ni-yu-quo i-gv-dv-ne-di ni-ge-sv-na u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-ga na-na-dv-ne ge-tsa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi.

011203.english: But he said unto them, Have ye not read what David did, when he was an hungred, and they that were with him;
011203.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏝᏍᎪ ᏱᏥᎪᎵᏰᎣ ᏕᏫ ᏄᏛᏁᎸ ᎠᎪᏄ ᏧᏲᏏᏍᎨ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏁᎯ,
011203.phonetic: A-se-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Tla-s-go yi-tsi-go-li-ye-o De-wi nu-dv-ne-lv a-go-nu tsu-yo-si-s-ge, a-le na-s-gi a-ne-hi,

011204.english: How he entered into the house of God, and did eat the shewbread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for them which were with him, but only for the priests?
011204.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᏧᏴᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎦᏙᏗ ᎦᏚ ᏧᎨᎢ, ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᎩᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏥᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎠᏥᎸᏍᎩᏂ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎤᏅᏒ?
011204.phonetic: U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li a-da-ne-lv tsu-yv-le-i, a-le a-ga-do-di ga-du tsu-ge-i, ye-li gv-wa-gi-s-di ni-ge-sv-na tsi-ge-se-i, a-le na-s-gi a-ne-hi, a-tsi-lv-s-gi-ni--a-ne-lo-hi u-nv-sv?

011205.english: Or have ye not read in the law, how that on the sabbath days the priests in the temple profane the sabbath, and are blameless?
011205.cherokee: ᏝᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏱᏥᎪᎵᏰᎣ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ ᎤᏛᎾ ᏗᎦᏔᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎠᏂᏲᏍᏗᏍᎬ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ?
011205.phonetic: Tla-s-go na-s-quo yi-tsi-go-li-ye-o di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di-yi na-s-gi u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-ga u-dv-na di-ga-ta-wi-i-s-di-yi a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv a-ni-yo-s-di-s-gv, a-le u-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv-hi ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i?

011206.english: But I say unto you, That in this place is one greater than the temple.
011206.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎠᏂ ᎡᏙᎭ ᎤᏟ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎤᏛᎾ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ.
011206.phonetic: A-se-no hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; a-ni e-do-ha u-tli a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu e-s-ga-quo u-dv-na di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi.

011207.english: But if ye had known what this meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice, ye would not have condemned the guiltless.
011207.cherokee: ᏱᏥᎦᏙᎥᏎᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᏛᎬ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏂᎦᏪᎠ, ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏆᏚᎵᎭ, ᎥᏝᏃ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎳᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨᎯ ᏱᏙᎨᏥᏰᎸᏁ ᏄᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨᎾ.
011207.phonetic: Yi-tsi-ga-do-v-se-s-gi-ni ga-dv-gv hi-a na-s-gi tsi-ni-ga-we-a, a-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv a-qua-du-li-ha, v-tla-no a-tsi-lv--ge-la-s-di-yi ge-sv-i, v-tla u-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv-hi yi-do-ge-tsi-ye-lv-ne nu-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv-na.

011208.english: For the Son of man is Lord even of the sabbath day.
011208.cherokee: ᏴᏫᏰᏃ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ.
011208.phonetic: Yv-wi-ye-no U-we-tsi U-gv-wi-yu-hi na-s-quo u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-ga.

011209.english: And when he was departed thence, he went into their synagogue:

011210.english: And, behold, there was a man which had his hand withered. And they asked him, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath days? that they might accuse him.
011210.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎡᏙᎲᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ ᎤᏩᎢᏎᎸᎯ.  ᎬᏩᏛᏛᏅᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏥᎪ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎬᏓᏅᏬᏗ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬᎢ; ᎬᏩᏱᎵᏓᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᎾᏚᎵᏍᎬᎩ.
011210.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo-no e-do-hv-gi a-s-ga-ya u-wo-ye-ni u-wa-i-se-lv-hi.  Gv-wa-dv-dv-nv-gi-no hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; tsi-go ye-li-quo gv-da-nv-wo-di u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv-i; gv-wa-yi-li-da-s-di-yi u-na-du-li-s-gv-gi.

011211.english: And he said unto them, What man shall there be among you, that shall have one sheep, and if it fall into a pit on the sabbath day, will he not lay hold on it, and lift it out?
011211.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏥᎪ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏓᏑᏯ ᎢᏤᎲᎢ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏌᏉ ᎠᏫ ᎤᏃᏕᎾ ᏳᎾᏝᎠ, ᎠᏔᎴᏒᏃ ᏳᎸᏨ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏭᏂᏴᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏭᎴᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ?
011211.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Tsi-go a-s-ga-ya u-da-su-ya i-tse-hv-i, i-yu-no sa-quo a-wi u-no-de-na yu-na-tla-a, a-ta-le-sv-no yu-lv-tsv u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv-i, na-s-gi wu-ni-yv-di a-le wu-le-s-di ni-ge-sv-na?

011212.english: How much then is a man better than a sheep? Wherefore it is lawful to do well on the sabbath days.
011212.cherokee: ᏂᎦᎥᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏟᎯᏳ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏴᏫ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎠᏫ ᎤᏃᏕᎾ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ.
011212.phonetic: Ni-ga-v-s-gi-ni u-tli-hi-yu a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu yv-wi e-s-ga-quo a-wi u-no-de-na.  Na-s-gi-s-gi-ni i-yu-s-di ye-li-quo o-s-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-ga.

011213.english: Then saith he to the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it forth; and it was restored whole, like as the other.
011213.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ; ᎭᏙᏯᏅᎯᏓ; ᎤᏙᏯᏅᎯᏛᎩᏃ.  ᎠᎴ ᎤᏗᏩᏒᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏐᎢ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ.
011213.phonetic: Hna-quo-no hi-a nu-we-se-lv a-s-ga-ya; Ha-do-ya-nv-hi-da; u-do-ya-nv-hi-dv-gi-no.  A-le u-di-wa-sv-gi, na-s-gi-ya so-i nu-s-dv nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi.

011214.english: Then the Pharisees went out, and held a council against him, how they might destroy him.
011214.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏨ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸ ᎢᏳᏅᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
011214.phonetic: Hna-quo-no A-ni-qua-li-si u-ni-nu-go-tsv u-ni-no-he-lv i-yu-nv-ne-di-yi u-ni-hi-s-di-yi.

011215.english: But when Jesus knew it, he withdrew himself from thence: and great multitudes followed him, and he healed them all;
011215.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏓᏅᏒᎩ; ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏒᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏚᏅᏩᏅᎩ,
011215.phonetic: A-se-no Tsi-sa u-do-le-ho-sv na-hna u-da-nv-sv-gi; u-ni-tsa-ti-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-sv-gi; a-le ni-ga-dv du-nv-wa-nv-gi,

011216.english: And charged them that they should not make him known:

011217.english: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying,
011217.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏗᏱ ᎠᏰᎸᏒᎩ ᎢᏌᏯ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᏧᏁᏤᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏎᎢ;
011217.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-di-yi a-ye-lv-sv-gi I-sa-ya a-do-le-ho-s-gi tsu-ne-tse-i, hi-a tsi-nu-we-se-i;

011218.english: Behold my servant, whom I have chosen; my beloved, in whom my soul is well pleased: I will put my spirit upon him, and he shall shew judgment to the Gentiles.
011218.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᏥᏯᏑᏰᏛ ᏥᎨᏳᎢ ᏗᏆᏓᏅᏛ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏥᏰᎸᎢ; ᏓᏥᏁᎵ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏙ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏧᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011218.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo tsi-nv-si-da-s-di tsi-ya-su-ye-dv tsi-ge-yu-i di-qua-da-nv-dv i-yv-dv o-s-dv tsi-ye-lv-i; da-tsi-ne-li a-qua-da-nv-do, a-le Tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi du-yu-go-dv tsu-na-nu-go-wi-se-di ge-se-s-di.

011219.english: He shall not strive, nor cry; neither shall any man hear his voice in the streets.
011219.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᏧᏘᏲᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᎷᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏛᎦᏁᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎧᏁᎬ ᏕᎦᎳᏅᏛᎢ.
011219.phonetic: V-tla tsu-ti-yo-s-di a-le u-we-lu-di yi-ge-se-s-di, v-tla a-le gi-lo u-dv-ga-ne-di yi-ge-se-s-di ka-ne-gv de-ga-la-nv-dv-i.

011220.english: A bruised reed shall he not break, and smoking flax shall he not quench, till he send forth judgment unto victory.
011220.cherokee: ᏩᏐᎾ ᎤᏰᏓᏬᏨᎯ ᎥᏝ ᏧᏍᏆᎵᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏙᎴᏛ ᏧᎦᏒᏍᏗ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏩᏜᏓᏗᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎬᏂ ᎠᏓᎵᏁᎯᏙᏅᎭ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒ ᏓᏰᎵᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
011220.phonetic: Wa-so-na u-ye-da-wo-tsv-hi v-tla tsu-s-qua-li-s-di yi-ge-se-s-di, a-le do-le-dv tsu-ga-sv-s-di v-tla u-wa-dla-di-s-di yi-ge-se-s-di, gv-ni a-da-li-ne-hi-do-nv-ha du-yu-go-dv ge-sv da-ye-li-hi-s-di-s-gv-i.

011221.english: And in his name shall the Gentiles trust.

011222.english: Then was brought unto him one possessed with a devil, blind, and dumb: and he healed him, insomuch that the blind and dumb both spake and saw.
011222.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᎦᏘᏃᎮᎸᎩ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᏯᎢ ᏗᎨᏫ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᎨᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏅᏩᏅᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏗᎨᏫ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᎨᏫ ᎤᏬᏂᏒᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎪᎲᎩ.
011222.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-ga-ti-no-he-lv-gi a-s-gi-na u-ya-i di-ge-wi a-le u-wa-ge-wi, a-le u-nv-wa-nv-gi; na-s-gi-no di-ge-wi a-le u-wa-ge-wi u-wo-ni-sv-gi a-le u-go-hv-gi.

011223.english: And all the people were amazed, and said, Is not this the son of David?
011223.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏒᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏝᏍᎪ ᎯᎠ ᏕᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏱᎩ?
011223.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no yv-wi u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-sv-gi, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Tla-s-go hi-a De-wi U-we-tsi yi-gi?

011224.english: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, This fellow doth not cast out devils, but by Beelzebub the prince of the devils.
011224.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᎥᏝ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏱᏗᎦᏄᎪᏫᏍᎦ ᎬᏂ ᏧᏍᏕᎵᏍᎪ ᏇᎵᏥᏆ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ.
011224.phonetic: A-se-no A-ni-qua-li-si u-na-dv-ga-nv, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Hi-a v-tla a-ni-s-gi-na yi-di-ga-nu-go-wi-s-ga gv-ni tsu-s-de-li-s-go Que-li-tsi-qua u-gv-wi-yu-hi a-ni-s-gi-na u-na-tse-li-ga.

011225.english: And Jesus knew their thoughts, and said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and every city or house divided against itself shall not stand:
011225.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏥᏌ ᎠᎦᏔᎲ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏂᎦᎥ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎪᏢᏒᎢ ᏔᎵ ᏥᎾᏓᏗᏍᎪ ᎤᏩᏒᏉ ᏣᏓᏡᏗᏍᎪ ᎠᏲᎪᏉ; ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᏔᎵ ᏥᎾᏓᏗᏍᎪ ᎤᏩᏒᏉ ᏣᏓᏡᏗᏍᎪ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏂᎬᏩᏍᏙᏉ.
011225.phonetic: A-le tsi-sa a-ga-ta-hv nu-s-dv a-na-da-nv-te-s-gv-i, hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Ni-ga-v a-ye-li go-tlv-sv-i ta-li tsi-na-da-di-s-go u-wa-sv-quo tsa-da-tlu-di-s-go a-yo-go-quo; a-le ni-ga-v ga-du-hv a-le a-da-ne-lv ta-li tsi-na-da-di-s-go u-wa-sv-quo tsa-da-tlu-di-s-go v-tla yi-ni-gv-wa-s-do-quo.

011226.english: And if Satan cast out Satan, he is divided against himself; how shall then his kingdom stand?
011226.cherokee: ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᏎᏓᏂ ᏎᏓᏂ ᏱᎦᏄᎪᏫᏍᎦ, ᏔᎵ ᎾᏓᏗᏍᎪᎢ ᎤᏩᏒᏉ ᎠᏓᏡᏗᏍᎪᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᏱᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᏱᏂᎬᏩᏍᏗᏉ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ?
011226.phonetic: I-yu a-le Se-da-ni Se-da-ni yi-ga-nu-go-wi-s-ga, ta-li na-da-di-s-go-i u-wa-sv-quo a-da-tlu-di-s-go-i; ga-do-no yi-ga-li-s-do-da yi-ni-gv-wa-s-di-quo u-tse-li-go-hi?

011227.english: And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your children cast them out? therefore they shall be your judges.
011227.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᏴ ᏇᎵᏥᏆ ᏯᎩᏍᏕᎵ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏱᏗᏥᏄᎪᏫᏍᎦ, ᎦᎪ ᎤᏂᏍᏕᎵᏍᎪ ᏗᏤᏥ ᏥᏓᏂᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪᎢ?  ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᏗᎨᏧᎪᏓᏁᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011227.phonetic: I-yu-no a-yv Que-li-tsi-qua ya-gi-s-de-li a-ni-s-gi-na yi-di-tsi-nu-go-wi-s-ga, ga-go u-ni-s-de-li-s-go di-tse-tsi tsi-da-ni-nu-go-wi-s-go-i?  Na-s-gi-s-gi-ni di-ge-tsu-go-da-ne-hi ge-se-s-di.

011228.english: But if I cast out devils by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God is come unto you.
011228.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏓᏅᏙ ᏱᎬᏗ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏱᏗᏥᏄᎪᏫᏍᎦ, ᎿᎭᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏥᎷᏤᎸ.
011228.phonetic: I-yu-s-gi-ni a-yv U-ne-la-nv-hi U-da-nv-do yi-gv-di a-ni-s-gi-na yi-di-tsi-nu-go-wi-s-ga, hna-quo-s-gi-ni ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv i-tsi-lu-tse-lv.

011229.english: Or else how can one enter into a strong man's house, and spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man? and then he will spoil his house.
011229.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙ ᏯᏛᎦ ᎩᎶ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎦᏁᎸ ᏯᏴᎭ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎿᎭᎥ ᏯᏬᏅ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏄᎸᎸᎾ ᏱᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ?  ᎩᎳ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏯᏬᏅ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ ᎤᎿᎭᎥᎢ.
011229.phonetic: A-le ga-do ya-dv-ga gi-lo u-li-ni-gi-dv a-s-ga-ya ga-ne-lv ya-yv-ha a-le u-hna-v ya-wo-nv, i-yu-no i-gv-yi nu-lv-lv-na yi-gi na-s-gi u-li-ni-gi-dv a-s-ga-ya?  Gi-la hna-quo ya-wo-nv ga-ne-lv-i u-hna-v-i.

011230.english: He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not with me scattereth abroad.
011230.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎾᏆᎵᎪᏁᎲᎾ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏆᏡᏗᎭ; ᎩᎶᏃ ᏃᏍᏗᏟᏏᏍᎬᎾ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏯᏗᎦᎴᏯᏍᎦ.
011230.phonetic: Gi-lo na-qua-li-go-ne-hv-na, na-s-gi a-qua-tlu-di-ha; gi-lo-no no-s-di-tli-si-s-gv-na, na-s-gi ya-di-ga-le-ya-s-ga.

011231.english: Wherefore I say unto you, All manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men: but the blasphemy against the Holy Ghost shall not be forgiven unto men.
011231.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᏂᎦᎥ ᏄᏓᎴᏒ ᎠᏍᎦᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏐᏢᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎨᏥᏙᎵᏍᏗᏉ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏴᏫ; ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎠᏐᏢᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏝ ᎨᏥᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏴᏫ.
011231.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; ni-ga-v nu-da-le-sv a-s-ga-nv-i-s-di ge-sv a-le a-so-tlv-do-di ge-sv ge-tsi-do-li-s-di-quo ge-se-s-di yv-wi; Ga-lv-quo-di-yu-s-gi-ni A-da-nv-do a-so-tlv-do-di ge-sv v-tla ge-tsi-do-li-s-di yi-ge-se-s-di yv-wi.

011232.english: And whosoever speaketh a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but whosoever speaketh against the Holy Ghost, it shall not be forgiven him, neither in this world, neither in the world to come.
011232.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎠᏡᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᎠᏥᏙᎵᏍᏗᏉ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎠᏡᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᏥᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎪᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏐᎢᏱ ᏗᎨᏒᎢ.
011232.phonetic: A-le gi-lo ga-wo-ni-s-gv a-tlu-di-s-ge-s-di yv-wi U-we-tsi, a-tsi-do-li-s-di-quo ge-se-s-di; gi-lo-s-gi-ni ga-wo-ni-s-gv a-tlu-di-s-ge-s-di Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do, v-tla a-tsi-do-li-s-di yi-ge-se-s-di go-hi ge-sv a-le na-s-quo so-i-yi di-ge-sv-i.

011233.english: Either make the tree good, and his fruit good; or else make the tree corrupt, and his fruit corrupt: for the tree is known by his fruit.
011233.cherokee: ᏡᎬ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏂᏨᎦ ᎤᏛᏃ ᎣᏍᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᏡᎬ ᎤᏐᏅ ᏂᏨᎦ ᎤᏛᏃ ᎤᏐᏅᎢ; ᏡᎬᏰᏃ ᎪᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏐ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᏛᎢ.
011233.phonetic: Tlu-gv o-s-dv ni-tsv-ga u-dv-no o-s-dv, a-le tlu-gv u-so-nv ni-tsv-ga u-dv-no u-so-nv-i; tlu-gv-ye-no go-li-s-do-di ge-so nu-s-dv u-dv-i.

011234.english: O generation of vipers, how can ye, being evil, speak good things? for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.
011234.cherokee: Ᏺ ᎢᎾᏛ ᏧᏁᏥ ᏂᎯ!  ᏥᏂᏥᏲᎢᏴ ᎦᏙ ᏱᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏱᏥᏬᏂᎯ?  ᎤᏣᏛᏰᏃ ᎣᎾᏫᏱ ᎣᏪᎲᎢ ᎥᎰᎵ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎪᎢ.
011234.phonetic: Yo i-na-dv tsu-ne-tsi ni-hi!  Tsi-ni-tsi-yo-i-yv ga-do yi-ga-li-s-do-da o-s-dv yi-tsi-wo-ni-hi?  U-tsa-dv-ye-no o-na-wi-yi o-we-hv-i v-ho-li ga-wo-ni-s-go-i.

011235.english: A good man out of the good treasure of the heart bringeth forth good things: and an evil man out of the evil treasure bringeth forth evil things.
011235.cherokee: ᎣᏍᏛ ᏴᏫ, ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏛ ᎤᎾᏫᏱ, ᎣᏍᏛ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪᎢ.  ᎤᏲᏃ ᏴᏫ, ᎤᏲ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏛ ᎤᏲ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪᎢ.
011235.phonetic: O-s-dv yv-wi, o-s-dv u-s-qua-ni-go-dv u-na-wi-yi, o-s-dv ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-go-i.  U-yo-no yv-wi, u-yo u-s-qua-ni-go-dv u-yo ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-go-i.

011236.english: But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.
011236.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏲ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᏴᏫ, ᎤᏂᏃᎮᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏒᎢ.
011236.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-tsv-we-se-ha; Ni-ga-v u-yo a-ni-wo-ni-s-gv yv-wi, u-ni-no-he-di ge-se-s-di di-gu-go-do-di-yi i-ga ge-sv-i.

011237.english: For by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.
011237.cherokee: ᎯᏁᎬᏰᏃ ᏣᏚᏓᎴᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᏁᎬ ᏣᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᏗᏣᏚᎪᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011237.phonetic: Hi-ne-gv-ye-no tsa-du-da-le-s-do-di ge-se-s-di, a-le hi-ne-gv tsa-s-ga-nv-tsv di-tsa-du-go-da-ne-di ge-se-s-di.

011238.english: Then certain of the scribes and of the Pharisees answered, saying, Master, we would see a sign from thee.
011238.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᏂᏁᏨ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎣᎦᏚᎵ ᎢᏨᎪᏩᏛᎡᏗᏱ.
011238.phonetic: Hna-quo-no gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di di-no-we-li-s-gi a-le A-ni-qua-li-si u-ni-ne-tsv, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, u-ye-lv-dv o-ga-du-li i-tsv-go-wa-dv-e-di-yi.

011239.english: But he answered and said unto them, An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas:
011239.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎪᎯ ᏣᏁᎭ ᎤᏂᏲ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎾᏓᏲᏁᎯ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎤᏂᏲᎰᎢ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎥᏝ ᎨᏥᏁᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏦᎾ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎤᏩᏒ.
011239.phonetic: U-ne-tsv-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Go-hi tsa-ne-ha u-ni-yo a-le di-na-da-yo-ne-hi u-ye-lv-dv u-ni-yo-ho-i; a-se-no u-ye-lv-dv v-tla ge-tsi-ne-di yi-ge-se-s-di, Tso-na a-do-le-ho-s-gi u-tse-li-ga u-ye-lv-dv u-wa-sv.

011240.english: For as Jonas was three days and three nights in the whale's belly; so shall the Son of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
011240.cherokee: ᏦᎾᏰᏃ ᏦᎢ ᎢᎦ ᎠᎴ ᏦᎢ ᏒᏃᏱ ᎠᏣᏗ ᎡᏆ ᎤᏍᏉᎵᏱ ᏣᏯᎡᎢ, ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏦᎢ ᎢᎦ ᎠᎴ ᏦᎢ ᏒᏃᏱ ᎦᏙᎯ ᎭᏫᏂ ᎠᏯᎡᏍᏗ.
011240.phonetic: Tso-na-ye-no tso-i i-ga a-le tso-i sv-no-yi a-tsa-di e-qua u-s-quo-li-yi tsa-ya-e-i, na-s-quo-s-gi-ni yv-wi U-we-tsi tso-i i-ga a-le tso-i sv-no-yi ga-do-hi ha-wi-ni a-ya-e-s-di.

011241.english: The men of Nineveh shall rise in judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: because they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas is here.
011241.cherokee: ᏴᏫ ᏂᏂᏫ ᏣᏁᎲᎩ ᏙᏛᎾᎴᏂ ᏓᎬᏩᎾᏡᏔᏂ ᎪᎯ ᏣᏁᎭ, ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎤᏛᏅᏁᎵ; ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᏚᏂᏁᏟᏴᏒᎩ ᏦᎾ ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎬ ᏄᏩᏂᏌᏅᎩ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎠᏂ ᎡᏙᎭ ᎤᏟ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏦᎾ.
011241.phonetic: Yv-wi Ni-ni-wi tsa-ne-hv-gi do-dv-na-le-ni da-gv-wa-na-tlu-ta-ni go-hi tsa-ne-ha, di-gu-go-do-di-yi i-ga ge-sv-i, a-le u-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv gv-ni-ge-sv u-dv-nv-ne-li; na-s-gi-ye-no du-na-da-nv-dv du-ni-ne-tli-yv-sv-gi Tso-na a-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gv nu-wa-ni-sa-nv-gi, gv-ni-yu-quo-no a-ni e-do-ha u-tli a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu e-s-ga-quo Tso-na.

011242.english: The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for she came from the uttermost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon is here.
011242.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᎨᏴ ᏧᎦᎾᏮ ᏤᎮᎢ ᏙᏛᎴᏂ ᏓᏳᎾᏡᏔᏂ ᎪᎯ ᏣᏁᎭ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏅᏓᎬᏁᎵ; ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏩᏍᏛ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏧᏪᏅᏎ ᎤᏛᎦᏂᎴ ᏐᎵᎼᏅ ᎠᎦᏔᎿᎭᎢᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎠᏂ ᎡᏙᎭ ᎤᏟ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏐᎵᎼᏅ.
011242.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi a-ge-yv tsu-ga-na-wv tse-he-i do-dv-le-ni da-yu-na-tlu-ta-ni go-hi tsa-ne-ha di-gu-go-do-di-yi i-ga ge-sv-i, a-le u-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv gv-ni-ge-sv nv-da-gv-ne-li; na-s-gi-ye-no ga-do-hi wa-s-dv i-yv-dv tsu-we-nv-se u-dv-ga-ni-le So-li-mo-nv a-ga-ta-hna-i-yu ge-sv-i, gv-ni-yu-quo-no a-ni e-do-ha u-tli a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu e-s-ga-quo So-li-mo-nv.

011243.english: When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest, and findeth none.
011243.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᏴᏫᎯ ᎦᏄᎪᎢ, ᎤᎧᏲᏛᎯ ᎡᏙᎰᎢ ᎤᏯᏪᏐᎸᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏲᎰᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏯᏩᏘᏍᎪᎢ.
011243.phonetic: I-yu-no ga-da-ha a-da-nv-do yv-wi-hi ga-nu-go-i, u-ka-yo-dv-hi e-do-ho-i u-ya-we-so-lv-s-di-yi u-yo-ho-i, a-se-no v-tla ya-wa-ti-s-go-i.

011244.english: Then he saith, I will return into my house from whence I came out; and when he is come, he findeth it empty, swept, and garnished.
011244.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎪᎢ; ᏗᏥᏁᎸ ᏗᎩᏄᎪᏨ ᏴᏛᏥᎶᏏ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏫᎦᎷᎩ ᎠᏩᏘᏍᎪ ᎤᏏᏩ ᎨᏐ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏃᏌᏛᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎪᏚᎢᏍᏙᎢ.
011244.phonetic: Hna-quo-no hi-a ni-ga-we-s-go-i; di-tsi-ne-lv di-gi-nu-go-tsv yv-dv-tsi-lo-si.  Hna-quo-no wi-ga-lu-gi a-wa-ti-s-go u-si-wa ge-so a-le gv-no-sa-dv-hi a-le go-du-i-s-do-i.

011245.english: Then goeth he, and taketh with himself seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last state of that man is worse than the first. Even so shall it be also unto this wicked generation.
011245.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎢᎡᎪᎢ ᏫᏓᏯᏂᏍᎪ ᎤᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᏟ ᎢᏳᏂᏁᎫᏥᏛ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎤᏩᏒ, ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎠᏂᏴᎯᎰᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏁᎳᏗᏍᎪᎢ, ᎣᏂᏃ ᏄᏍᏛ Ꮎ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏟ ᎤᏲᎢᏳ ᎨᏐᎢ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᏄᎾᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎪᎯ ᎤᏂᏁᎫᏥᏛ ᏣᏁᎭ.
011245.phonetic: Hna-quo-no i-e-go-i wi-da-ya-ni-s-go u-wa-na-da-le ga-li-quo-gi a-ni-s-gi-na u-tli i-yu-ni-ne-gu-tsi-dv e-s-ga-quo u-wa-sv, na-hna-no a-ni-yv-hi-ho-i a-le a-na-ne-la-di-s-go-i, o-ni-no nu-s-dv na a-s-ga-ya u-tli u-yo-i-yu ge-so-i e-s-ga-quo i-gv-yi-yi.  Na-s-gi-s-gi-ni nu-na-s-de-s-di go-hi u-ni-ne-gu-tsi-dv tsa-ne-ha.

011246.english: While he yet talked to the people, behold, his mother and his brethren stood without, desiring to speak with him.
011246.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉ ᏕᎦᏬᏁᏗᏍᎬ ᏴᏫ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎤᏥ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ ᏙᏱᏢ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎥᎩ, ᎤᎾᏚᎵᏍᎬᎩ ᎬᏩᎵᏃᎮᏙᏗᏱ.
011246.phonetic: A-si-quo de-ga-wo-ne-di-s-gv yv-wi, gv-ni-yu-quo u-tsi a-le a-na-li-nv-tli do-yi-tlv a-ni-do-na-v-gi, u-na-du-li-s-gv-gi gv-wa-li-no-he-do-di-yi.

011247.english: Then one said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desiring to speak with thee.
011247.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏣᏥ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᎵᏅᏟ ᏙᏱ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎠ, ᎨᏣᎵᏃᎮᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᎾᏚᎵᎭ.
011247.phonetic: Gi-lo-no hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Gv-ni-yu-quo tsa-tsi a-le i-tsa-li-nv-tli do-yi a-ni-do-na-a, ge-tsa-li-no-he-do-di-yi u-na-du-li-ha.

011248.english: But he answered and said unto him that told him, Who is my mother? and who are my brethren?
011248.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸ ᎤᏃᏁᎸᎯ; ᎦᎪ ᎡᏥ?  ᎠᎴ ᎦᎪ ᎣᏣᎵᏅᏟ?
011248.phonetic: U-ne-tsv-no hi-a nu-we-se-lv u-no-ne-lv-hi; Ga-go e-tsi?  A-le ga-go o-tsa-li-nv-tli?

011249.english: And he stretched forth his hand toward his disciples, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
011249.cherokee: ᏫᏚᏐᏅᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎡᏥ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏣᎵᏅᏟ.
011249.phonetic: Wi-du-so-nv-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Gv-ni-yu-quo e-tsi a-le o-tsa-li-nv-tli.

011250.english: For whosoever shall do the will of my Father which is in heaven, the same is my brother, and sister, and mother.
011250.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎾᏛᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᎡᏙᏓ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎥᎩᏅᏟ ᎠᎴ ᎥᎩᏙ ᎠᎴ ᎡᏥ.
011250.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no na-dv-ne-he-s-di nu-s-dv u-du-li-s-gv E-do-da ga-lv-la-di e-hi, na-s-gi v-gi-nv-tli a-le v-gi-do a-le e-tsi.

011301.english: The same day went Jesus out of the house, and sat by the sea side.
011301.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳ ᎢᎦ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏄᎪᏨᎩ ᎠᏓᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏓᎷᎶᏗ ᎤᏪᏅᎩ.
011301.phonetic: Na-hi-yu i-ga Tsi-sa u-nu-go-tsv-gi a-da-ne-lv-i, a-le v-da-lu-lo-di u-we-nv-gi.

011302.english: And great multitudes were gathered together unto him, so that he went into a ship, and sat; and the whole multitude stood on the shore.
011302.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎬᏩᏓᏡᏫᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᏣᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᏅᎩ.  ᏂᎦᏗᏳᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᎹᏳᎶᏗ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎥᎩ.
011302.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no yv-wi gv-wa-da-tlu-wi-s-ta-nv-gi, na-s-gi i-yu-s-di tsi-yu-hi u-tsa-nv-gi, a-le u-we-nv-gi.  Ni-ga-di-yu-no na-s-gi u-ni-tsa-ti a-ma-yu-lo-di a-ni-do-na-v-gi.

011303.english: And he spake many things unto them in parables, saying, Behold, a sower went forth to sow;
011303.cherokee: ᏕᎤᏬᏁᏔᏅᏃ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏚᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ, ᎠᏫᏍᎩ ᎤᏫᏒᏎᎢ.
011303.phonetic: De-u-wo-ne-ta-nv-no u-tsa-ti tsu-da-le-nv-dv du-tli-lo-s-da-ne-lv-gi; hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Gv-ni-yu-quo, a-wi-s-gi u-wi-sv-se-i.

011304.english: And when he sowed, some seeds fell by the way side, and the fowls came and devoured them up:
011304.cherokee: ᎤᏫᏒᏃ ᎢᎦᏛ ᏅᏃᎱᎶᏗ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ, ᏥᏍᏆᏃ ᎤᏂᎷᏤ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏄᏘᏐᏁᎢ.
011304.phonetic: U-wi-sv-no i-ga-dv nv-no-hu-lo-di u-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i, tsi-s-qua-no u-ni-lu-tse a-le u-nu-ti-so-ne-i.

011305.english: Some fell upon stony places, where they had not much earth: and forthwith they sprung up, because they had no deepness of earth:
