011305.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᏅᏲᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ, ᎦᏲᎵ ᎦᏓ ᎦᎳᎨᏴᎢ, ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᎵᏰᏁᎢ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᏌᎨᎢᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎦᏓ.
011305.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no nv-yo-hi ge-sv u-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i, ga-yo-li ga-da ga-la-ge-yv-i, gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv u-li-ye-ne-i, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge sa-ge-i-yu ge-sv ga-da.

011306.english: And when the sun was up, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.
011306.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏅᏙ ᏧᎧᎸᏨ ᎤᎴᏴᏎᎢ, ᏂᏚᎿᎭᏍᏕᏢᎾᏃ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᎿᎭᏍᎬᏤᏉ.
011306.phonetic: A-se-no nv-do tsu-ka-lv-tsv u-le-yv-se-i, ni-du-hna-s-de-tlv-na-no ge-sv i-yu-s-di u-hna-s-gv-tse-quo.

011307.english: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprung up, and choked them:
011307.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎠᏄᎦᎸᎯ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ, ᎠᏄᎦᎸᏃ ᏧᏛᏒ ᎤᏁᏄᎳᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
011307.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no a-nu-ga-lv-hi u-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i, a-nu-ga-lv-no tsu-dv-sv u-ne-nu-la-s-ta-ne-i.

011308.english: But other fell into good ground, and brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold.
011308.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏍᎩᏂ ᎣᏒ ᎦᏙᎯ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎦᏔᏔᏁᎢ, ᎢᎦᏛ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᎤᏁᏉᏤᎢ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᏑᏓᎳᏍᎪᎯ ᎤᏁᏉᏤᎢ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᏦᎠᏍᎪᎯ ᎤᏁᏉᏤᎢ.
011308.phonetic: I-ga-dv-s-gi-ni o-sv ga-do-hi u-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i, na-s-gi-no u-ga-ta-ta-ne-i, i-ga-dv a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui u-ne-quo-tse-i, i-ga-dv-no su-da-la-s-go-hi u-ne-quo-tse-i, i-ga-dv-no tso-a-s-go-hi u-ne-quo-tse-i.

011309.english: Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.

011310.english: And the disciples came, and said unto him, Why speakest thou unto them in parables?
011310.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᏕᎯᏬᏁᏗᏍᎬ ᏥᏕᎭᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎭ?
011310.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no gv-wa-lu-tse-lv hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; Ga-do-no de-hi-wo-ne-di-s-gv tsi-de-ha-tli-lo-s-da-ne-ha?

011311.english: He answered and said unto them, Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.
011311.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᏂᎯ ᎡᏥᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏦᎵᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬ ᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, Ꮎ-ᏍᎩᏂ ᎥᏝ ᎨᎦᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ.
011311.phonetic: U-ne-tsv-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gi-ni ni-hi e-tsi-ne-lv-hi ge-sv i-tso-li-s-di-yi u-de-li-dv ge-sv ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i, na--s-gi-ni v-tla ge-ga-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-lv-hi yi-gi.

011312.english: For whosoever hath, to him shall be given, and he shall have more abundance: but whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken away even that he hath.
011312.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎤᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎤᏣᏘᏃ ᎤᎮᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂ ᏄᎲᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᎩᎡᏗᏉ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᎲᎢ.
011312.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no u-he-s-di a-tsi-ne-di ge-se-s-di, u-tsa-ti-no u-he-s-di; gi-lo-s-gi-ni nu-hv-na ge-se-s-di a-tsi-gi-e-di-quo ge-se-s-di na-s-gi na-s-quo u-hv-i.

011313.english: Therefore speak I to them in parables: because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.
011313.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᏥᏕᎦᏥᏯᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎭ, ᎠᏂᎪᏩᏘᏍᎬᏰᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᎾᏛᎩᎭ ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏃᎵᎦ.
011313.phonetic: Na-s-gi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha tsi-de-ga-tsi-ya-tli-lo-s-da-ne-ha, a-ni-go-wa-ti-s-gv-ye-no v-tla yi-na-dv-gi-ha a-le v-tla yi-no-li-ga.

011314.english: And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and shall not perceive:
011314.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎠᏁᎲ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏅ ᎢᏌᏯ ᏧᏙᎴᎰᏎᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎢᏣᏛᎩᏍᎬ ᎢᏣᏛᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏦᎵᎨᏍᏗ; ᎠᎴ ᎢᏥᎪᏩᏘᏍᎬ ᎢᏥᎪᏩᏘᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏣᏙᎴᎰᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
011314.phonetic: A-le a-ne-hv u-do-hi-yu-nv I-sa-ya tsu-do-le-ho-se-i, hi-a tsi-nu-we-se-i; I-tsa-dv-gi-s-gv i-tsa-dv-gi-s-ge-s-di, a-se-no v-tla yi-tso-li-ge-s-di; a-le i-tsi-go-wa-ti-s-gv i-tsi-go-wa-ti-s-ge-s-di, a-se-no v-tla yi-tsa-do-le-ho-s-ge-s-di.

011315.english: For this people's heart is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes they have closed; lest at any time they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and should understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.
011315.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏴᏫ ᏧᏂᎾᏫ ᏚᎵᏦᎲᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏂᎵᎷᎬ ᎦᏲᎵᏳ ᏓᏛᎩᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎦᏙᎵ ᏚᏂᏍᏚᏅ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏂᎦᏙᎵ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᎪᏩᏛᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏂᎵᎷᎬ ᏗᎬᏩᎾᏛᎪᏙᏗᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏂᎾᏫ ᏗᎬᏩᏃᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎬᏩᎾᏓᏁᏟᏴᎡᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᎦᏥᏅᏬᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
011315.phonetic: Hi-a-ye-no yv-wi tsu-ni-na-wi du-li-tso-hv-sv, a-le da-ni-li-lu-gv ga-yo-li-yu da-dv-gi-ha, a-le di-ni-ga-do-li du-ni-s-du-nv; na-s-gi di-ni-ga-do-li di-gv-wa-ni-go-wa-dv-do-di ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi, a-le da-ni-li-lu-gv di-gv-wa-na-dv-go-do-di-ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi, a-le tsu-ni-na-wi di-gv-wa-no-li-s-do-di ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi, a-le di-gv-wa-na-da-ne-tli-yv-e-di ni-ge-sv-na du-na-da-nv-dv, a-le di-ga-ga-tsi-nv-wo-di ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi.

011316.english: But blessed are your eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear.
011316.cherokee: ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᏗᏥᎦᏙᎵ ᏗᎦᎸᏉᏔᏅᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᏓᎪᏩᏘᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᏥᎵᎷᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᏓᏛᎩᎭ;
011316.phonetic: Ni-hi-s-gi-ni di-tsi-ga-do-li di-ga-lv-quo-ta-nv-hi, na-s-gi-ye-no da-go-wa-ti-ha, a-le de-tsi-li-lu-gv-i, na-s-gi-ye-no da-dv-gi-ha;

011317.english: For verily I say unto you, That many prophets and righteous men have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard them.
011317.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᎤᎾᏚᎸᏛ ᎤᏥᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏥᎪᏩᏘᎭ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏂᎪᎲᎯ ᏱᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏛᎪᏗᏱ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏣᏛᎩᎭ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᎯ ᏱᎩ.
011317.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya-ye-no hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha, u-ni-tsa-ti a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi a-le u-na-da-nv-ti u-na-du-lv-dv u-tsi-go-wa-dv-di-yi hi-a na-s-gi tsi-tsi-go-wa-ti-ha, a-se-no v-tla u-ni-go-hv-hi yi-gi; a-le u-na-dv-go-di-yi hi-a na-s-gi tsi-tsa-dv-gi-ha, a-se-no v-tla u-na-dv-ga-nv-hi yi-gi.

011318.english: Hear ye therefore the parable of the sower.
011318.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏣᏛᎬᎦ ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏛ ᎠᏫᏍᎩ.
011318.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di ni-hi i-tsa-dv-gv-ga da-tli-lo-s-dv a-wi-s-gi.

011319.english: When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, then cometh the wicked one, and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart. This is he which received seed by the way side.
011319.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏛᎬᎦ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᎾᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ, ᏂᎪᎵᎬᎾᏃ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏁᎫᏥᏛ ᎦᎷᎪᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎩᎡᎰ ᎠᏫᏒ ᎤᎾᏫᏱ.  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎤᏃᎱᎶᏗ ᏣᏫᏎᎢ.
011319.phonetic: I-yu-no gi-lo a-dv-gv-ga ka-no-he-dv ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-na-yu-hi ge-sv u-gv-wa-li, ni-go-li-gv-na-no tsi-ge-so-i, hna-quo u-ne-gu-tsi-dv ga-lu-go-i a-le u-gi-e-ho a-wi-sv u-na-wi-yi.  Na-s-gi hi-a u-no-hu-lo-di tsa-wi-se-i.

011320.english: But he that received the seed into stony places, the same is he that heareth the word, and anon with joy receiveth it;
011320.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏲᎯ ᏣᏫᏎᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏛᎩᏍᎪ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏨᎯ ᏥᏓᏓᏂᎸᎪᎢ;
011320.phonetic: Hi-a-no na-s-gi nv-yo-hi tsa-wi-se-i; na-s-gi tsa-dv-gi-s-go ka-no-he-dv, a-le gi-la-quo i-yv-dv u-li-he-li-tsv-hi tsi-da-da-ni-lv-go-i;

011321.english: Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while: for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended.
011321.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏂᏚᎿᎭᏍᏕᏢᎾᏉ ᎨᏐᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏞᎦᏉ ᏥᏓᎧᎿᎭᏩᏕᎪᎢ; ᎠᎩᎵᏴᏰᏃ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏛᎵᏰᎢᎸᏍᏓ ᎠᎦᏕᏯᏙᏓ, ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᏬᏕᎯᎰᎢ.
011321.phonetic: A-se-no ni-du-hna-s-de-tlv-na-quo ge-so-i, a-le tle-ga-quo tsi-da-ka-hna-wa-de-go-i; a-gi-li-yv-ye-no ga-na-nu-go-i a-le ka-no-he-dv dv-li-ye-i-lv-s-da a-ga-de-ya-do-da, gi-la-quo i-yv-dv du-wo-de-hi-ho-i.

011322.english: He also that received seed among the thorns is he that heareth the word; and the care of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, choke the word, and he becometh unfruitful.
011322.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏄᎦᎸᎯ ᏣᏫᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏛᎩᏍᎪ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ, ᎡᎶᎯᏃ ᎤᏪᎵᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎨᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏓᎵᏓᏍᏛ ᎠᏂᏁᏄᎳᏍᏗᏍᎪ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎾᎦᏔᏛᏍᎬᎾᏉ ᎨᏐᎢ.
011322.phonetic: Hi-a-no na-s-quo a-nu-ga-lv-hi tsa-wi-se-i, na-s-gi tsa-dv-gi-s-go ka-no-he-dv, e-lo-hi-no u-we-li-hi-s-di ge-sv a-le ge-nv-i-s-di ge-sv u-da-li-da-s-dv a-ni-ne-nu-la-s-di-s-go ka-no-he-dv, na-s-gi-no na-ga-ta-dv-s-gv-na-quo ge-so-i.

011323.english: But he that received seed into the good ground is he that heareth the word, and understandeth it; which also beareth fruit, and bringeth forth, some an hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.
011323.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ Ꮎ ᎣᏒ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏣᏫᏎᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏛᎩᏍᎪ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏥᎪᎵᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏣᎦᏔᏛᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪᎢ, ᎢᎦᏛ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᎤᏁᏉᏨᎯ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᏑᏓᎳᏍᎪᎯ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᏦᎠᏍᎪᎯ.
011323.phonetic: Na-s-gi-s-gi-ni na o-sv ga-do-hi tsa-wi-se-i; na-s-gi tsa-dv-gi-s-go ka-no-he-dv a-le tsi-go-li-go-i, a-le na-s-quo tsa-ga-ta-dv-s-go-i, a-le tsi-ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-go-i, i-ga-dv a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui u-ne-quo-tsv-hi, i-ga-dv-no su-da-la-s-go-hi, i-ga-dv-no tso-a-s-go-hi.

011324.english: Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good seed in his field:
011324.cherokee: ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᏚᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏉ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᎦᏔ ᎤᎶᎨᏒ ᏧᏫᏎᎢ.
011324.phonetic: Nv-wa-da-le du-tli-lo-s-da-ne-lv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv na-s-gi-ya-quo a-s-ga-ya o-s-dv u-ga-ta u-lo-ge-sv tsu-wi-se-i.

011325.english: But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way.
011325.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏂᎵᏅᏨ, ᎤᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᎷᏤ ᎤᏲ ᎤᎦᏔ ᎤᏫᏎ ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗ ᎠᏫᏒ ᎤᏑᏴᏁᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎢᎤᏪᏅᏎ.
011325.phonetic: A-se-no yv-wi u-ni-li-nv-tsv, u-s-ga-ya u-lu-tse u-yo u-ga-ta u-wi-se u-tsa-le-s-di a-wi-sv u-su-yv-ne-i; a-le i-u-we-nv-se.

011326.english: But when the blade was sprung up, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also.
011326.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᎵᏰᏅ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎦᏔᏔᏅ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏲ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏤᎢ.
011326.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-li-ye-nv a-le u-ga-ta-ta-nv na-s-quo u-yo u-na-nu-go-tse-i.

011327.english: So the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst not thou sow good seed in thy field? from whence then hath it tares?
011327.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎦᏁᎳ ᏧᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏍᎩᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ, ᏝᏍᎪ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᎦᏔ ᏱᏣᏫᏎ ᏣᎶᎨᏒᎢ?  ᎦᏙ ᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓᎭ ᎤᏲ ᏧᏰᎦ?
011327.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-s-ga-ya ga-ne-la tsu-nv-si-da-s-di gv-wa-lu-tse-le-i, hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i; s-gi-nv-si-da-s-di, Tla-s-go o-s-dv u-ga-ta yi-tsa-wi-se tsa-lo-ge-sv-i?  Ga-do di-ga-li-s-do-da-ha u-yo tsu-ye-ga?

011328.english: He said unto them, An enemy hath done this. The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?
011328.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎩᎶ ᎠᎩᏍᎦᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏛᏁᎸ.  ᏧᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏎᎴᎢ; ᏣᏚᎵᏍᎪᏃ ᎣᎩᏰᎯᏒᏍᏗᏱ?
011328.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i; Gi-lo a-gi-s-ga-gi na-s-gi nu-dv-ne-lv.  Tsu-nv-si-da-s-di hi-a ni-gv-wa-se-le-i; Tsa-du-li-s-go-no o-gi-ye-hi-sv-s-di-yi?

011329.english: But he said, Nay; lest while ye gather up the tares, ye root up also the wheat with them.
011329.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎥᏝ, ᎢᏥᏰᎯᏍᎬᏰᏃ ᎤᏲ, ᏱᏕᏥᎿᎭᏍᏕᏢᏓ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗ.
011329.phonetic: A-se-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; v-tla, i-tsi-ye-hi-s-gv-ye-no u-yo, yi-de-tsi-hna-s-de-tlv-da na-s-quo u-tsa-le-s-di.

011330.english: Let both grow together until the harvest: and in the time of harvest I will say to the reapers, Gather ye together first the tares, and bind them in bundles to burn them: but gather the wheat into my barn.
011330.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᎢᎫᎳᎭ ᏫᏓᏛᎯ ᎬᏂ ᎠᎦᏛᎾᏨᎭ; ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᎦᏛᎾᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏅᏓᎦᏥᏪᏎᎵ ᎠᏂᏍᎫᏕᏍᎩ; ᎢᎬᏱ ᎢᏥᏟᏌ ᎤᏲ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏦᏛᏣ ᏗᎪᎲᏍᏙᏗ, ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᏥᏟᏌ ᎠᎩᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗᏱ.
011330.phonetic: U-ne-la-gi i-gu-la-ha wi-da-dv-hi gv-ni a-ga-dv-na-tsv-ha; hna-quo-no a-ga-dv-na-tsv hi-a nv-da-ga-tsi-we-se-li a-ni-s-gu-de-s-gi; I-gv-yi i-tsi-tli-sa u-yo, a-le di-tso-dv-tsa di-go-hv-s-do-di, u-tsa-le-s-di-s-gi-ni i-tsi-tli-sa a-gi-s-qua-ni-go-do-di-yi.

011331.english: Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is like to a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and sowed in his field:
011331.cherokee: ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᏚᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏉ ᎠᏥᎸ ᎤᎦᏔ, ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏧᎩᏎ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏫᏎ ᎤᎶᎨᏒᎢ.
011331.phonetic: Nv-wa-da-le du-tli-lo-s-da-ne-lv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv na-s-gi-ya-quo a-tsi-lv u-ga-ta, a-s-ga-ya tsu-gi-se a-le tsu-wi-se u-lo-ge-sv-i.

011332.english: Which indeed is the least of all seeds: but when it is grown, it is the greatest among herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the birds of the air come and lodge in the branches thereof.
011332.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏒ ᏥᏬᏓᎪᎾᏛᏗ ᎤᏍᏗᎩᏳ ᎨᏒ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᎦᏔ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏛᎯ ᏥᏬᏓᎪᎾᏛᏗ ᎤᏛᏂᏳ ᎨᏒ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏰᎿᎭᎥᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏡᎬ ᏥᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏥᏍᏆ ᏣᏂᎷᎪᎢ, ᏚᏩᏂᎦᎸ ᏣᎾᎭᏗᏍᎪᎢ.
011332.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-do-hi-yu-sv tsi-wo-da-go-na-dv-di u-s-di-gi-yu ge-sv ni-ga-v u-ga-ta, a-se-no a-dv-hi tsi-wo-da-go-na-dv-di u-dv-ni-yu ge-sv ni-ga-v u-ye-hna-v-i, a-le tlu-gv tsi-ni-ga-li-s-di-ha, na-s-gi-no tsi-s-qua tsa-ni-lu-go-i, du-wa-ni-ga-lv tsa-na-ha-di-s-go-i.

011333.english: Another parable spake he unto them; The kingdom of heaven is like unto leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.
011333.cherokee: ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᏚᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏬᏒᎩ; ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏉ ᎠᎪᏙᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎨᏴ ᏧᎩᏎ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏑᏴᏁ ᏦᎢ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᎢᏒ ᎦᎸᎢ, ᎬᏂ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᎪᏐᏅ.
011333.phonetic: Nv-wa-da-le du-tli-lo-s-da-ne-lv-gi, hi-a nu-wo-sv-gi; Ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv na-s-gi-ya-quo a-go-do-di, na-s-gi a-ge-yv tsu-gi-se a-le tsu-su-yv-ne tso-i i-yu-tli-lo-dv i-sv ga-lv-i, gv-ni ni-ga-dv u-go-so-nv.

011334.english: All these things spake Jesus unto the multitude in parables; and without a parable spake he not unto them:
011334.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏗᏳ ᏥᏌ ᏚᏬᏁᏔᏅᎩ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏚᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ, ᏂᏓᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎲᎾᏃ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏚᏬᏁᏔᏁᎢ.
011334.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a ni-ga-di-yu Tsi-sa du-wo-ne-ta-nv-gi u-ni-tsa-ti du-tli-lo-s-da-ne-lv-gi, ni-da-tli-lo-s-da-ne-hv-na-no ge-sv v-tla yi-du-wo-ne-ta-ne-i.

011335.english: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying, I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter things which have been kept secret from the foundation of the world.
011335.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏗᏱ ᎠᏰᎸᏒᎩ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᏧᏁᏤᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏓᏥᏍᏚᎢᏏ ᏥᎣᎵ ᏕᎦᏟᎶᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᏓᏥᏁᎢᏍᏔᏂ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᎠᏍᏆᏂᎪᏛ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏧᏙᏢᏅ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏓᎴᏅᏛ.
011335.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-di-yi a-ye-lv-sv-gi a-do-le-ho-s-gi tsu-ne-tse-i, hi-a tsi-nu-we-se-i; da-tsi-s-du-i-si tsi-o-li de-ga-tli-lo-s-di-s-gv-i, da-tsi-ne-i-s-ta-ni u-de-li-dv a-s-qua-ni-go-dv e-lo-hi tsu-do-tlv-nv nv-da-gv-wa-da-le-nv-dv.

011336.english: Then Jesus sent the multitude away, and went into the house: and his disciples came unto him, saying, Declare unto us the parable of the tares of the field.
011336.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᏚᏰᎵᎯᏍᏔᏅ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ, ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᏭᏴᎸᎩ; ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏗᏍᎩᏲᏏᏌᏏ ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏛ ᎤᏲ ᏧᎵᏰᏁ ᏠᎨᏏ.
011336.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa du-ye-li-hi-s-ta-nv u-ni-tsa-ti, a-da-ne-lv wu-yv-lv-gi; gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-gi, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Di-s-gi-yo-si-sa-si da-tli-lo-s-dv u-yo tsu-li-ye-ne tlo-ge-si.

011337.english: He answered and said unto them, He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man;
011337.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᎦᏔ ᏧᏫᏎᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ;
011337.phonetic: U-ne-tsv-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; na-s-gi na O-s-dv u-ga-ta tsu-wi-se-i na-s-gi yv-wi U-we-tsi;

011338.english: The field is the world; the good seed are the children of the kingdom; but the tares are the children of the wicked one;
011338.cherokee: ᏠᎨᏏᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᎳᏂᎬᎢ; ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᎦᏔ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏁᎯ; ᎤᏲᏃ ᏧᎵᏰᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎫᏥᏛ ᏧᏪᏥ;
011338.phonetic: Tlo-ge-si-no na-s-gi e-la-ni-gv-i; o-s-dv u-ga-ta na-s-gi ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv a-ne-hi; u-yo-no tsu-li-ye-ne na-s-gi u-ne-gu-tsi-dv tsu-we-tsi;

011339.english: The enemy that sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are the angels.
011339.cherokee: ᎠᎦᏍᎩᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏫᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ; ᎤᎦᏛᎾᎢᏍᏗᏱᏃ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎵᏍᏆᏛᎭ ᎡᎶᎯ; ᎠᏂᏍᎫᏕᏍᎩᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ.
011339.phonetic: A-ga-s-gi-no na-s-gi tsu-wi-se-i, na-s-gi a-s-gi-na; u-ga-dv-na-i-s-di-yi-no ge-sv na-s-gi a-li-s-qua-dv-ha e-lo-hi; a-ni-s-gu-de-s-gi-no na-s-gi di-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi.

011340.english: As therefore the tares are gathered and burned in the fire; so shall it be in the end of this world.
011340.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏲ ᏣᏂᏟᏏᏍᎪ ᎠᎴ ᏣᎾᎪᎲᏍᏗᏍᎪ ᎠᏥᎸᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏍᎩᏂ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏄᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎠᎵᏍᏆᏛᎭ ᎯᎠ ᎡᎶᎯ.
011340.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-yo tsa-ni-tli-si-s-go a-le tsa-na-go-hv-s-di-s-go a-tsi-lv-yi, na-s-gi-ya-s-gi-ni na-s-quo nu-s-de-s-di a-li-s-qua-dv-ha hi-a e-lo-hi.

011341.english: The Son of man shall send forth his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that offend, and them which do iniquity;
011341.cherokee: ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏙᏓᎦᏅᏏ ᏧᏤᎵ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᏙᏛᏃᏣᎴᏏ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏗᎾᏓᏙᏕᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏅᎩ,
011341.phonetic: Yv-wi U-we-tsi do-da-ga-nv-si tsu-tse-li di-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi, a-le na-s-gi u-tse-li-go-hi do-dv-no-tsa-le-si ni-ga-dv di-na-da-do-de-s-di-s-gi a-le ni-ga-dv a-ni-s-ga-nv-gi,

011342.english: And shall cast them into a furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
011342.cherokee: ᏗᎪᏢᏒᏃ ᎠᏥᎸᏱ ᏫᏙᏛᎾᏓᎡᏏ; ᎾᎿᎭᏓᏂᏴᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏓᏂᎸᏓᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏓᏂᏄᏙᎬᎢ.
011342.phonetic: Di-go-tlv-sv-no a-tsi-lv-yi wi-do-dv-na-da-e-si; na-hna da-ni-yv-ge-s-di a-le da-ni-lv-da-gi-s-ge-s-di da-ni-nu-do-gv-i.

011343.english: Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.
011343.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᎬᏩᏂᏔᎷᎩᏍᎩ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏙᏓ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ, ᏅᏙ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ.  ᎩᎶ ᏕᎦᎵᎷᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏛᎪᏗᏱ ᏩᏛᎬᎦ.
011343.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-na-da-nv-ti gv-wa-ni-ta-lu-gi-s-gi ge-se-s-di U-ni-do-da u-tse-li-go-hi, nv-do na-s-gi-ya-i.  Gi-lo de-ga-li-lu-ge-s-di u-dv-go-di-yi wa-dv-gv-ga.

011344.english: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field; the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for joy thereof goeth and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field.
011344.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏉ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᏠᎨᏏ ᏣᎰᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᏩᏛ, ᏣᏕᎸᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎵᎮᎵᎬᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏘ ᏤᎪᎢ ᏥᏫᎦᎾᏕᎪ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏭᏩᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏠᎨᏏ.
011344.phonetic: A-le na-s-quo Ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv na-s-gi-ya-quo tsu-gv-wa-lo-di u-de-li-dv tlo-ge-si tsa-ho-i, na-s-gi i-yu a-s-ga-ya a-wa-dv, tsa-de-lv-s-go-i, a-li-he-li-gv-no i-yu-s-ti tse-go-i tsi-wi-ga-na-de-go ni-ga-dv u-s-qua-ni-go-dv-i, a-le tsi-wu-wa-s-go na-s-gi tlo-ge-si.

011345.english: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a merchant man, seeking goodly pearls:
011345.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏉ ᎦᏃᏗᏍᎩ ᏥᏚᏲᎮ ᏧᏬᏚᎯ ᏓᎬᎾ-ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ.
011345.phonetic: A-le na-s-quo Ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv na-s-gi-ya-quo ga-no-di-s-gi tsi-du-yo-he tsu-wo-du-hi da-gv-na--i-yu-na-s-di.

011346.english: Who, when he had found one pearl of great price, went and sold all that he had, and bought it.
011346.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏩᏛᎲ ᏌᏉ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᏓᎬᎾ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏪᏅᏎ ᏭᎾᏗᏅᏎ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏛᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏩᏎᎢ.
011346.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hna-quo u-wa-dv-hv sa-quo u-tsa-ti tsu-gv-wa-lo-di da-gv-na i-yu-s-di u-we-nv-se wu-na-di-nv-se ni-ga-dv u-s-qua-ni-go-dv-i, na-s-gi-no u-wa-se-i.

011347.english: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a net, that was cast into the sea, and gathered of every kind:
011347.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏉ ᎠᎦᏯᎷᏗ ᏥᏭᎾᏓᎢᏅᏎ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏚᏂᏂᏴᎮ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ;
011347.phonetic: A-le na-s-quo Ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv na-s-gi-ya-quo a-ga-ya-lu-di tsi-wu-na-da-i-nv-se a-me-quo-hi, a-le tsi-du-ni-ni-yv-he na-ni-v tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv;

011348.english: Which, when it was full, they drew to shore, and sat down, and gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away.
011348.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᎧᎵᏨ ᎠᎹᏳᎶᏗ ᎤᏂᎾᏏᏃᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏅᏁᎢ, ᎠᏃᏍᏛᏃ ᏗᏖᎵᏙ ᏚᏂᎸᏔᏁᎢ, ᎤᏂᏲᏃ ᏚᎾᏓᎢᏅᏎᎢ.
011348.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hna-quo u-ka-li-tsv a-ma-yu-lo-di u-ni-na-si-no-le-i, a-le u-na-nv-ne-i, a-no-s-dv-no di-te-li-do du-ni-lv-ta-ne-i, u-ni-yo-no du-na-da-i-nv-se-i.

011349.english: So shall it be at the end of the world: the angels shall come forth, and sever the wicked from among the just,
011349.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᏄᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᎵᏍᏆᏛᎭ; ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏛᏂᎷᏥ, ᏙᏛᎾᎦᎴᏅᏔᏂ ᎤᏂᏁᎫᏥᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ;
011349.phonetic: Na-s-gi-s-gi-ni nu-s-de-s-di e-lo-hi a-li-s-qua-dv-ha; di-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi dv-ni-lu-tsi, do-dv-na-ga-le-nv-ta-ni u-ni-ne-gu-tsi-dv a-le u-na-da-nv-ti;

011350.english: And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
011350.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏁᎫᏥᏛᏃ ᏫᏙᏛᎾᏓᎡᏏ ᏗᎪᏢᏒ ᎠᏥᎸᏱ, ᎾᎿᎭᏓᏂᏴᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏓᏂᎸᏓᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏓᏂᏄᏙᎬᎢ.
011350.phonetic: U-ni-ne-gu-tsi-dv-no wi-do-dv-na-da-e-si di-go-tlv-sv a-tsi-lv-yi, na-hna da-ni-yv-ge-s-di a-le da-ni-lv-da-gi-s-ge-s-di da-ni-nu-do-gv-i.

011351.english: Jesus saith unto them, Have ye understood all these things? They say unto him, Yea, Lord.
011351.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏦᎵᎩᏧ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎯᎠ?  ᎥᎥ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎥᎬᏬᏎᎸᎩ.
011351.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; i-tso-li-gi-tsu ni-ga-dv hi-a?  v-v, tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, v-gv-wo-se-lv-gi.

011352.english: Then said he unto them, Therefore every scribe which is instructed unto the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which bringeth forth out of his treasure things new and old.
011352.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᏗᎪᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᎠᎨᏲᏅᎯ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏉ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎦᏁᎳ ᏥᏙᏗᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏛ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏗᏤ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏪᏘ.
011352.phonetic: Hna-quo-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di gi-lo di-go-we-li-s-gi ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv u-gv-wa-li a-ge-yo-nv-hi tsi-ge-so-i, na-s-gi-ya-quo a-s-ga-ya ga-ne-la tsi-do-di-ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-go tsu-gv-wa-lo-di u-s-qua-ni-go-dv tsu-da-le-nv-dv di-tse a-le tsu-we-ti.

011353.english: And it came to pass, that when Jesus had finished these parables, he departed thence.
011353.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏍᏆᏛ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᏂᎩᏒᎩ.
011353.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi, hna-quo Tsi-sa u-s-qua-dv hi-a na-s-gi da-tli-lo-s-di-s-gv-i, u-ni-gi-sv-gi.

011354.english: And when he was come into his own country, he taught them in their synagogue, insomuch that they were astonished, and said, Whence hath this man this wisdom, and these mighty works?
011354.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎤᎷᏨ ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏚᏪᏲᏅᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᏙ ᎤᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏣᎦᏔ ᎿᎭᎠ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏥᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎭ?
011354.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-wa-sv u-tse-li-go-hi u-lu-tsv tsu-ni-la-wi-i-s-di-yi du-we-yo-nv-gi; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-sv-gi, a-le hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Ga-do u-li-s-do-ta-nv hi-a na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya tsa-ga-ta hna-a a-le u-s-qua-ni-go-di tsi-du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-ha?

011355.english: Is not this the carpenter's son? is not his mother called Mary? and his brethren, James, and Joses, and Simon, and Judas?
011355.cherokee: ᎯᎠᎧᏂ ᏗᏁᏍᎨᏍᎩ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᎺᎵᎧᏂ ᎤᏥ, ᎠᎴᎧᏂ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ ᏥᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᏦᏏ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏩᏂ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏓᏏ?
011355.phonetic: Hi-a-ka-ni di-ne-s-ge-s-gi u-we-tsi, Me-li-ka-ni u-tsi, a-le-ka-ni a-na-li-nv-tli Tsi-mi, a-le Tso-si a-le Sa-wa-ni a-le Tsu-da-si?

011356.english: And his sisters, are they not all with us? Whence then hath this man all these things?
011356.cherokee: ᎠᎴᎧᏂ ᏧᏙ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎡᏍᎦᏂ ᏣᏁᎭ?  ᎦᏙᏃ ᎤᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏧᏪᎭ?
011356.phonetic: A-le-ka-ni tsu-do ni-ga-dv e-s-ga-ni tsa-ne-ha?  Ga-do-no u-li-s-do-ta-nv hi-a ni-ga-dv tsu-we-ha?

011357.english: And they were offended in him. But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, and in his own house.
011357.cherokee: ᎥᎬᏩᎪᏁᎶᎯᏎᎸᎩᏃ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎾᏥᎸᏉᏛᎾ ᏱᎨᏐᎢ, ᎤᏩᏒᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ.
011357.phonetic: V-gv-wa-go-ne-lo-hi-se-lv-gi-no; a-se-no Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; A-do-le-ho-s-gi v-tla na-tsi-lv-quo-dv-na yi-ge-so-i, u-wa-sv-s-gi-ni-no-nv u-tse-li-go-hi a-le u-wa-sv ga-ne-lv-i.

011358.english: And he did not many mighty works there because of their unbelief.
011358.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏃ ᏳᏣᏖ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᎾᎿᎭᏱᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎴᎢ, ᎾᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎨᎾ ᎨᏒ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ.
011358.phonetic: V-tla-no yu-tsa-te u-s-qua-ni-go-di na-hna yi-du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-le-i, na-no-hi-yu-hv-s-ge-na ge-sv nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi.

011401.english: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard of the fame of Jesus,
011401.cherokee: ᎾᏁᏳ ᎡᎶᏛ ᏅᎩ ᎢᎦᏚᎩ ᎨᏒ ᏍᏓᏚᎩ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ ᎤᏛᎦᏁ ᏕᎦᏃᏣᎸ ᏥᏌ.
011401.phonetic: Na-ne-yu E-lo-dv nv-gi i-ga-du-gi ge-sv s-da-du-gi a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di u-dv-ga-ne de-ga-no-tsa-lv Tsi-sa.

011402.english: And said unto his servants, This is John the Baptist; he is risen from the dead; and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him.
011402.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴ ᏧᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ; ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏂ ᏗᏓᏪᏍᎩ; ᏙᎤᎴᏅ ᏧᏲᎱᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗᏳ ᏥᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎭ.
011402.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le tsu-nv-si-da-s-di; Hi-a na-s-gi Tsa-ni Di-da-we-s-gi; do-u-le-nv tsu-yo-hu-sv-gi, a-le na-s-gi i-yu-s-di u-s-qua-ni-go-di-yu tsi-du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-ha.

011403.english: For Herod had laid hold on John, and bound him, and put him in prison for Herodias' sake, his brother Philip's wife.
011403.cherokee: ᎡᎶᏛᏰᏃ ᎤᏂᏴᎮ ᏣᏂ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎸᎴᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏚᏁ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ,  ᏈᎵᎩ ᎡᎶᏛ ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ ᎡᎶᏗᏏ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᎤᏍᏛᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
011403.phonetic: E-lo-dv-ye-no u-ni-yv-he Tsa-ni a-le u-lv-le-i a-le u-s-du-ne di-da-s-du-di-yi,  Qui-li-gi E-lo-dv di-na-da-nv-tli u-da-li-i E-lo-di-si tsu-do-i-dv u-s-dv-di-s-ge-i.

011404.english: For John said unto him, It is not lawful for thee to have her.
011404.cherokee: ᏣᏂᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎡᎶᏛ; ᎥᏝ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏱᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏓᏰᎭ.
011404.phonetic: Tsa-ni-ye-no hi-a nu-we-se-le E-lo-dv; v-tla du-yu-go-dv yi-gi na-s-gi tsa-da-ye-ha.

011405.english: And when he would have put him to death, he feared the multitude, because they counted him as a prophet.
011405.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎨ ᎤᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᏓᏍᎦᎢᎮ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏎ ᏣᏂ.
011405.phonetic: A-le u-du-li-s-ge u-hi-s-di-yi, a-se-no da-s-ga-i-he yv-wi, a-do-le-ho-s-gi-ye-no u-ni-ye-lv-se Tsa-ni.

011406.english: But when Herod's birthday was kept, the daughter of Herodias danced before them, and pleased Herod.
011406.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎡᎶᏛ ᎤᏕᏅ ᎢᏳ ᎢᎦ ᏚᏂᎳᏫᏨ, ᎡᎶᏗᏏ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᎩᎡᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏎ ᎤᏰᎸᏁ ᎡᎶᏛ.
011406.phonetic: A-se-no hna-quo E-lo-dv u-de-nv i-yu i-ga du-ni-la-wi-tsv, E-lo-di-si u-we-tsi a-dv u-na-li-s-gi-e-le-i, a-le o-se u-ye-lv-ne E-lo-dv.

011407.english: Whereupon he promised with an oath to give her whatsoever she would ask.
011407.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏎᎵᏔᏁ ᎤᏚᎢᏍᏓᏁᎴ ᎤᏁᏗᏱ ᏄᏍᏛᏉ ᎤᏔᏲᎸᎢ.
011407.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di u-se-li-ta-ne u-du-i-s-da-ne-le u-ne-di-yi nu-s-dv-quo u-ta-yo-lv-i.

011408.english: And she, being before instructed of her mother, said, Give me here John Baptist's head in a charger.
011408.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏥ ᎤᏪᏲᏅᎯ ᏂᎨᏎᎢ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎠᏂ ᏍᎩᏲᎮᎸᎭ ᏣᏂ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ ᎤᏍᎪ ᎠᏖᎵᏙ ᎦᎶᏕᏍᏗ.
011408.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-tsi u-we-yo-nv-hi ni-ge-se-i hi-a nu-we-se-i; A-ni s-gi-yo-he-lv-ha Tsa-ni Di-da-wo-s-gi u-s-go a-te-li-do ga-lo-de-s-di.

011409.english: And the king was sorry: nevertheless for the oath's sake, and them which sat with him at meat, he commanded it to be given her.
011409.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᏲᎢᏳ ᎤᏰᎸᏁᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏎᎵᏔᏅᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎩ ᏅᏧᎵᏍᏙᏔᏁᎢ, ᎤᏁᏤ ᎠᏥᏁᏗᏱ.
011409.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no u-yo-i-yu u-ye-lv-ne-i, a-se-no u-se-li-ta-nv-i, a-le na-s-quo na-s-gi a-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gi nv-tsu-li-s-do-ta-ne-i, u-ne-tse a-tsi-ne-di-yi.

011410.english: And he sent, and beheaded John in the prison.

011411.english: And his head was brought in a charger, and given to the damsel: and she brought it to her mother.
011411.cherokee: ᎤᏍᎪᏃ ᎤᏂᏲᎴ ᎠᏖᎵᏙᎩᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏁᎴ ᎠᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏥ ᏭᏁᎴᎢ.
011411.phonetic: U-s-go-no u-ni-yo-le a-te-li-do-gi-hi, a-le u-ni-ne-le a-dv, na-s-gi-no u-tsi wu-ne-le-i.

011412.english: And his disciples came, and took up the body, and buried it, and went and told Jesus.
011412.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎤᏂᎷᏤ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏁᏎ ᎠᏰᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏂᏌᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏅᏎ ᏭᏂᏃᏁᎴ ᏥᏌ.
011412.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no u-ni-lu-tse a-le u-ni-ne-se a-ye-lv-i, a-le u-ni-ni-sa-ne-i, a-le u-ne-nv-se wu-ni-no-ne-le Tsi-sa.

011413.english: When Jesus heard of it, he departed thence by ship into a desert place apart: and when the people had heard thereof, they followed him on foot out of the cities.
011413.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ, ᏥᏳ ᎤᏦᏔᏅᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏂᎩᏒᎩ ᎤᏓᏰᎵᎸ ᎢᎾᎨ ᏭᎶᏒᎩ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ, ᎡᎳᏗ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᎡᎩ ᏕᎦᏚᎲ ᏙᏓᏳᏁᏅᏒᎩ.
011413.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-dv-ga-nv, tsi-yu u-tso-ta-nv-gi na-hna u-ni-gi-sv-gi u-da-ye-li-lv i-na-ge wu-lo-sv-gi.  Hna-quo-no yv-wi u-na-dv-ga-nv, e-la-di gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-e-gi de-ga-du-hv do-da-yu-ne-nv-sv-gi.

011414.english: And Jesus went forth, and saw a great multitude, and was moved with compassion toward them, and he healed their sick.
011414.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏣᎢᏒ ᏚᎪᎲᎩ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏪᏙᎵᏨᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏅᏩᏅ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ.
011414.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-tsa-i-sv du-go-hv-gi u-ni-tsa-ti yv-wi, a-le du-we-do-li-tsv-gi, a-le du-nv-wa-nv-tsu-ni-tlv-gi.

011415.english: And when it was evening, his disciples came to him, saying, This is a desert place, and the time is now past; send the multitude away, that they may go into the villages, and buy themselves victuals.
011415.cherokee: ᎤᏒᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏙᎯ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎠᏂ ᎢᎾᎨᏉ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎦ ᎤᎶᏐᏂᏗ, ᏥᏤᎾᏉᏃ ᎩᏲᏏ ᎯᎠ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᏙᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᏫᎠᏂᎶᎯ, ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏅᏒ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᎤᏂᏩᏒᎭ.
011415.phonetic: U-sv-no nu-li-s-ta-nv gv-wa-s-da-wa-do-hi gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-gi hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; A-ni i-na-ge-quo, a-le i-ga u-lo-so-ni-di, tsi-tse-na-quo-no gi-yo-si hi-a u-ni-tsa-ti yv-wi, a-le do-di-ga-du-hv wi-a-ni-lo-hi, na-hna u-nv-sv u-na-li-s-da-yv-di u-ni-wa-sv-ha.

011416.english: But Jesus said unto them, They need not depart; give ye them to eat.
011416.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᎠᏎ ᎥᎤᏁᏅᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ, ᏂᎯᏉ ᏗᏤᎳᏍᏓ.
011416.phonetic: A-se-no Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; V-tla a-se v-u-ne-nv-s-di yi-gi, ni-hi-quo di-tse-la-s-da.

011417.english: And they say unto him, We have here but five loaves, and two fishes.
011417.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏅᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎠᏂ ᎯᏍᎩᏉ ᎦᏚ ᏙᎩᎭ, ᏔᎵᏉᏃ ᎠᏣᏗ.
011417.phonetic: Hi-a-no nv-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; A-ni hi-s-gi-quo ga-du do-gi-ha, ta-li-quo-no a-tsa-di.

011418.english: He said, Bring them hither to me.

011419.english: And he commanded the multitude to sit down on the grass, and took the five loaves, and the two fishes, and looking up to heaven, he blessed, and brake, and gave the loaves to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.
011419.cherokee: ᏚᏁᏤᎸᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎧᏁᏍᎪᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎾᏅᏗᏱ, ᏚᎩᏒᏃ ᎯᏍᎩ ᎦᏚ ᎠᎴ ᏔᎵ ᎠᏣᏗ ᏚᏁᏒ, ᎦᎸᎳᏗᏃ ᏫᏚᎧᎾᏅ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏨᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎬᎭᎷᏴ ᏚᏁᎸᎩ ᎦᏚ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏫᏚᏂᏁᎸᎩ.
011419.phonetic: Du-ne-tse-lv-no u-ni-tsa-ti ka-ne-s-go-hi ge-sv u-na-nv-di-yi, du-gi-sv-no hi-s-gi ga-du a-le ta-li a-tsa-di du-ne-sv, ga-lv-la-di-no wi-du-ka-na-nv u-li-he-li-tsv-gi, a-le du-gv-ha-lu-yv du-ne-lv-gi ga-du gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, na-s-gi-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi u-ni-tsa-ti wi-du-ni-ne-lv-gi.

011420.english: And they did all eat, and were filled: and they took up of the fragments that remained twelve baskets full.
011420.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏅᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏃᎸᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏄᏘᏒᎩ ᎤᎵᎬᎭᎷᏴᎯ ᎤᏘᏴᎯ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏯᎧᎵᎢ ᏔᎷᏣ.
011420.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no u-na-li-s-da-yv-nv-gi a-le du-no-lv-sv-gi, a-le u-nu-ti-sv-gi u-li-gv-ha-lu-yv-hi u-ti-yv-hi ta-la-du i-ya-ka-li-i ta-lu-tsa.

011421.english: And they that had eaten were about five thousand men, beside women and children.
011421.cherokee: ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏅᎯᏃ ᎯᏍᎩᎭ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎾᏂᎥᎩ, ᏂᏓᏁᏢᏛᎾ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ.
011421.phonetic: U-na-li-s-da-yv-nv-hi-no hi-s-gi-ha i-ya-ga-yv-li a-ni-s-ga-ya na-ni-v-gi, ni-da-ne-tlv-dv-na a-ni-ge-yv a-le di-ni-yo-li.

011422.english: And straightway Jesus constrained his disciples to get into a ship, and to go before him unto the other side, while he sent the multitudes away.
011422.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏥᏌ ᏚᏅᏫᏍᏔᏅᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᎾᏦᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏂᏚᏂᏐᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏏ ᏓᏰᎵᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ.
011422.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv Tsi-sa du-nv-wi-s-ta-nv-gi gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi tsi-yu-hi u-na-tso-di-yi, a-le i-gv-yi ni-du-ni-so-hi-s-di-yi a-si da-ye-li-hi-s-di-s-gv u-ni-tsa-ti.

011423.english: And when he had sent the multitudes away, he went up into a mountain apart to pray: and when the evening was come, he was there alone.
011423.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏚᏰᎵᎯᏍᏔᏅ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎣᏓᎸ ᎤᎿᎭᎷᏒᎩ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅᏒᎩ.  ᎤᏒᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏩᏒᏉ ᎨᏒᎩ.
011423.phonetic: Hna-quo-no du-ye-li-hi-s-ta-nv u-ni-tsa-ti o-da-lv u-hna-lu-sv-gi u-wa-sv ge-sv-i, u-da-do-li-s-ta-nv-sv-gi.  U-sv-no nu-li-s-ta-nv na-hna u-wa-sv-quo ge-sv-gi.

011424.english: But the ship was now in the midst of the sea, tossed with waves: for the wind was contrary.
011424.cherokee: ᏥᏳᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎥᏓᎵ ᏩᏂᏂᏒᎩ ᎤᏝᏗᏍᏔᏂᏙᎲ ᎠᎹ ᏓᎵᏍᏗᎳᏁᎬᎢ, ᎤᏃᎴᏰᏃ ᎢᎬᏱᏢ ᏓᏳᎦᏛᎩ.
011424.phonetic: Tsi-yu-no hna-quo a-ye-li v-da-li wa-ni-ni-sv-gi u-tla-di-s-ta-ni-do-hv a-ma da-li-s-di-la-ne-gv-i, u-no-le-ye-no i-gv-yi-tlv da-yu-ga-dv-gi.

011425.english: And in the fourth watch of the night Jesus went unto them, walking on the sea.
011425.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏅᎩᏁ ᎢᏳᎾᏓᏁᏟᏴᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᏒᏃᏱ ᏥᏌ ᏫᏚᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᎥᏓᎵ ᎦᏚᎢ ᎠᎢᏒᎩ.
011425.phonetic: Hna-quo-no nv-gi-ne i-yu-na-da-ne-tli-yv-dv ge-sv a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi sv-no-yi Tsi-sa wi-du-lu-tse-lv-gi v-da-li ga-du-i a-i-sv-gi.

011426.english: And when the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were troubled, saying, It is a spirit; and they cried out for fear.
011426.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎬᏩᎪᎲ ᎥᏓᎵ ᎦᏚᎩ; ᎠᎢᏒᎢ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏖᏔᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎾᏰᎯ ᎯᎠ.  ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎲᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ.
011426.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no gv-wa-go-hv v-da-li ga-du-gi; a-i-sv-i u-na-da-nv-te-ta-nv-gi, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Na-ye-hi hi-a.  A-ni-s-ga-i-hv-no i-yu-s-di u-na-li-s-ta-nv-gi.

011427.english: But straightway Jesus spake unto them, saying, Be of good cheer; it is I; be not afraid.
011427.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏥᏌ ᏚᏁᏤᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎤᎦᎵᏍᏗᏉ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏓᏓ, ᎠᏴᏉᏰᏃ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏥᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ.
011427.phonetic: A-se-no gi-la-quo i-yv-dv Tsi-sa du-ne-tse-lv-gi, hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; U-ga-li-s-di-quo i-tsa-da-nv-da-da, a-yv-quo-ye-no, tle-s-di yi-tsi-s-ga-i-he-s-di.

011428.english: And Peter answered him and said, Lord, if it be thou, bid me come unto thee on the water.
011428.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏂᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏍᎩᏁᏥ ᏫᎬᎷᏤᏗᏱ ᎠᎹᏱ ᎦᏚᎢ.
011428.phonetic: Qui-da-no u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, i-yu-no ni-hi ge-se-s-di, s-gi-ne-tsi wi-gv-lu-tse-di-yi a-ma-yi ga-du-i.

011429.english: And he said, Come. And when Peter was come down out of the ship, he walked on the water, to go to Jesus.
011429.cherokee: ᎡᎮᎾ, ᎤᏛᏅᎩ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏈᏓ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᏣᎢᏒ ᎠᎹᏱ ᎦᏚᎢ ᎠᎢᏒᎩ ᏥᏌ ᏤᏙᎲ ᏩᎦᏛᎩ.
011429.phonetic: E-he-na, u-dv-nv-gi.  Hna-quo-no Qui-da tsi-yu-hi u-tsa-i-sv a-ma-yi ga-du-i a-i-sv-gi Tsi-sa tse-do-hv wa-ga-dv-gi.

011430.english: But when he saw the wind boisterous, he was afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried, saying, Lord, save me.
011430.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏅᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎠᏍᏓᏱᏳ ᎦᏃᎸᎥᏍᎬ ᎤᏍᎦᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎴᏅᎲ ᎦᏃᏴᎬ ᎤᏪᎷᏅᎩ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏍᎩᏍᏕᎸ, ᎤᏛᏅᎩ.
011430.phonetic: A-se-no nv-do-le-ho-sv a-s-da-yi-yu ga-no-lv-v-s-gv u-s-ga-lv-gi, a-le u-le-nv-hv ga-no-yv-gv u-we-lu-nv-gi, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi s-gi-s-de-lv, u-dv-nv-gi.

011431.english: And immediately Jesus stretched forth his hand, and caught him, and said unto him, O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?
011431.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏥᏌ ᏭᏙᏯᏅᎯᏛ ᏭᏂᏴᎲᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎤᏍᏗ ᏦᎯᏳᎯ, ᎦᏙᎯ ᎥᏣᏜᏓᏏᏛᏏ?
011431.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv Tsi-sa wu-do-ya-nv-hi-dv wu-ni-yv-hv-gi, hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; u-s-di tso-hi-yu-hi, ga-do-hi v-tsa-dla-si-dv-si?

011432.english: And when they were come into the ship, the wind ceased.

011433.english: Then they that were in the ship came and worshipped him, saying, Of a truth thou art the Son of God.
011433.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᎾᏣᎢ ᎤᏂᎷᏨ ᎬᏩᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᏂᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ.
011433.phonetic: Hna-quo-no tsi-yu-hi u-na-tsa-i u-ni-lu-tsv gv-wa-da-do-li-s-da-ne-lv-gi, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya ni-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi.

011434.english: And when they were gone over, they came into the land of Gennesaret.

011435.english: And when the men of that place had knowledge of him, they sent out into all that country round about, and brought unto him all that were diseased;
011435.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎬᏬᎵᏨ, ᎬᏩᏚᏫᏛ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏏᏙᎸᎩ, ᏕᎬᏩᏘᏃᎮᎸᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ.
011435.phonetic: Na-hna-no a-ne-hi a-ni-s-ga-ya gv-wo-li-tsv, gv-wa-du-wi-dv du-na-da-nv-si-do-lv-gi, de-gv-wa-ti-no-he-lv-gi ni-ga-dv tsu-ni-tlv-gi.

011436.english: And besought him that they might only touch the hem of his garment: and as many as touched were made perfectly whole.
011436.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩᏃ ᎦᏓᎷᏯᏛᏉ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏄᏩᎥ ᎤᎾᏒᏁᏗᏱ, ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᎾᏒᏂᎸᎯ ᏚᎾᏗᏩᏒᎩ.
011436.phonetic: Gv-wa-ta-yo-se-lv-gi-no ga-da-lu-ya-dv-quo na-s-quo u-nu-wa-v u-na-sv-ne-di-yi, ni-ga-dv-no u-na-sv-ni-lv-hi du-na-di-wa-sv-gi.

011501.english: Then came to Jesus scribes and Pharisees, which were of Jerusalem, saying,
011501.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ;
011501.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-gi di-no-we-li-s-gi a-le A-ni-qua-li-si Tsi-lu-si-li-mi a-ne-hi, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi;

011502.english: Why do thy disciples transgress the tradition of the elders? for they wash not their hands when they eat bread.
011502.cherokee: ᎦᏙᏃ ᎨᏣᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᏂᏲᏍᏗᏍᎪ ᎡᏘ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸᎯ?  ᏝᏰᏃ ᏱᏓᏃᏑᎴᏍᎪ ᏧᏃᏰᏂ ᏨᏗᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏂᏐᎢ.
011502.phonetic: Ga-do-no ge-tsa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi a-ni-yo-s-di-s-go e-ti a-ne-hi u-ni-no-he-lv-hi?  Tla-ye-no yi-da-no-su-le-s-go tsu-no-ye-ni tsv-di-na-li-s-da-yv-ni-so-i.

011503.english: But he answered and said unto them, Why do ye also transgress the commandment of God by your tradition?
011503.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏂᎯ ᎡᏘ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸᎯ ᎢᏨᏗᏍᎪ ᎢᏥᏲᏍᏗᏍᎪ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏁᏨᎯ?
011503.phonetic: A-se-no u-ne-tsv hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; ga-do-no na-s-quo ni-hi e-ti a-ne-hi u-ni-no-he-lv-hi i-tsv-di-s-go i-tsi-yo-s-di-s-go U-ne-la-nv-hi u-ne-tsv-hi?

011504.english: For God commanded, saying, Honour thy father and mother: and, He that curseth father or mother, let him die the death.
011504.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏰᏃ ᎤᏁᏤ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎯᎸᏉᏕᏍᏗ ᏣᏙᏓ ᎠᎴ ᏣᏥ; ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎯᎠ; ᎩᎶ ᏓᏍᎩᏅᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥ ᎠᏎ ᎤᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011504.phonetic: U-ne-la-nv-hi-ye-no u-ne-tse hi-a nu-we-se-i; Hi-lv-quo-de-s-di tsa-do-da a-le tsa-tsi; a-le na-s-quo hi-a; Gi-lo da-s-gi-nv-di-s-ge-s-di u-do-da a-le u-tsi a-se u-yo-hu-hi-s-di ge-se-s-di.

011505.english: But ye say, Whosoever shall say to his father or his mother, It is a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me;
011505.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏂᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏥᏪᏍᎪᎢ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᏎᎮᏍᏗ ᎤᏙᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥ, ᎠᏆᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏅᎯ ᏰᎵ ᎨᏣᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎲ,
011505.phonetic: A-se-no ni-hi hi-a ni-tsi-we-s-go-i; I-yu-no gi-lo hi-a ni-ga-we-se-he-s-di u-do-da, a-le u-tsi, A-qua-li-s-go-lv-ta-nv-hi ye-li ge-tsa-li-s-de-lv-do-di ge-sv-i ni-tsa-dv-ne-hv,

011506.english: And honour not his father or his mother, he shall be free. Thus have ye made the commandment of God of none effect by your tradition.
011506.cherokee: ᏂᎦᎸᏉᏗᏍᎬᎾᏃ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏙᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥ, [ᎥᏝ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨᎯ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ,] ᎢᏣᏗᏍᎪᎢ.  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎡᏘ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸᎯ ᎢᏨᏔᏅ ᎠᏎᏉ ᏂᏨᏁᎸ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏁᏨᎯ.
011506.phonetic: Ni-ga-lv-quo-di-s-gv-na-no i-ge-se-s-di u-do-da, a-le u-tsi, [v-tla u-s-ga-nv-tsv-hi yi-ge-se-s-di,] i-tsa-di-s-go-i.  Na-s-gi hi-a e-ti a-ne-hi u-ni-no-he-lv-hi i-tsv-ta-nv a-se-quo ni-tsv-ne-lv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-ne-tsv-hi.

011507.english: Ye hypocrites, well did Esaias prophesy of you, saying,
011507.cherokee: ᎢᏣᏠᎾᏍᏗ, ᏰᎵᏉ ᎢᏌᏯ ᏧᏙᎴᎰᏎ ᏥᏥᏛᎨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏎᎢ;
011507.phonetic: I-tsa-tlo-na-s-di, ye-li-quo I-sa-ya tsu-do-le-ho-se tsi-tsi-dv-ge-i, hi-a tsi-nu-we-se-i;

011508.english: This people draweth nigh unto me with their mouth, and honoureth me with their lips; but their heart is far from me.
011508.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏴᏫ ᎾᎥ ᎬᎩᎷᏤᎭ ᎠᏂᎰᎵ ᎠᏅᏗᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᎭᏁᎦᎸ ᏓᏅᏗ ᎬᎩᎸᏉᏗᎭ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏂᎾᏫ ᎢᏅᎯᏳ ᏄᏅᏅ.
011508.phonetic: Hi-a yv-wi na-v gv-gi-lu-tse-ha a-ni-ho-li a-nv-di-ha, a-le du-ni-ha-ne-ga-lv da-nv-di gv-gi-lv-quo-di-ha, a-se-no u-ni-na-wi i-nv-hi-yu nu-nv-nv.

011509.english: But in vain they do worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.
011509.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎭ ᎬᏆᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎭ, ᏥᏓᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎦ ᏓᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬ ᏴᏫᏉ ᎤᏂᏁᏨᎯ.
011509.phonetic: A-se-quo-s-gi-ni na-na-dv-ne-ha gv-qua-da-do-li-s-da-ne-ha, tsi-da-na-de-yo-hv-s-ga da-na-de-yo-hv-s-gv yv-wi-quo u-ni-ne-tsv-hi.

011510.english: And he called the multitude, and said unto them, Hear, and understand:
011510.cherokee: ᏫᏚᏯᎲᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏣᏛᎬᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏦᎵᎩ.
011510.phonetic: Wi-du-ya-hv-no u-ni-tsa-ti hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; I-tsa-dv-gv-ga a-le i-tso-li-gi.

011511.english: Not that which goeth into the mouth defileth a man; but that which cometh out of the mouth, this defileth a man.
011511.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏍᎩᏂ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᎰᎵ ᏭᏴᎸᎯ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏱᏄᏩᏁᎰ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᎰᎵᏍᎩᏂ ᏅᏓᏳᏄᎪᏨᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏄᏩᏁᎰ ᏴᏫ.
011511.phonetic: V-tla-s-gi-ni na-s-gi na a-ho-li wu-yv-lv-hi ga-da-ha yi-nu-wa-ne-ho yv-wi, a-ho-li-s-gi-ni nv-da-yu-nu-go-tsv-hi na-s-gi ga-da-ha nu-wa-ne-ho yv-wi.

011512.english: Then came his disciples, and said unto him, Knowest thou that the Pharisees were offended, after they heard this saying?
011512.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᏂᎷᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏎᎸᎩ; ᎯᎦᏔᎭᏍᎪ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᏂᎿᎭᎸᏨᎢ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏣᏪᏒᎢ.
011512.phonetic: Hna-quo-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi u-ni-lu-tsv hi-a ni-gv-wa-se-lv-gi; Hi-ga-ta-ha-s-go A-ni-qua-li-si u-ni-hna-lv-tsv-i u-na-dv-ga-nv na-s-gi hi-a ni-tsa-we-sv-i.

011513.english: But he answered and said, Every plant, which my heavenly Father hath not planted, shall be rooted up.
011513.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏂᎦᎥ ᏚᏰᎬ ᎡᏙᏓ ᏧᏫᏒᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏗᏰᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011513.phonetic: A-se-no u-ne-tsv-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; ni-ga-v du-ye-gv E-do-da tsu-wi-sv-hi ni-ge-sv-na di-ye-hi-s-di ge-se-s-di.

011514.english: Let them alone: they be blind leaders of the blind. And if the blind lead the blind, both shall fall into the ditch.
011514.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏁᎳᎩ, ᏗᏂᎨᏫᏉ ᏗᏂᎨᏫ ᏗᎾᏘᏂᏙᎯ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏗᎨᏫ ᏯᏘᏂ ᏗᎨᏫ ᎢᏧᎳ ᎠᏔᎴᏒ ᏧᏂᎸᎢᏍᏗ.
011514.phonetic: U-na-ne-la-gi, di-ni-ge-wi-quo di-ni-ge-wi di-na-ti-ni-do-hi; i-yu-no di-ge-wi ya-ti-ni di-ge-wi i-tsu-la a-ta-le-sv tsu-ni-lv-i-s-di.

011515.english: Then answered Peter and said unto him, Declare unto us this parable.
011515.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏗᏍᎩᏲᏏᏌᏏ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏛᎢ.
011515.phonetic: Qui-da-no u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; di-s-gi-yo-si-sa-si hi-a na-s-gi da-tli-lo-s-dv-i.

011516.english: And Jesus said, Are ye also yet without understanding?
011516.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎠᏏᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏂᎯ ᏂᏦᎵᎬᎾᏉ ᎢᎩ?
011516.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; A-si-s-go na-s-quo ni-hi ni-tso-li-gv-na-quo i-gi?

011517.english: Do not ye yet understand, that whatsoever entereth in at the mouth goeth into the belly, and is cast out into the draught?
011517.cherokee: ᏝᏍᎪ ᎠᏏ ᏱᏦᎵᎦ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎥᎰᎵ ᎤᏴᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎣᏍᏉᎵᏱ ᏫᎦᎷᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏫᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᏉ?
011517.phonetic: Tla-s-go a-si yi-tso-li-ga na-s-gi ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di v-ho-li u-yv-s-ta-nv-hi ge-sv o-s-quo-li-yi wi-ga-lu-gv-i, na-s-gi-no wi-ga-lo-hi-s-di-s-gv-quo?

011518.english: But those things which proceed out of the mouth come forth from the heart; and they defile the man.
011518.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏂ ᎥᎰᎵ ᏅᏓᏳᏄᎪᏨᎯ ᎣᎾᏫᏱ ᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎪᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏄᏩᏁᎰ ᏴᏫ.
011518.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ni v-ho-li nv-da-yu-nu-go-tsv-hi o-na-wi-yi di-da-le-hv-s-go-i, na-s-gi-no ga-da-ha nu-wa-ne-ho yv-wi.

011519.english: For out of the heart proceed evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false witness, blasphemies:
011519.cherokee: ᎣᎾᏫᏱᏰᏃ ᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎪ ᎤᏐᏅ ᎠᏓᏅᏖᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏲᏁᏗᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᏗᏂᏏᏗᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏃᏍᎩᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᎪᏗᏱ ᏓᎾᏓᏱᎵᏙᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏐᏢᎢᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ.
011519.phonetic: O-na-wi-yi-ye-no di-da-le-hv-s-go u-so-nv a-da-nv-te-di ge-sv-i, a-le a-da-hi-s-di-yi ge-sv-i, a-le a-da-yo-ne-di-yi ge-sv-i, a-le u-de-li-dv di-ni-si-di-yi ge-sv-i, a-le ga-no-s-gi-s-di-yi ge-sv-i, a-le a-tsi-go-di-yi da-na-da-yi-li-do-hv-i, a-le a-so-tlv-i-s-do-di-yi ge-sv-i.

011520.english: These are the things which defile a man: but to eat with unwashen hands defileth not a man.
011520.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᎯᎠ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎢᏳᏩᏁᎯ ᏴᏫ; ᏂᏓᏑᎴᎲᎾᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ ᎥᏝ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏱᏄᏩᏁᎰ ᏴᏫ.
011520.phonetic: Na-s-gi-s-gi-ni hi-a ga-da-ha i-yu-wa-ne-hi yv-wi; ni-da-su-le-hv-na-s-gi-ni a-li-s-da-yv-di-yi v-tla ga-da-ha yi-nu-wa-ne-ho yv-wi.

011521.english: Then Jesus went thence, and departed into the coasts of Tyre and Sidon.
011521.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏂᎩᏒ ᏔᏲᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏙᏂᏱ ᏭᎶᏒᎩ.
011521.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no na-hna u-ni-gi-sv Ta-yo-i a-le Sa-do-ni-yi wu-lo-sv-gi.

011522.english: And, behold, a woman of Canaan came out of the same coasts, and cried unto him, saying, Have mercy on me, O Lord, thou Son of David; my daughter is grievously vexed with a devil.
011522.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎨᎾᏂ ᎡᎯ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎾᎿᎭᎾᎥ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᏒᎯ ᎤᏪᎷᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏍᎩᏙᎵᎩ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏕᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᎠᏇᏥ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎤᏣᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᏕᏯᏙᏗᎭ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ.
011522.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo-no Ge-na-ni e-hi a-ge-yv na-hna na-v nv-da-yu-lo-sv-hi u-we-lu-nv-gi, hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; S-gi-do-li-gi, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi De-wi u-we-tsi, a-que-tsi a-ge-yv u-tsa-ta-nv-hi u-de-ya-do-di-ha a-s-gi-na.

011523.english: But he answered her not a word. And his disciples came and besought him, saying, Send her away; for she crieth after us.
011523.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏄᏁᏤᎸᎾᏉ ᎥᎨᏒᎩ.  ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᎩ, ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏫᎡᎾ, ᎢᎩᏍᎪᏂᎭᏰᏃ.
011523.phonetic: A-se-no nu-ne-tse-lv-na-quo v-ge-sv-gi.  Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-gi, gv-wa-ta-yo-se-lv-gi, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Wi-e-na, i-gi-s-go-ni-ha-ye-no.

011524.english: But he answered and said, I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
011524.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏗᏥᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᏴᎩᏁᏤᎸ, ᎠᏫ ᎤᏂᏃᏕᎾ ᎤᎾᎴᎾᎸᎯ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎢᏏᎵ ᏏᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᎬᏒᎢ.
011524.phonetic: U-ne-tsv-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; v-tla gi-lo di-tsi-wa-dv-di-yi yv-gi-ne-tse-lv, a-wi u-ni-no-de-na u-na-le-na-lv-hi u-nv-sv I-si-li si-da-ne-lv-hi gv-sv-i.

011525.english: Then came she and worshipped him, saying, Lord, help me.
011525.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎤᎷᏨ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏍᎩᏍᏕᎸ.
011525.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-ge-yv u-lu-tsv u-da-do-li-s-da-ne-lv-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, s-gi-s-de-lv.

011526.english: But he answered and said, It is not meet to take the children's bread, and to cast it to dogs.
011526.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᏱᏚᏳᎪᏗ ᏗᎩᎡᏗᏱ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᎦᏚ, ᎩᎵᏃ ᏫᏓᏗᏁᏗᏱ.
011526.phonetic: U-ne-tsv-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; v-tla yi-du-yu-go-di di-gi-e-di-yi di-ni-yo-li u-na-tse-li ga-du, gi-li-no wi-da-di-ne-di-yi.

011527.english: And she said, Truth, Lord: yet the dogs eat of the crumbs which fall from their masters' table.
011527.cherokee: ᎠᎨᏴᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎩᎵ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏍᎪ ᎤᏅᎪᎣᏒᎯ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᎾᏝᎢ ᏚᏂᏍᎩᎸᎢ.
011527.phonetic: A-ge-yv-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; U-do-hi-yu-hi, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, a-se-no gi-li a-na-li-s-da-yv-di-s-go u-nv-go-o-sv-hi di-gv-wa-ni-na-tla-i du-ni-s-gi-lv-i.

011528.english: Then Jesus answered and said unto her, O woman, great is thy faith: be it unto thee even as thou wilt. And her daughter was made whole from that very hour.
011528.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎯᎨᏴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏦᎯᏳᎭ; ᏄᏍᏛ ᏣᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᏫᏂᏣᎵᏍᏓᏏ.  ᎾᎯᏳᏉᏃ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᏗᏩᏎᎢ.
011528.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Hi-ge-yv u-tsa-ti tso-hi-yu-ha; nu-s-dv tsa-du-li-s-gv wi-ni-tsa-li-s-da-si.  Na-hi-yu-quo-no u-we-tsi u-di-wa-se-i.

011529.english: And Jesus departed from thence, and came nigh unto the sea of Galilee; and went up into a mountain, and sat down there.
011529.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏂᎩᏒ ᎨᎵᎵ ᎥᏓᎷᎶᏗ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ, ᎤᎿᎭᎷᏒᏃ ᎤᏌᎯᎸ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏪᏅᎩ.
011529.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no na-hna u-ni-gi-sv Ge-li-li v-da-lu-lo-di u-lu-tsv-gi, u-hna-lu-sv-no u-sa-hi-lv na-hna u-we-nv-gi.

011530.english: And great multitudes came unto him, having with them those that were lame, blind, dumb, maimed, and many others, and cast them down at Jesus' feet; and he healed them:
011530.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᎩ, ᏓᎾᏘᏁᎲ ᏗᏂᏲᎤᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎨᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏅᎨᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᏛ ᏗᏂᏰᎸ ᏧᏂᏲᎱᏎᎸᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏌ ᏚᎳᏍᎬ ᎾᎥ ᏚᏂᏅᏅᎩ, ᏚᏅᏩᏅᎩᏃ.
011530.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no yv-wi gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-gi, da-na-ti-ne-hv di-ni-yo-u-li, a-le di-ni-ge-wi, a-le tsu-nv-ge-wi, a-le go-hu-s-di u-s-dv di-ni-ye-lv tsu-ni-yo-hu-se-lv-hi, a-le u-ni-tsa-ti nv-wa-na-da-le-i, a-le Tsi-sa du-la-s-gv na-v du-ni-nv-nv-gi, du-nv-wa-nv-gi-no.

011531.english: Insomuch that the multitude wondered, when they saw the dumb to speak, the maimed to be whole, the lame to walk, and the blind to see: and they glorified the God of Israel.
011531.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏒᎩ, ᏚᏂᎪᎲ ᏧᏅᎨᏫ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᏛ ᏗᏂᏰᎸ ᏧᏂᏲᎱᏎᎸᎯ ᎤᏂᏃᏍᏛ ᏄᎾᎵᏍᏔᏅᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏗᏲᎤᎵ, ᎬᏩᏁᏓᏍᏗ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎨᏫ ᎬᏩᏂᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎢ; ᎤᏂᎸᏉᏔᏅᎩᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᏏᎵ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ.
011531.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-sv-gi, du-ni-go-hv tsu-nv-ge-wi a-ni-wo-ni-s-go-i, a-le a-hu-s-di u-s-dv di-ni-ye-lv tsu-ni-yo-hu-se-lv-hi u-ni-no-s-dv nu-na-li-s-ta-nv-i, a-le di-di-yo-u-li, gv-wa-ne-da-s-di nu-li-s-ta-nv-i a-le di-ni-ge-wi gv-wa-ni-go-wa-dv-di nu-li-s-ta-nv-i; u-ni-lv-quo-ta-nv-gi-no U-ne-la-nv-hi I-si-li u-na-tse-li-ga.

011532.english: Then Jesus called his disciples unto him, and said, I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days, and have nothing to eat: and I will not send them away fasting, lest they faint in the way.
011532.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎲ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᏥᏯᏙᎵᎦ ᎯᎠ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏦᎢᏰᏃ ᎾᏙᏓᏆ ᎠᏂ ᎨᏙᎲ ᎠᏁᏙᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏂᏰᎭ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ.  ᎠᎴ ᎢᏝ ᏯᏆᏚᎵᎭ ᏙᎤᏁᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏲᏏᏍᎩ, ᏩᎾᏰᏃ ᏱᏓᏂᏩᏂᎦᎶᎩ ᏩᎾᎢᏒᎢ.
011532.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no wi-du-ya-nv-hv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; ga-tsi-ya-do-li-ga hi-a u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv-i, tso-i-ye-no na-do-da-qua a-ni ge-do-hv a-ne-do-ha, a-le v-tla yi-ni-ye-ha u-na-li-s-da-yv-di.  A-le i-tla ya-qua-du-li-ha do-u-ne-nv-s-di-yi di-gv-wa-ni-yo-si-s-gi, wa-na-ye-no yi-da-ni-wa-ni-ga-lo-gi wa-na-i-sv-i.

011533.english: And his disciples say unto him, Whence should we have so much bread in the wilderness, as to fill so great a multitude?
011533.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏅᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎭᏢᏃ ᏲᏥᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᎢᎾᎨᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏂᏣᏘ ᏰᎵ ᏧᏃᎸᎯᏍᏗ?
011533.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no hi-a nv-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; Ha-tlv-no yo-tsi-gi na-s-gi i-ga-i a-li-s-da-yv-di i-na-ge-i, na-s-gi i-yu-ni-tsa-ti ye-li tsu-no-lv-hi-s-di?

011534.english: And Jesus saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven, and a few little fishes.
011534.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᎳᎪ ᎦᏚ ᏕᏥᏰᎭ?  ᎦᎵᏉᎩ, ᎢᎸᏍᎩᏃ ᏧᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏣᏗ, ᎤᎾᏛᏅᎩ.
011534.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; I-la-go ga-du de-tsi-ye-ha?  Ga-li-quo-gi, i-lv-s-gi-no tsu-na-s-di a-tsa-di, u-na-dv-nv-gi.

011535.english: And he commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground.

011536.english: And he took the seven loaves and the fishes, and gave thanks, and brake them, and gave to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.
011536.cherokee: ᎦᎵᏉᎩᏃ ᎦᏚ ᏚᎩᏒᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏁᏒ ᎠᏣᏗ, ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏨᏃ, ᏚᎬᎭᎷᏴᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏫᏚᏁᎸᎩ, ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏫᏚᏂᏁᎸᎩ.
011536.phonetic: Ga-li-quo-gi-no ga-du du-gi-sv-gi a-le du-ne-sv a-tsa-di, u-li-he-li-tsv-no, du-gv-ha-lu-yv-gi, a-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi wi-du-ne-lv-gi, gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no u-ni-tsa-ti wi-du-ni-ne-lv-gi.

011537.english: And they did all eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets full.
011537.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏃᎸᏒᎩ.  ᎠᎴ ᎤᏄᏖᏒᎩ ᎤᎵᎬᎭᎷᏴᎯ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᎢᏯᎧᎵᎢ ᏔᎷᏣ.
011537.phonetic: A-le ni-ga-dv u-na-li-s-da-yv-nv-gi, a-le du-no-lv-sv-gi.  A-le u-nu-te-sv-gi u-li-gv-ha-lu-yv-hi ga-li-quo-gi i-ya-ka-li-i ta-lu-tsa.

011538.english: And they that did eat were four thousand men, beside women and children.
011538.cherokee: ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎯᏃ ᏅᎩᎭ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᎾᏂᎥᎩ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ, ᏂᏓᏁᏢᏛᎾ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ.
011538.phonetic: U-na-li-s-da-yv-hi-no nv-gi-ha i-ya-ga-yv-li na-ni-v-gi a-ni-s-ga-ya, ni-da-ne-tlv-dv-na a-ni-ge-yv a-le di-ni-yo-li.

011539.english: And he sent away the multitude, and took ship, and came into the coasts of Magdala,
011539.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏚᏰᎵᎯᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏳᎯ ᏭᏣᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎹᎩᏕᎵ ᏭᎾᏄᎪᏨᎩ.
011539.phonetic: A-le u-ni-tsa-ti du-ye-li-hi-s-ta-nv-gi, a-le tsi-yu-hi wu-tsa-nv-gi, a-le ma-gi-de-li wu-na-nu-go-tsv-gi.

011601.english: The Pharisees also with the Sadducees came, and tempting desired him that he would shew them a sign from heaven.
011601.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏌᏚᏏ ᎤᏂᎷᏨᎩ, ᎬᏩᎪᎵᏰᏍᎬᏃ ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩ ᏧᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᏗᏱ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᏒᎯ.
011601.phonetic: A-ni-qua-li-si-no a-le A-ni-sa-du-si u-ni-lu-tsv-gi, gv-wa-go-li-ye-s-gv-no gv-wa-ta-yo-se-lv-gi tsu-na-nu-go-wi-se-di-yi u-ye-lv-dv ga-lv-la-di nv-da-yu-lo-sv-hi.

011602.english: He answered and said unto them, When it is evening, ye say, It will be fair weather: for the sky is red.
011602.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎤᏒ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏥᏪᏍᎪᎢ; ᎤᎸᎦᎵᏱᏌᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎦᎸᎶᏰᏃ ᎩᎦᎨᎢᏳ ᎢᎩ.
011602.phonetic: U-ne-tsv-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; U-sv-ni-ga-li-s-da hi-a ni-tsi-we-s-go-i; u-lv-ga-li-yi-sa-dv ge-se-s-di, ga-lv-lo-ye-no gi-ga-ge-i-yu i-gi.

011603.english: And in the morning, It will be foul weather to day: for the sky is red and lowring. O ye hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky; but can ye not discern the signs of the times?
011603.cherokee: ᏑᎾᎴᏃ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ, ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᏥᎩ ᏛᎦᎿᎭᏂ, ᎦᎸᎶᏰᏃ ᎩᎦᎨᎢᏳ ᎢᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎦᎶᎩᎵᏰᎭ, ᎢᏣᏗᏍᎪᎢ.  ᎢᏣᏠᎾᏍᏗ, ᏰᎵᏉ ᎢᏣᏙᎴᎰᏍᎪ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎦᎸᎶᎢ, ᏝᏍᎪᏃ ᏰᎵ ᎨᏣᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎢᏳ ᎵᏍᏔᏂᏓᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ?
011603.phonetic: Su-na-le-no tsi-ge-so-i, Go-hi i-ga tsi-gi dv-ga-hna-ni, ga-lv-lo-ye-no gi-ga-ge-i-yu i-gi, a-le i-ga-lo-gi-li-ye-ha, i-tsa-di-s-go-i.  I-tsa-tlo-na-s-di, ye-li-quo i-tsa-do-le-ho-s-go nu-s-dv ga-lv-lo-i, tla-s-go-no ye-li ge-tsa-do-le-ho-hi-s-di yi-gi u-ye-lv-dv i-yu li-s-ta-ni-da-s-di ge-sv-i?

011604.english: A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas. And he left them, and departed.
011604.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏲ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏁᎫᏥᏛ ᏣᏁᎭ ᎤᏂᏲᎰ ᎤᏰᎸᏛᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏴᎦᎨᎦᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏏ ᎤᏰᎸᏛᎢ, ᏦᎾ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎤᏩᏒ.  ᏚᏓᏅᎡᎸᏃ ᎤᏂᎩᏒᎩ.
011604.phonetic: U-ni-yo a-le u-ni-ne-gu-tsi-dv tsa-ne-ha u-ni-yo-ho u-ye-lv-dv-i, a-se-no v-tla yv-ga-ge-ga-li-s-go-lv-da-si u-ye-lv-dv-i, Tso-na a-do-le-ho-s-gi u-tse-li u-ye-lv-dv u-wa-sv.  Du-da-nv-e-lv-no u-ni-gi-sv-gi.

011605.english: And when his disciples were come to the other side, they had forgotten to take bread.

011606.english: Then Jesus said unto them, Take heed and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.
011606.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏤᏯᏔᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᎪᏙᏗ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏌᏚᏏ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ.
011606.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; I-tse-ya-ta-he-s-di a-go-do-di A-ni-qua-li-si a-le A-ni-sa-du-si u-na-tse-li-ga.

011607.english: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, It is because we have taken no bread.
011607.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸᎩᏃ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏅᏒ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏂᎦᏕᏅᎾ ᏥᎩ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ.
011607.phonetic: U-ni-no-he-lv-gi-no ge-sv u-nv-sv, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Ni-ga-de-nv-na tsi-gi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha.

011608.english: Which when Jesus perceived, he said unto them, O ye of little faith, why reason ye among yourselves, because ye have brought no bread?
011608.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎤᏍᏗ ᎢᏦᎯᏳᎯ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᏨᏒ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏥᏃᎮᎭ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᏂᏥᏰᎲᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ?
011608.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no na-s-gi u-do-le-ho-sv hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; U-s-di i-tso-hi-yu-hi, ga-do-no i-tsv-sv ge-sv i-tsi-no-he-ha a-li-s-da-yv-di ni-tsi-ye-hv-na ge-sv-i?

011609.english: Do ye not yet understand, neither remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?
011609.cherokee: ᏝᏍᎪ ᎠᏏ ᏱᏦᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏝᏍᎪ ᏱᏣᏅᏔ ᎯᏍᎩ ᎦᏚ ᎠᎴ ᎯᏍᎩ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᏥᎾᏂᎥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏯᎧᎵᎢ ᏔᎷᏣ ᎢᏧᏖᏒᎢ?
011609.phonetic: Tla-s-go a-si yi-tso-li-ga, a-le tla-s-go yi-tsa-nv-ta hi-s-gi ga-du a-le hi-s-gi i-ya-ga-yv-li tsi-na-ni-v-gi, a-le i-ya-ka-li-i ta-lu-tsa i-tsu-te-sv-i?

011610.english: Neither the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?
011610.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᎦᏚ ᏅᎩᏃ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᏥᎾᏂᎥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏯᎧᎵᎢ ᏔᎳᏣ ᎢᏧᏖᏒᎢ?
011610.phonetic: A-le ga-li-quo-gi ga-du nv-gi-no i-ya-ga-yv-li tsi-na-ni-v-gi, a-le i-ya-ka-li-i ta-la-tsa i-tsu-te-sv-i?

011611.english: How is it that ye do not understand that I spake it not to you concerning bread, that ye should beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees?
011611.cherokee: ᎦᏙ ᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏂᏦᎵᎬᎾ ᏥᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᏨᏪᏏ ᏝᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᏚ ᏱᏥᏛᎦ, ᎢᏤᏯᏔᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᎪᏙᏗ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏌᏚᏏ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ, ᏥᏨᏲᏏ?
011611.phonetic: Ga-do di-ga-li-s-do-di ni-tso-li-gv-na tsi-gi, na-s-gi hi-a tsi-ni-tsv-we-si tla-s-gi-ni ga-du yi-tsi-dv-ga, i-tse-ya-ta-he-s-di a-go-do-di A-ni-qua-li-si A-le A-ni-sa-du-si u-na-tse-li-ga, tsi-tsv-yo-si?

011612.english: Then understood they how that he bade them not beware of the leaven of bread, but of the doctrine of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.
011612.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏃᎵᏨᎩ ᏄᏂᏁᏤᎲᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᏯᏙᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎦᏚ ᎠᎪᏙᏗ, ᏓᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏌᏚᏏ.
011612.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-no-li-tsv-gi nu-ni-ne-tse-hv-na ge-sv u-ne-ya-do-i-s-di-yi ga-du a-go-do-di, da-na-de-yo-hv-s-gv-s-gi-ni A-ni-qua-li-si a-le A-ni-sa-du-si.

011613.english: When Jesus came into the coasts of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, Whom do men say that I the Son of man am?
011613.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᎷᏨ ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᏇᎵᎩᏱ, ᏚᏛᏛᏅᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎠᏴ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏥᎩ ᎦᎪ ᎬᏉᏎᎰ ᏴᏫ?
011613.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-lu-tsv Si-sa-li-yi Que-li-gi-yi, du-dv-dv-nv-gi gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; A-yv yv-wi U-we-tsi tsi-gi ga-go gv-quo-se-ho yv-wi?

011614.english: And they said, Some say that thou art John the Baptist: some, Elias; and others, Jeremias, or one of the prophets.
011614.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎢᎦᏛ ᏣᏂ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ [ᎨᏦᏎᎰᎢ,] ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎢᎳᏯ, ᎠᏂᏐᎢᏃ ᏤᎵᎹᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ.
011614.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-ni-we-sv-gi; I-ga-dv Tsa-ni di-da-wo-s-gi [ge-tso-se-ho-i,] i-ga-dv-no I-la-ya, a-ni-so-i-no Tse-li-ma-ya a-le gi-lo i-yu-s-di a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi ge-sv-i.

011615.english: He saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am?

011616.english: And Simon Peter answered and said, Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.
011616.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏂᎯ, ᎬᏂᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ.
011616.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni-no Qui-da u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Ga-lo-ne-dv ni-hi, gv-ni-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi.

011617.english: And Jesus answered and said unto him, Blessed art thou, Simon Barjona: for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven.
011617.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏂᎯ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏦᎾ ᎤᏪᏥ; ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎤᏇᏓᎵ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎬ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᏣᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᎸ, ᎡᏙᏓᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ.
011617.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; O-si-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di ni-hi Sa-wa-ni Tso-na u-we-tsi; v-tla-ye-no u-que-da-li a-le gi-gv na-s-gi yi-tsa-na-nu-go-wi-se-lv, E-do-da-s-gi-ni ga-lv-la-di e-hi.

011618.english: And I say also unto thee, That thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church; and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it.
011618.cherokee: ᎠᏴᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏪᏎᎭ; ᏈᏓ ᏂᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂ ᎯᎠ ᏅᏲᎯ ᏓᎦᏁᏍᎨᎯ ᎠᏆᏤᎵ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏨᏍᎩᏃᎢ ᏓᏍᏚᎲᎢ ᎡᏍᎦ ᎢᏳᏅᏁᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011618.phonetic: A-yv-no hi-a ni-gv-we-se-ha; Qui-da ni-hi a-le na-s-gi a-ni hi-a nv-yo-hi da-ga-ne-s-ge-hi a-qua-tse-li tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv-i, a-le tsv-s-gi-no-i da-s-du-hv-i e-s-ga i-yu-nv-ne-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-se-s-di.

011619.english: And I will give unto thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.
011619.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏗᏍᏚᎢᏍᏗ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏧᏤᎵ ᏙᏓᎬᏕᎵ, ᏂᎦᎥᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎭᎸᎢᎮᏍᏗ ᎡᎶᎯ, ᎦᎸᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ, ᏂᎦᎥᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎭᎸᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎦᎸᎩᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ.
011619.phonetic: A-le di-s-du-i-s-di ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv tsu-tse-li do-da-gv-de-li, ni-ga-v-no go-hu-s-di ha-lv-i-he-s-di e-lo-hi, ga-lv-s-di ge-se-s-di ga-lv-la-di, ni-ga-v-no go-hu-s-di ha-lv-gi-s-ge-s-di e-lo-hi ga-lv-gi-s-di ge-se-s-di ga-lv-la-di.

011620.english: Then charged he his disciples that they should tell no man that he was Jesus the Christ.
011620.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏚᏅᏍᏓᏕᎸᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏂᏃᏁᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.
011620.phonetic: Hna-quo-no du-nv-s-da-de-lv-gi gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi gi-lo u-ni-no-ne-di-yi na-s-gi u-wa-sv Ga-lo-ne-dv ge-sv-i.

011621.english: From that time forth began Jesus to shew unto his disciples, how that he must go unto Jerusalem, and suffer many things of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and be raised again the third day.
011621.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎴᏅᎲᎩ ᏚᏃᏁᎸᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᏎ ᎤᏪᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏒ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏧᎩᎵᏲᏤᏗᏱ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏦᎢᏁ ᎢᎦ ᏧᎴᏗᏱ.
011621.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa u-le-nv-hv-gi du-no-ne-lv-gi gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi a-se u-we-nv-s-di-yi ge-sv Tsi-lu-si-li-mi, A-le tsu-da-le-nv-dv tsu-gi-li-yo-tse-di-yi di-ni-la-wi-gi a-le nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv-a-ne-lo-hi a-le di-no-we-li-s-gi; a-le a-tsi-i-s-di-yi, a-le tso-i-ne i-ga tsu-le-di-yi.

011622.english: Then Peter took him, and began to rebuke him, saying, Be it far from thee, Lord: this shall not be unto thee.
011622.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏭᏘᏅᏍᏔᏅ ᎤᎴᏅᎲᎩ ᎤᏓᏅᏍᏓᏕᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏭᏅᏍᏙᎯ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎥᏝ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᏅᎨᏣᎵᏍᏓᏏ.
011622.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Qui-da i-yv-dv wu-ti-nv-s-ta-nv u-le-nv-hv-gi u-da-nv-s-da-de-lv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi, U-ne-la-nv-hi wu-nv-s-do-hi, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, v-tla na-s-gi yi-nv-ge-tsa-li-s-da-si.

011623.english: But he turned, and said unto Peter, Get thee behind me, Satan: thou art an offence unto me: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.
011623.cherokee: ᎤᎦᏔᎲᏒᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏥᏪᏎᎸ ᏈᏓ; ᎠᏆᏐᎭᏛ ᎭᎴᎲᎦ, ᏎᏓᏂ, ᏗᏍᏉᏕᏍᏗᏍᎩᏉ ᏂᎯ, ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᏯᏓᏅᏕᎭ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᏴᏫᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᎭᏓᏅᏕᎭ.
011623.phonetic: U-ga-ta-hv-sv-no hi-a na-tsi-we-se-lv Qui-da; A-qua-so-ha-dv ha-le-hv-ga, Se-da-ni, di-s-quo-de-s-di-s-gi-quo ni-hi, v-tla-ye-no ya-da-nv-de-ha U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga, yv-wi-quo-s-gi-ni u-na-tse-li ha-da-nv-de-ha.

011624.english: Then said Jesus unto his disciples, If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
011624.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎩᏍᏚᏩᏛᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᏓᏓᏱᎮᏍᏗ ᎤᏩᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏓᎿᎭᏩᏛ ᎠᏱᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎩᏍᏓᏩᏕᎨᏍᏗ.
011624.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi; I-yu-no gi-lo u-du-li-s-ge-s-di a-gi-s-du-wa-dv-s-di-yi, a-da-da-yi-he-s-di u-wa-sv, a-le tsu-da-hna-wa-dv a-yi-s-ge-s-di, a-le a-gi-s-da-wa-de-ge-s-di.

011625.english: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it.
011625.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᏕᎸᏗᏱ ᎬᏅᎢ, ᎤᏲᎱᏎᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏃ ᎤᏲᎱᏎᎮᏍᏗ ᎬᏅᎢ, ᎠᏴ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎤᏩᏛᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
011625.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no u-du-li-s-ge-s-di u-s-de-lv-di-yi gv-nv-i, u-yo-hu-se-di ge-se-s-di; gi-lo-no u-yo-hu-se-he-s-di gv-nv-i, a-yv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di, u-wa-dv-di ge-se-s-di.

011626.english: For what is a man profited, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul? or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?
011626.cherokee: ᎦᏙ ᏴᏫ ᏳᏁᏉᏥ ᎢᏳᏃ ᎡᎳᏂᎬ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏱᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ, ᎤᏓᏅᏙᏃ ᏳᏲᎱᏎᎸ; ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙ ᏯᎲᎦ ᏴᏫ ᏱᎦᏁᏟᏴᏍᏓ ᎤᏓᏅᏙ.
011626.phonetic: Ga-do yv-wi yu-ne-quo-tsi i-yu-no e-la-ni-gv u-tse-li yi-nu-li-s-ta-nv, u-da-nv-do-no yu-yo-hu-se-lv; a-le ga-do ya-hv-ga yv-wi yi-ga-ne-tli-yv-s-da u-da-nv-do.

011627.english: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of his Father with his angels; and then he shall reward every man according to his works.
011627.cherokee: ᏴᏫᏰᏃ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏓᎦᎷᏥ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏄᏬᏍᏕᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏓᏘᏁᎮᏍᏗ; ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏙᏛᎫᏴᎡᎵ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏴᏫ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ.
011627.phonetic: Yv-wi-ye-no U-we-tsi da-ga-lu-tsi U-do-da u-tse-li ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv u-nu-wo-s-de-s-di, a-le di-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi da-ti-ne-he-s-di; hna-quo-no do-dv-gu-yv-e-li na-ni-v yv-wi nu-s-dv du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-i.

011628.english: Verily I say unto you, There be some standing here, which shall not taste of death, till they see the Son of man coming in his kingdom.
011628.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎩᎶ ᎠᏂ ᏓᏂᏙᎦ ᎥᏝ ᏴᏛᎾᏙᎴᎰᏏ ᎠᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎬᏂ ᎠᏂᎪᎲᎭ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᏥᎸᎭ.
011628.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha, Gi-lo a-ni da-ni-do-ga v-tla yv-dv-na-do-le-ho-si a-yo-hu-hi-s-di ge-sv-i, gv-ni a-ni-go-hv-ha yv-wi U-we-tsi na-hna u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv ga-na-nu-go-tsi-lv-ha.

011701.english: And after six days Jesus taketh Peter, James, and John his brother, and bringeth them up into an high mountain apart,
011701.cherokee: ᏑᏓᎵᏃ ᏫᏄᏒᎸ ᏥᏌ ᏚᏘᏅᏒᎩ ᏈᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᎻ ᎠᎴ ᏣᏂ ᏥᎻ ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎿᎭᎷᏔᏅᎩ ᎢᏅ ᎢᎦᏘ ᎣᏓᎸ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎨᏒᎢ,
011701.phonetic: Su-da-li-no wi-nu-sv-lv Tsi-sa du-ti-nv-sv-gi Qui-da, a-le Tsi-mi a-le Tsa-ni Tsi-mi di-na-da-nv-tli, a-le du-hna-lu-ta-nv-gi i-nv i-ga-ti o-da-lv u-nv-sv ge-sv-i,

011702.english: And was transfigured before them: and his face did shine as the sun, and his raiment was white as the light.
011702.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏁᏟᏴᏎ ᏓᏂᎧᏅᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎧᏛ ᏗᎬᏩᎸᏌᏓ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ ᏅᏙ ᎢᏗᎬᏤᎵᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏄᏩᎥ ᎢᎦ-ᎦᏛ ᏥᏄᏁᎩᏴ ᏄᏁᎩᏰᎢ.
011702.phonetic: A-le u-da-ne-tli-yv-se da-ni-ka-nv-i, a-le u-ka-dv di-gv-wa-lv-sa-da nu-li-s-ta-ne-i nv-do i-digv-tse-li-dv, a-le u-nu-wa-v i-ga--ga-dv tsi-nu-ne-gi-yv nu-ne-gi-ye-i.

011703.english: And, behold, there appeared unto them Moses and Elias talking with him.
011703.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎾᎿᎭᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᎬᏩᎾᏛᏁᎴ ᎼᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎳᏯ ᎬᏩᎵᏃᎮᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
011703.phonetic: A-le gv-ni-yu-quo na-hna gv-ni-ge-sv ni-gv-wa-na-dv-ne-le Mo-si a-le I-la-ya gv-wa-li-no-he-di-s-ge-i.

011704.english: Then answered Peter, and said unto Jesus, Lord, it is good for us to be here: if thou wilt, let us make here three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.
011704.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᏥᏌ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎣᏏᏳ ᎠᏂ ᏥᏕᏙᎭ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᏣᏰᎸᏅ, ᎠᏂ ᏦᎢ ᏱᏙᏥᎵᏦᏛᎦ, ᏌᏉ ᏂᎯ ᏣᏤᎵᎦ, ᏐᎢᏃ ᎼᏏ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᏐᎢᏃ ᎢᎳᏯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
011704.phonetic: Qui-da-no u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-le Tsi-sa; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, o-si-yu a-ni tsi-de-do-ha, i-yu-no o-si-yu yi-tsa-ye-lv-nv, a-ni tso-i yi-do-tsi-li-tso-dv-ga, sa-quo ni-hi tsa-tse-li-ga, so-i-no Mo-si u-tse-li-ga, so-i-no I-la-ya u-tse-li-ga.

011705.english: While he yet spake, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them: and behold a voice out of the cloud, which said, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased; hear ye him.
011705.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉᏃ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎨᎢ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎤᏍᎪᏍᏛ ᎤᎶᎩᎳ ᎤᎾᏓᏩᏗᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᎶᎩᎸ ᏧᏁᏤᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏅᏧᏪᏎᎢ; ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏇᏥ ᏥᎨᏳᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏥᏰᎸᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᏣᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ.
011705.phonetic: A-si-quo-no ga-wo-ni-s-ge-i, gv-ni-yu-quo u-s-go-s-dv u-lo-gi-la u-na-da-wa-di-s-ta-ne-i, a-le gv-ni-yu-quo na-hna u-lo-gi-lv tsu-ne-tse-i, Hi-a nv-tsu-we-se-i; hi-a na-s-gi A-que-tsi tsi-ge-yu-i, na-s-gi o-si-yu tsi-ye-lv-i, na-s-gi e-tsa-dv-da-s-da-ne-he-s-di.

011706.english: And when the disciples heard it, they fell on their face, and were sore afraid.
011706.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᏚᏂᏅᏤ ᏚᏂᏯᏍᏚᏎᎢ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᎴᎢ.
011706.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no u-na-dv-ga-nv du-ni-nv-tse du-ni-ya-s-du-se-i, u-tsa-ti u-ni-s-ga-le-i.

011707.english: And Jesus came and touched them, and said, Arise, and be not afraid.
011707.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᎷᏤ ᏚᏒᏂᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏗᏣᎴᎲᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏥᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ.
011707.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-lu-tse du-sv-ni-le-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i; Di-tsa-le-hv-ga, a-le tle-s-di yi-tsi-s-ga-i-he-s-di.

011708.english: And when they had lifted up their eyes, they saw no man, save Jesus only.
011708.cherokee: ᏚᎾᎧᎾᏅᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏳᏂᎪᎮ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏩᏒ.
011708.phonetic: Du-na-ka-na-nv-no v-tla gi-lo yu-ni-go-he Tsi-sa u-wa-sv.

011709.english: And as they came down from the mountain, Jesus charged them, saying, Tell the vision to no man, until the Son of man be risen again from the dead.
011709.cherokee: ᏧᏂᎦᏐᎠᏏᏗᏒᏃ ᎣᏓᎸᎢ ᏥᏌ ᏚᏅᏍᏓᏕᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎡᏥᏃᏁᎸᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏥᎪᏩᏛ ᎬᏂ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᏚᎴᏁᏍᏗ.
011709.phonetic: Tsu-ni-ga-so-a-si-di-sv-no o-da-lv-i tsi-sa du-nv-s-da-de-le-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i; Tle-s-di gi-lo e-tsi-no-ne-lv-ha na-s-gi tsi-tsi-go-wa-dv gv-ni yv-wi U-we-tsi u-yo-hu-sv du-le-ne-s-di.

011710.english: And his disciples asked him, saying, Why then say the scribes that Elias must first come?
011710.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎬᏩᏛᏛᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ, ᎢᎳᏯ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏓᎦᎷᏥ, ᏣᎾᏗᏍᎪᎢ?
011710.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no gv-wa-dv-dv-ne-i, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Ga-do-no di-no-we-li-s-gi, I-la-ya i-gv-yi da-ga-lu-tsi, tsa-na-di-s-go-i?

011711.english: And Jesus answered and said unto them, Elias truly shall first come, and restore all things.
011711.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎢᎳᏯ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏬᏢᎯᏐᏗ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ.
011711.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-ne-tsv hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; U-do-hi-yu-hi I-la-ya i-gv-yi u-lu-hi-s-di a-le u-wo-tlv-hi-so-di ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di.

011712.english: But I say unto you, That Elias is come already, and they knew him not, but have done unto him whatsoever they listed. Likewise shall also the Son of man suffer of them.
011712.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎢᎳᏯ ᎦᏳᎳ ᎤᎷᏨ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏱᎬᏬᎵᏦᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᎾᏚᎵᏍᎬᎢ ᏂᎬᏩᏁᎸ.  ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏁᎵ.
011712.phonetic: A-se-no hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; I-la-ya ga-yu-la u-lu-tsv, a-le v-tla yi-gv-wo-li-tso-i, a-se-no ni-ga-v u-na-du-li-s-gv-i ni-gv-wa-ne-lv.  Na-s-quo-s-gi-ni yv-wi U-we-tsi na-s-gi-ya nv-da-gv-wa-ne-li.

011713.english: Then the disciples understood that he spake unto them of John the Baptist.
011713.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᏃᎵᏤ ᏣᏂ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ ᎧᏁᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
011713.phonetic: Hna-quo-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi u-no-li-tse Tsa-ni Di-da-wo-s-gi ka-ne-i-s-di-s-gv-i.

011714.english: And when they were come to the multitude, there came to him a certain man, kneeling down to him, and saying,
011714.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᏁᏙᎲ ᎤᏂᎷᏨ, ᎤᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏚᎵᏂᏆᏅᏁᎸᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ;
011714.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-ni-tsa-ti a-ne-do-hv u-ni-lu-tsv, u-lu-tse-lv-gi a-s-ga-ya du-li-ni-qua-nv-ne-lv-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi;

011715.english: Lord, have mercy on my son: for he is lunatick, and sore vexed: for ofttimes he falleth into the fire, and oft into the water.
011715.cherokee: ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎯᏯᏙᎵᎩ ᎠᏧᏣ ᎠᏇᏥ, ᎬᎵᏰᏃ ᎬᏩᎯᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎠᎩᎵᏲᎦ, ᏯᏃᎩᏳᏰᏃ ᎠᏥᎸᏱ ᎬᏛᎪᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎹᏱ ᎠᎬᎪᎢ.
011715.phonetic: Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, hi-ya-do-li-gi a-tsu-tsa a-que-tsi, gv-li-ye-no gv-wa-hi-ha, a-le u-tsa-ti a-gi-li-yo-ga, ya-no-gi-yu-ye-no a-tsi-lv-yi gv-dv-go-i a-le a-ma-yi a-gv-go-i.

011716.english: And I brought him to thy disciples, and they could not cure him.

011717.english: Then Jesus answered and said, O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him hither to me.
011717.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏂᏦᎯᏳᏒᎾ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᎦᏔᎲᏛ ᎪᎯ ᏥᏤᎭ, ᎢᎳᎪ ᏂᎪᎯᎴᏍᏗ ᎢᏨᏰᎳᏗᏙᎮᏍᏗ?  ᎢᎳᎪ ᏂᎪᎯᎴᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᏁᎳᎩ ᎢᏨᏰᎵᏎᎮᏍᏗ?  ᏍᎩᏯᏘᏃᎯᏏ.
011717.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Ni-tso-hi-yu-sv-na a-le i-tsa-ga-ta-hv-dv go-hi tsi-tse-ha, i-la-go ni-go-hi-le-s-di i-tsv-ye-la-di-do-he-s-di?  I-la-go ni-go-hi-le-s-di u-na-ne-la-gi i-tsv-ye-li-se-he-s-di?  S-gi-ya-ti-no-hi-si.

011718.english: And Jesus rebuked the devil; and he departed out of him: and the child was cured from that very hour.
011718.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᏍᎦᏨᎩ; ᎠᏍᎩᎾᏃ ᎤᏄᎪᏨᎩ ᎠᏧᏣ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᎯᏳᏉ ᎤᏗᏩᏒᎩ.
011718.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no a-s-gi-na u-s-ga-tsv-gi; a-s-gi-na-no u-nu-go-tsv-gi a-tsu-tsa, a-le na-hi-yu-quo u-di-wa-sv-gi.

011719.english: Then came the disciples to Jesus apart, and said, Why could not we cast him out?
011719.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᏦᎩᏄᎸᏅᎩ ᎣᏥᏄᎪᏫᏍᏗᏱ?
011719.phonetic: Hna-quo-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi Tsi-sa gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-gi u-nv-sv ge-sv-i, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Ga-do-no tso-gi-nu-lv-nv-gi o-tsi-nu-go-wi-s-di-yi?

011720.english: And Jesus said unto them, Because of your unbelief: for verily I say unto you, If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this mountain, Remove hence to yonder place; and it shall remove; and nothing shall be impossible unto you.
011720.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᏂᏦᎯᏳᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏱᏦᎯᏳᎭ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏥᎸᏉ ᎤᎦᏔ ᎢᎩᏛ, ᎯᎠ ᏱᏂᏥᏪᏏ ᎯᎠ ᏦᏓᎸ, ᎭᏓᏅᎾ ᎢᏢ ᏫᎶᎯ, ᎠᎭ ᎤᏓᏅᏍᏗ; ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏥᏄᎸᎲᏍᎦ.
011720.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha ni-tso-hi-yu-sv-na ge-sv-i.  U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya-ye-no hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; I-yu-no yi-tso-hi-yu-ha na-s-quo a-tsi-lv-quo u-ga-ta i-gi-dv, hi-a yi-ni-tsi-we-si hi-a tso-da-lv, ha-da-nv-na i-tlv wi-lo-hi, a-ha u-da-nv-s-di; v-tla a-le go-hu-s-di yi-ge-tsi-nu-lv-hv-s-ga.

011721.english: Howbeit this kind goeth not out but by prayer and fasting.
011721.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏄᎪᎢᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ, ᎬᏂ ᏴᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᎭ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎹᏟ ᏯᎬᏍᎦ.
011721.phonetic: Hi-a-s-gi-ni-no-nv i-yu-s-di v-tla u-nu-go-i-s-di yi-gi, gv-ni yv-da-do-li-s-di-ha a-le a-ma-tli ya-gv-s-ga.

011722.english: And while they abode in Galilee, Jesus said unto them, The Son of man shall be betrayed into the hands of men:
011722.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏃ ᎨᎵᎵ ᎠᏁᏙᎲᎢ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏴᏫ ᏙᏓᎨᏥᏲᎯᏎᎵ,
011722.phonetic: A-si-no Ge-li-li a-ne-do-hv-i Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; yv-wi U-we-tsi yv-wi do-da-ge-tsi-yo-hi-se-li,

011723.english: And they shall kill him, and the third day he shall be raised again. And they were exceeding sorry.
011723.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᎬᏩᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᏦᎢᏁ ᎢᎦ ᏙᏛᎠᎴᏂ.  ᎤᏣᏔᏅᎯᏃ ᎤᏲ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏅᎩ.
011723.phonetic: A-le na-s-gi da-gv-wa-li, a-le tso-i-ne i-ga do-dv-a-le-ni.  U-tsa-ta-nv-hi-no u-yo u-ni-ye-lv-nv-gi.

011724.english: And when they were come to Capernaum, they that received tribute money came to Peter, and said, Doth not your master pay tribute?
011724.cherokee: ᎨᏆᏂᏃ ᎤᏂᎷᏨ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏂᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴ ᏈᏓ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᏝᏍᎪ ᏗᏤᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᎠᏰᎵ ᏯᎫᏱᏍᎪᎢ?
011724.phonetic: Ge-qua-ni-no u-ni-lu-tsv a-ye-li a-de-lv a-ni-gi-si-do-hi gv-wa-lu-tse-le Qui-da, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Tla-s-go di-tse-yo-hv-s-gi a-ye-li ya-gu-yi-s-go-i?

011725.english: He saith, Yes. And when he was come into the house, Jesus prevented him, saying, What thinkest thou, Simon? of whom do the kings of the earth take custom or tribute? of their own children, or of strangers?
011725.cherokee: ᎥᎥ, ᎤᏛᏁᎢ.  ᎠᏓᏁᎸᏃ ᎤᎷᏨ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏲᏍᏙᏓᏁᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᏙ ᎮᎵᎭ, ᏌᏩᏂ; ᎦᎪ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᎾᎫᏴᎡᎰ ᎤᏂᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᏁᎯ; ᏧᏁᏥᏍᎪ, ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴᎨ?
011725.phonetic: V-v, u-dv-ne-i.  A-da-ne-lv-no u-lu-tsv Tsi-sa u-yo-s-do-da-ne-lv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Ga-do he-li-ha, Sa-wa-ni; ga-go a-ye-li gv-wa-na-gu-yv-e-ho u-ni-gv-wi-yu-hi e-lo-hi a-ne-hi; tsu-ne-tsi-s-go, nv-wa-na-da-le-ge?

011726.english: Peter saith unto him, Of strangers. Jesus saith unto him, Then are the children free.
011726.cherokee: ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴᎢ, ᎤᏓᏙᏎᎸᎩ ᏈᏓ.  ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏧᏁᏥᏍᎩᏂᎨᏃ ᎤᎾᏁᎳᎩᏉ.
011726.phonetic: Nv-wa-na-da-le-i, u-da-do-se-lv-gi Qui-da.  Tsi-sa-no hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsu-ne-tsi-s-gi-ni-ge-no u-na-ne-la-gi-quo.

011727.english: Notwithstanding, lest we should offend them, go thou to the sea, and cast an hook, and take up the fish that first cometh up; and when thou hast opened his mouth, thou shalt find a piece of money: that take, and give unto them for me and thee.
011727.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏗᏂᎿᎭᎸᏙᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎥᏓᎵ ᎮᎾ, ᎠᏑᏗᏃ ᏫᏣᏓᎡᏒᎭ, ᎢᎬᏱᏱᏃ ᎯᏴᎩᏛ ᎠᏣᏗ ᎯᏁᏒᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᏍᏚᎢᏒ ᎠᎰᎵ ᎠᏰᎵ-ᎠᏕᎸ ᏘᏩᏛᎯ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎩᏒᎭ ᎠᎴ ᏕᎯᏁᎸᎭ ᎠᏴ ᎩᎾᎫᏴᏗ.
011727.phonetic: A-se-no di-ni-hna-lv-do-s-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na, v-da-li he-na, a-su-di-no wi-tsa-da-e-sv-ha, i-gv-yi-yi-no hi-yv-gi-dv a-tsa-di hi-ne-sv-ha, a-le hi-s-du-i-sv a-ho-li a-ye-li--a-de-lv ti-wa-dv-hi; na-s-gi hi-gi-sv-ha a-le de-hi-ne-lv-ha a-yv gi-na-gu-yv-di.

011801.english: At the same time came the disciples unto Jesus, saying, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?
011801.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳ ᏥᏌ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎦᎪ ᎤᏟᎠ ᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
011801.phonetic: Na-hi-yu Tsi-sa gv-wa-lu-tse-lv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; Ga-go u-tli-a tsi-lv-quo-di-yu na-hna ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i.

011802.english: And Jesus called a little child unto him, and set him in the midst of them,
011802.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎠᏲᎵ ᏭᏯᏅᎲ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎠᏂᏅ ᎤᏪᎧᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ;
011802.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no a-yo-li wu-ya-nv-hv a-ye-li a-ni-nv u-we-ka-nv-gi, a-le hi-a nu-we-sv-gi;

011803.english: And said, Verily I say unto you, Except ye be converted, and become as little children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.
011803.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏂᏣᏓᏁᏟᏴᏒᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᏥᏄᎾᏍᏗ ᏂᏣᎵᏍᏔᏅᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎥᏝ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏴᎨᏥᏴᎭ.
011803.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; I-yu-no ni-tsa-da-ne-tli-yv-sv-na i-ge-se-s-di, a-le di-ni-yo-li tsi-nu-na-s-di ni-tsa-li-s-ta-nv-na i-ge-se-s-di, v-tla ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv yv-ge-tsi-yv-ha.

011804.english: Whosoever therefore shall humble himself as this little child, the same is greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
011804.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏲᎵ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏟ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
011804.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di gi-lo u-da-do-li-s-di ge-se-s-di na-s-gi-ya hi-a a-yo-li, na-s-gi u-tli a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu ge-se-s-di na-hna ga-lv-la-di e-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i.

011805.english: And whoso shall receive one such little child in my name receiveth me.
011805.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᏓᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ ᏌᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏲᎵ ᏓᏆᏙᎥ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏴ ᏓᏆᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ.
011805.phonetic: A-le gi-lo da-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di sa-quo na-s-gi hi-a i-yu-s-di a-yo-li da-qua-do-v nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di, a-yv da-qua-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di.

011806.english: But whoso shall offend one of these little ones which believe in me, it were better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and that he were drowned in the depth of the sea.
011806.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂ ᏕᎪᏕᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏌᏉ ᎯᎠ ᏧᎾᏍᏗᎦ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏉᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ, ᎤᏟ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎠᏍᏙᏍᎩ ᏅᏯ ᏯᏥᏯᏝᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᏛᎬ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᏯᏥᎬᏴ.
011806.phonetic: Gi-lo-s-gi-ni de-go-de-s-di-s-ge-s-di sa-quo hi-a tsu-na-s-di-ga ge-sv na-s-gi gv-quo-hi-yu-hv-s-gi, u-tli o-si-yu a-s-do-s-gi nv-ya ya-tsi-ya-tla-nv, a-le a-s-dv-gv a-me-quo-hi ya-tsi-gv-yv.

011807.english: Woe unto the world because of offences! for it must needs be that offences come; but woe to that man by whom the offence cometh!
011807.cherokee: ᎤᏂᎢᏳ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᏗᎾᏓᏙᏕᏍᏗᏍᎩ, ᎠᏎᏰᏃ ᏗᎾᏓᏙᏕᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᎾᏄᎪᎢᏍᏗ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏲᎢᏳ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏗᏓᏙᏕᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏫᏒᎯ.
011807.phonetic: U-ni-i-yu i-yu-li-s-da-ne-di ge-se-s-di e-lo-hi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha di-na-da-do-de-s-di-s-gi, a-se-ye-no di-na-da-do-de-s-di-s-gi u-ni-na-nu-go-i-s-di; a-se-no u-yo-i-yu i-yu-li-s-da-ne-di na-s-gi na a-s-ga-ya di-da-do-de-s-di-s-gi u-na-nu-go-wi-sv-hi.

011808.english: Wherefore if thy hand or thy foot offend thee, cut them off, and cast them from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life halt or maimed, rather than having two hands or two feet to be cast into everlasting fire.
011808.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᏃ ᏦᏰᏂ ᎠᎴ ᏣᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏕᏦᏕᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎯᏍᏆᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏫᏣᏕᎨᏍᏗ; ᎤᏟ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏦᏰᏂ ᎠᎴ ᏣᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏣᏲᎱᏎᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ ᎬᏂᏛ ᏗᎨᏒ ᏫᏱᏣᏴᎸ, ᎠᏃ ᎢᏧᎳ ᏱᏘᏰᏌᏓ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏧᎳ ᏗᏣᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏫᏰᏣᏓᎢᏅ ᏂᎬᏠᏍᎬᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏥᎸᏱ.
011808.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-yu-no tso-ye-ni a-le tsa-la-si-de-ni de-tso-de-s-di-s-ge-s-di, hi-s-qua-li-s-ge-s-di a-le wi-tsa-de-ge-s-di; u-tli o-si-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di tso-ye-ni a-le tsa-la-si-de-ni tsa-yo-hu-se-lv-hi yi-gi gv-ni-dv di-ge-sv wi-yi-tsa-yv-lv, a-no i-tsu-la yi-ti-ye-sa-da a-le i-tsu-la di-tsa-la-si-de-ni wi-ye-tsa-da-i-nv ni-gv-tlo-s-gv-na ge-sv a-tsi-lv-yi.

011809.english: And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire.
011809.cherokee: ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᎯᎦᏙᎵ ᏕᏦᏕᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎭᏓᎦᏖᏙᎢᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏫᏣᏕᎨᏍᏗ; ᎤᏟ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏌᏉ ᏱᎧᎿᎭᎬᏂᏛ ᏗᎨᏒ ᏫᏱᏣᏴᎸ, ᎠᏃ ᎢᏧᎳ ᏱᏘᎧᎿᎭᏨᏍᎩᏃᎢ ᎠᏥᎸᏱ ᏫᏰᏣᏓᎢᏅ.
011809.phonetic: I-yu a-le hi-ga-do-li de-tso-de-s-di-s-ge-s-di, ha-da-ga-te-do-i-s-ge-s-di a-le wi-tsa-de-ge-s-di; u-tli o-si-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di sa-quo yi-ka-hna gv-ni-dv di-ge-sv wi-yi-tsa-yv-lv, a-no i-tsu-la yi-ti-ka-hna tsv-s-gi-no-i a-tsi-lv-yi wi-ye-tsa-da-i-nv.

011810.english: Take heed that ye despise not one of these little ones; for I say unto you, That in heaven their angels do always behold the face of my Father which is in heaven.
011810.cherokee: ᎢᏤᏯᏔᎮᏍᏗ ᏥᎦᏰᏥᏐᏢᏔᏂ ᏌᏉ ᎯᎠ ᏧᎾᏍᏗᎦ; ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏦᏒ ᏧᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎠᏂᎪᏩᏘᏍᎪ ᎤᎧᏛ ᎡᏙᏓ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ.
011810.phonetic: I-tse-ya-ta-he-s-di tsi-ga-ye-tsi-so-tlv-ta-ni sa-quo hi-a tsu-na-s-di-ga; hi-a-ye-no ni-tsv-we-se-ha; Ga-lv-la-di tso-sv tsu-na-tse-li-ga di-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi ni-go-hi-lv a-ni-go-wa-ti-s-go u-ka-dv E-do-da ga-lv-la-di e-hi.

011811.english: For the Son of man is come to save that which was lost.

011812.english: How think ye? if a man have an hundred sheep, and one of them be gone astray, doth he not leave the ninety and nine, and goeth into the mountains, and seeketh that which is gone astray?
011812.cherokee: ᎦᏙ ᎢᏤᎵᎭ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᎠᏫ ᏱᏚᎾᏝᎠ, ᏌᏉᏃ ᏳᎴᏲᎡᎸ, ᏝᏍᎪ ᏐᎣᏁᎳᏍᎪᎯ ᏐᎣᏁᎳᎦᎵ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᏱᏗᎬᏕᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏱᏗᎧᎾᎷᏏᏙᎰ ᎦᏚᏏ ᏳᏲᎰ ᎤᎴᏲᎡᎸᎯ?
011812.phonetic: Ga-do i-tse-li-ha; I-yu-no gi-lo a-s-ga-ya a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui a-wi yi-du-na-tla-a, sa-quo-no yu-le-yo-e-lv, tla-s-go so-o-ne-la-s-go-hi so-o-ne-la-ga-li i-ya-ni-dv yi-di-gv-de-go-i, a-le yi-di-ka-na-lu-si-do-ho ga-du-si yu-yo-ho u-le-yo-e-lv-hi?

011813.english: And if so be that he find it, verily I say unto you, he rejoiceth more of that sheep, than of the ninety and nine which went not astray.
011813.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᏩᏛ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎢᏨᏲᏎᎭ, ᎤᏟ ᏂᎦᎣ ᎠᎵᎮᎵᎪ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏐᎣᏁᎳᏍᎪᎯ ᏐᎣᏁᎳᎦᎵ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᏄᎾᎴᏲᎥᎾ.
011813.phonetic: I-yu-no a-wa-dv, u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya i-tsv-yo-se-ha, u-tli ni-ga-o a-li-he-li-go na-s-gi ge-sv i-yu-s-di, e-s-ga-quo so-o-ne-la-s-go-hi so-o-ne-la-ga-li i-ya-ni-dv nu-na-le-yo-v-na.

011814.english: Even so it is not the will of your Father which is in heaven, that one of these little ones should perish.
011814.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏚᎵ ᎢᏥᏙᏓ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ, ᏌᏉ ᎯᎠ ᏧᎾᏍᏗᎦ ᎨᏒ ᏧᏂᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
011814.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo v-tla yu-du-li I-tsi-do-da ga-lv-la-di e-hi, sa-quo hi-a tsu-na-s-di-ga ge-sv tsu-ni-yo-hu-hi-s-di-yi.

011815.english: Moreover if thy brother shall trespass against thee, go and tell him his fault between thee and him alone: if he shall hear thee, thou hast gained thy brother.
011815.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᏗᏍᏓᏓᏅᏟ ᎢᏣᏍᎦᏅᏤᎮᏍᏗ, ᎮᎨᏍᏗ ᎯᏃᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎢᏍᏛᏒᏉ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎢᏣᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ, ᎯᏩᏛᎮᏍᏗ ᏗᏍᏓᏓᏅᏟ.
011815.phonetic: I-yu-no di-s-da-da-nv-tli i-tsa-s-ga-nv-tse-he-s-di, he-ge-s-di hi-no-ne-he-s-di u-s-ga-nv-tsv i-s-dv-sv-quo ge-sv-i; i-yu-no i-tsa-dv-da-s-da-ne-he-s-di, hi-wa-dv-he-s-di di-s-da-da-nv-tli.

011816.english: But if he will not hear thee, then take with thee one or two more, that in the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may be established.
011816.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂᏃ ᏂᏣᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᎲᎾᏉ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᏕᎭᏘᏁᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᏂᏃᎮᏍᎬ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏦᎢ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎯ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏣᏁᏨ ᎠᏍᏓᏲᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
011816.phonetic: I-yu-s-gi-ni-no ni-tsa-dv-da-s-da-ne-hv-na-quo i-ge-se-s-di, a-si-yv-wi a-le a-ni-ta-li de-ha-ti-ne-ge-s-di, na-s-gi-no a-ni-no-he-s-gv a-ni-ta-li a-le a-ni-tso-i a-ni-ga-ta-hi ni-ga-dv tsa-ne-tsv a-s-da-yo-s-ge-s-di.

011817.english: And if he shall neglect to hear them, tell it unto the church: but if he neglect to hear the church, let him be unto thee as an heathen man and a publican.
011817.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᏓᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᎲᎾᏉ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᎯᏃᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ; ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂᏃ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᏂᏓᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᎲᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏅᏩᏓᎴᏉ ᏴᏫ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏰᎵᏉ-ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎯᏯᏓᏅᏖᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
011817.phonetic: I-yu-no na-s-gi ni-da-dv-da-s-da-ne-hv-na-quo i-ge-se-s-di tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv hi-no-hv-s-ge-s-di; i-yu-s-gi-ni-no tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv ni-da-dv-da-s-da-ne-hv-na i-ge-se-s-di, nv-wa-da-le-quo yv-wi a-le a-ye-li-quo--a-de-lv a-gi-si-do-hi na-s-gi-ya hi-ya-da-nv-te-s-ge-s-di.
