012719.english: When he was set down on the judgment seat, his wife sent unto him, saying, Have thou nothing to do with that just man: for I have suffered many things this day in a dream because of him.
012719.cherokee: ᎤᏬᎸᏃ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎦᏍᎩᎸᎢ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ ᏧᏓᏅᏎᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏅᏧᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏂᎯ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ Ꮎ ᎾᏍᎦᏅᎾ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᏱᏗᏣᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎴᏍᏗ, ᎤᏣᏘᏰᏃ ᏥᎩᎵᏲᎩ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᎦᏍᎩᏓᏍᎬᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
012719.phonetic: U-wo-lv-no di-gu-go-do-di-yi ga-s-gi-lv-i u-da-li-i tsu-da-nv-se-i, hi-a nv-tsu-we-se-le-i; Tle-s-di ni-hi go-hu-s-di na na-s-ga-nv-na a-s-ga-ya u-gv-wa-li yi-di-tsa-lv-wi-s-da-ne-le-s-di, u-tsa-ti-ye-no tsi-gi-li-yo-gi go-hi i-ga ga-s-gi-da-s-gv-i na-s-gi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-i.

012720.english: But the chief priests and elders persuaded the multitude that they should ask Barabbas, and destroy Jesus.
012720.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ ᏚᏂᏍᏗᏰᏔᏁ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒ ᏆᎳᏆ ᎤᏂᏔᏲᏎᏗᏱ, ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
012720.phonetic: A-se-no nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi a-le di-ni-la-wi-gi du-ni-s-di-ye-ta-ne u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv Qua-la-qua u-ni-ta-yo-se-di-yi, Tsi-sa-no u-ni-hi-s-di-yi.

012721.english: The governor answered and said unto them, Whether of the twain will ye that I release unto you? They said, Barabbas.
012721.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎦᎪ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏣᏚᎵ ᏗᏨᏲᎯᏎᏗᏱ?  ᏆᎳᏆ, ᎤᎾᏛᏁᎢ.
012721.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no u-ne-tsv hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; Ga-go hi-a a-ni-ta-li ge-sv i-tsa-du-li di-tsv-yo-hi-se-di-yi?  Qua-la-qua, u-na-dv-ne-i.

012722.english: Pilate saith unto them, What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ? They all say unto him, Let him be crucified.
012722.cherokee: ᏆᎴᏗ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᏓᏥᏴᏁᎵ ᏥᏌ, ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏣᏃᏎᎰᎢ?  ᏩᎦᏛᎥᎦ, ᎬᏬᏎᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ.
012722.phonetic: Qua-le-di hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; Ga-do-no da-tsi-yv-ne-li Tsi-sa, Ga-lo-ne-dv tsa-no-se-ho-i?  Wa-ga-dv-v-ga, gv-wo-se-le ni-ga-dv.

012723.english: And the governor said, Why, what evil hath he done? But they cried out the more, saying, Let him be crucified.
012723.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ, ᎦᏙ ᎤᏛᏁᎸ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨ?  ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏟᏉ ᏂᎦᎡ ᎤᏁᎷᏁᎢ, ᏩᎦᏛᎥᎦ, ᎠᎾᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
012723.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ga-do-no, ga-do u-dv-ne-lv u-s-ga-nv-tsv?  A-se-no u-tli-quo ni-ga-e u-ne-lu-ne-i, wa-ga-dv-v-ga, a-na-di-s-gv-i.

012724.english: When Pilate saw that he could prevail nothing, but that rather a tumult was made, he took water, and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, I am innocent of the blood of this just person: see ye to it.
012724.cherokee: ᏆᎴᏗ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᏗᎬᏩᏎᎪᎩᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏓᏑᏰᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᏔᎸᏅᎩ, ᎠᎹ ᏬᏁᎩᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏑᎴᎮ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎠᏴ ᎥᏝ ᎠᎩᏍᎦᏅᏨᎯ ᏱᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᎦᏅᏨᎾ ᏴᏫ ᎤᎩᎬ.  ᏂᎯ ᎢᏣᎦᏌᏯᏍᏓ.
012724.phonetic: Qua-le-di u-do-le-ho-sv di-gv-wa-se-go-gi-s-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i, a-le u-na-da-su-ye-dv u-na-li-ta-lv-nv-gi, a-ma wo-ne-gi-se-i, a-le du-su-le-he u-ni-tsa-ti a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i; A-yv v-tla a-gi-s-ga-nv-tsv-hi yi-gi hi-a nu-s-ga-nv-tsv-na yv-wi u-gi-gv.  Ni-hi i-tsa-ga-sa-ya-s-da.

012725.english: Then answered all the people, and said, His blood be on us, and on our children.
012725.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏂᏁᏤᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎤᎩᎬ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎴ ᏦᎨᏥ ᎣᎩᏅᎦᎴᏍᏗ.
012725.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no yv-wi u-ni-ne-tse-i, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; U-gi-gv a-yv a-le tso-ge-tsi o-gi-nv-ga-le-s-di.

012726.english: Then released he Barabbas unto them: and when he had scourged Jesus, he delivered him to be crucified.
012726.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏆᎳᏆ ᏕᎤᏲᎯᏎᎴᎢ; ᎤᎵᎥᏃᏅᏃ ᏥᏌ ᏚᏲᏎ ᎠᎦᏛᏗᏱ.
012726.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Qua-la-qua de-u-yo-hi-se-le-i; u-li-v-no-nv-no Tsi-sa du-yo-se a-ga-dv-di-yi.

012727.english: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the common hall, and gathered unto him the whole band of soldiers.
012727.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏧᏤᎵᎦ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎦᏁᎸ ᎤᏂᏴᏔᏁ ᏥᏌ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿ ᎭᏕᎬᏩᏚᏫᏍᏔᏁ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ.
012727.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi u-gv-wi-yu-hi tsu-tse-li-ga u-gv-wi-yu-hi ga-ne-lv u-ni-yv-ta-ne Tsi-sa, a-le na-hna de-gv-wa-du-wi-s-ta-ne ni-ga-dv su-na-da-tlu-gi a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi.

012728.english: And they stripped him, and put on him a scarlet robe.

012729.english: And when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon his head, and a reed in his right hand: and they bowed the knee before him, and mocked him, saying, Hail, King of the Jews!
012729.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᏕᏲᏅ ᎠᎵᏍᏚᎶ ᏧᏣᏲᏍᏗ ᎪᏢᏔᏅᎯ, ᎤᏂᏍᏚᎳᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎾᏍᏓ ᎠᎦᏘᏏ ᎢᏗᏢ ᎤᎾᏒᎦᎳᏗᏍᏔᏁᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏓᎾᎵᏂᏆᏅᏁᎮ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏕᎰᏗᏍᎨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᎢᏨᏲᎵᎦ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ.
012729.phonetic: A-le u-ni-s-de-yo-nv a-li-s-du-lo tsu-tsa-yo-s-di go-tlv-ta-nv-hi, u-ni-s-du-la-ne-i, a-le ga-na-s-da a-ga-ti-si i-di-tlv u-na-sv-ga-la-di-s-ta-ne-i; a-le da-na-li-ni-qua-nv-ne-he a-le a-na-de-ho-di-s-ge-i, hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i; I-tsv-yo-li-ga, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi A-ni-tsu-si u-na-tse-li-ga.

012730.english: And they spit upon him, and took the reed, and smote him on the head.
012730.cherokee: ᏚᎾᎵᏥᏇᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏴᎡᎴ ᎦᎾᏍᏓ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᎸᏂᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
012730.phonetic: Du-na-li-tsi-que-no a-le u-ni-yv-e-le ga-na-s-da a-le u-ni-s-qua-lv-ni-s-ta-ne-i.

012731.english: And after that they had mocked him, they took the robe off from him, and put his own raiment on him, and led him away to crucify him.
012731.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏕᎰᏔᏃᏅᏃ ᎤᏂᏄᏪᏎ ᎦᏌᎴᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏧᏄᏪ ᏚᏂᏄᏬᎡᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏛᏗᏱ ᏭᎾᏘᏅᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
012731.phonetic: U-na-de-ho-ta-no-nv-no u-ni-nu-we-se ga-sa-le-na, a-le u-wa-sv tsu-nu-we du-ni-nu-wo-e-i; a-le u-na-dv-di-yi wu-na-ti-nv-s-ta-ne-i.

012732.english: And as they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name: him they compelled to bear his cross.
012732.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏨᏃ ᎤᏂᏩᏛᎮ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏌᎵᏂ ᎡᎯ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏍᎦᎢᏍᏓᎩ ᏄᏅᏁᎴ ᎤᏪᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᏧᏓᎿᎭᏩᏛ.
012732.phonetic: U-ni-nu-go-tsv-no u-ni-wa-dv-he a-s-ga-ya Sa-li-ni e-hi Sa-wa-ni tsu-do-i-dv; na-s-gi gv-s-ga-i-s-da-gi nu-nv-ne-le u-we-nv-s-di-yi tsu-da-hna-wa-dv.

012733.english: And when they were come unto a place called Golgotha, that is to say, a place of a skull,
012733.cherokee: ᏭᏂᎷᏨᏃ ᎪᎵᎦᏓ ᏚᏙᎥᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏍᏆᎷᎪᎢ ᏥᎦᏛᎦ,
012733.phonetic: Wu-ni-lu-tsv-no Go-li-ga-da du-do-v-i, na-s-gi u-s-qua-lu-go-i tsi-ga-dv-ga,

012734.english: They gave him vinegar to drink mingled with gall: and when he had tasted thereof, he would not drink.
012734.cherokee: ᏧᏂᏦᏯᏍᏗ ᎤᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎠᏓᎦ ᎠᏑᏱ ᎤᏂᏁᏁᎴᎢ, ᎤᏁᎸᏔᏅᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏚᎸᎮ ᎤᏗᏔᏍᏗᏱ.
012734.phonetic: Tsu-ni-tso-ya-s-di u-di-ta-s-di a-da-ga a-su-yi u-ni-ne-ne-le-i, u-ne-lv-ta-nv-no v-tla yu-du-lv-he u-di-ta-s-di-yi.

012735.english: And they crucified him, and parted his garments, casting lots: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, They parted my garments among them, and upon my vesture did they cast lots.
012735.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏛᏁᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᏯᏙᎴ ᏧᏄᏬ ᎠᎾᏌᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏁ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᏧᏁᏤᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏗᏆᏄᏬ ᏚᏂᏯᏙᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏆᏄᏬ ᎤᎾᏌᏍᏔᏅᎩ.
012735.phonetic: U-na-dv-ne-no a-le du-ni-ya-do-le tsu-nu-wo a-na-sa-s-di-s-ge-i; na-s-gi-no u-do-hi-yu-ne a-do-le-ho-s-gi tsu-ne-tse-i, hi-a tsi-nu-we-se-i; Di-qua-nu-wo du-ni-ya-do-lv-gi, a-le a-qua-nu-wo u-na-sa-s-ta-nv-gi.

012736.english: And sitting down they watched him there;

012737.english: And set up over his head his accusation written, THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
012737.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏯᎸᏁᏃ ᎦᎸᎳᏗᏢ ᎤᎵᏍᏓᏛᎢ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎵᏱᎵᏕᎲᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏁ ᎪᏪᎴᎢ; ᎯᎠᏲ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ.
012737.phonetic: U-ni-ya-lv-ne-no ga-lv-la-di-tlv u-li-s-da-dv-i i-yu-s-di ge-sv u-li-yi-li-de-hv-i, hi-a ni-gv-ne go-we-le-i; Hi-A-Yo Tsi-Sa U-Gv-Wi-Yu-Hi A-Ni-Tsu-Si U-Na-Tse-Li-Ga.

012738.english: Then were there two thieves crucified with him, one on the right hand, and another on the left.
012738.cherokee: ᎾᏍᏉᏃ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏕᎨᎦᏛᏁ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎠᏂᏃᏍᎩᏍᎩ, ᏌᏉ ᎠᎦᏘᏏ ᎢᏗᏢ, ᏐᎢᏃ ᎠᎦᏍᎦᏂ ᎢᏗᏢ.
012738.phonetic: Na-s-quo-no i-tsu-la-ha de-ge-ga-dv-ne a-ni-ta-li a-ni-no-s-gi-s-gi, sa-quo a-ga-ti-si i-di-tlv, so-i-no a-ga-s-ga-ni i-di-tlv.

012739.english: And they that passed by reviled him, wagging their heads,

012740.english: And saying, Thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest it in three days, save thyself. If thou be the Son of God, come down from the cross.
012740.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᎤᏛᎾ-ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎯᏲᏍᏗᏍᎩ, ᏦᎢᏉᏃ ᎢᎦ ᎯᏰᎢᎵᏙᎯ ᎭᏁᏍᎨᏍᎩ, ᎭᎵᏍᏕᎸ ᏨᏒ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏂᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏓᏓᎿᎭᏩᏍᏛ ᎡᎭᏠᎠᎯ.
012740.phonetic: Hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i; u-dv-na--di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi hi-yo-s-di-s-gi, tso-i-quo-no i-ga hi-ye-i-li-do-hi ha-ne-s-ge-s-gi, ha-li-s-de-lv tsv-sv; i-yu-no ni-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi ge-se-s-di, da-da-hna-wa-s-dv e-ha-tlo-a-hi.

012741.english: Likewise also the chief priests mocking him, with the scribes and elders, said,
012741.cherokee: ᎾᏍᏉᏃ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ ᎠᏂᏰᎵᏍᎬᎢ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ;
012741.phonetic: Na-s-quo-no nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv-a-ne-lo-hi a-le di-no-we-li-s-gi a-le di-ni-la-wi-gi a-ni-ye-li-s-gv-i hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i;

012742.english: He saved others; himself he cannot save. If he be the King of Israel, let him now come down from the cross, and we will believe him.
012742.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏐᎢ ᏓᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬᎩ, ᎤᏩᏒᏍᎩᏂ ᎥᏝ ᏴᎬᎵᏍᏕᎸ.  ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᏏᎵ ᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᏓᏓᎿᎭᏩᏍᏛ ᎡᎠᏠᎠᎯ, ᎩᎳᏃ ᏓᏰᏙᎢᏳᏂ.
012742.phonetic: A-ni-so-i da-s-de-li-s-gv-gi, u-wa-sv-s-gi-ni v-tla yv-gv-li-s-de-lv.  I-yu-no U-gv-wi-yu-hi I-si-li u-na-tse-li ge-se-s-di, hna-quo da-da-hna-wa-s-dv e-a-tlo-a-hi, gi-la-no da-ye-do-i-yu-ni.

012743.english: He trusted in God; let him deliver him now, if he will have him: for he said, I am the Son of God.
012743.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎵᏍᎦᏍᏙᏛᎩ; ᎿᎭᏉ ᏭᏍᏕᎸ ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᎸᏉᏕᏍᏗ; ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏥᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬᎩ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎡᏙᏓ.
012743.phonetic: U-ne-la-nv-hi u-li-s-ga-s-do-dv-gi; hna-quo wu-s-de-lv i-yu-no u-lv-quo-de-s-di; hi-a-ye-no tsi-ni-ga-we-s-gv-gi, U-ne-la-nv-hi E-do-da.

012744.english: The thieves also, which were crucified with him, cast the same in his teeth.
012744.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏃᏍᎩᏍᎩᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏗᎨᎦᏛᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎦᎬᏩᏐᏢᏕᎢ.
012744.phonetic: A-ni-no-s-gi-s-gi-no na-s-gi i-tsu-la-ha di-ge-ga-dv-nv-hi na-s-quo ga-gv-wa-so-tlv-de-i.

012745.english: Now from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the land unto the ninth hour.
012745.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎢᎦ ᎤᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏦᎢᏁ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᎧᎳᏩᏗᏒ ᎢᏯᏍᏘ ᎤᏒᎯᏰᏱ ᎢᏗᏢ ᎤᎵᏏᎲᏒᎩ ᏂᎬ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏙᎯ.
012745.phonetic: Hna-quo-no i-ga u-da-le-nv-dv tso-i-ne i-yu-tli-lo-dv ka-la-wa-di-sv i-ya-s-ti u-sv-hi-ye-yi i-di-tlv u-li-si-hv-sv-gi ni-gv na-s-gi ga-do-hi.

012746.english: And about the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani? that is to say, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
012746.cherokee: ᏦᎢᏁᏃ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᎢᏴ ᎧᏔᏩᏗᏒ ᎤᏒᎯᏰᏱ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏤ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎢᎳ, ᎢᎳ, ᎳᎹ ᏌᏆᏓᏂ?  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏗ ᎦᏛᎬᎢ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏆᏤᎵᎦ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏆᏤᎵᎦ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᏍᏋᏕᏨᏉ?
012746.phonetic: Tso-i-ne-no i-yu-tli-lo-dv i-yv ka-ta-wa-di-sv u-sv-hi-ye-yi i-di-tlv Tsi-sa u-ne-tse a-s-da-ya, hi-a nu-we-se-i; I-la, I-la, la-ma sa-qua-da-ni?  Na-s-gi hi-a nu-s-di ga-dv-gv-i, U-ne-la-nv-hi a-qua-tse-li-ga, U-ne-la-nv-hi a-qua-tse-li-ga, ga-do-no i-s-quv-de-tsv-quo?

012747.english: Some of them that stood there, when they heard that, said, This man calleth for Elias.
012747.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏂᏙᎾᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ, ᎢᎳᏯ ᎠᏯᏂᎭ ᎯᎠ, ᎤᎾᏛᏁᎢ.
012747.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no na-hna a-ni-do-na-i na-s-gi u-na-dv-ga-nv, I-la-ya a-ya-ni-ha hi-a, u-na-dv-ne-i.

012748.english: And straightway one of them ran, and took a spunge, and filled it with vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink.
012748.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᏚᏍᏆᎸᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏭᏁᏎ ᏚᏬᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏂᏦᏯᏍᏗ ᎤᎧᎵᎢᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎦᎾᏍᏙᎯᏃ ᎤᏪᏆᏙᏔᏅ ᏭᏅᏁᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏗᏔᏍᏗ.
012748.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv a-si-yv-wi du-s-qua-lv-ta-ne-i, a-le wu-ne-se du-wo-li, a-le tsu-ni-tso-ya-s-di u-ka-li-i-s-ta-ne-i, ga-na-s-do-hi-no u-we-qua-do-ta-nv wu-nv-ne-le na-s-gi u-di-ta-s-di.

012749.english: The rest said, Let be, let us see whether Elias will come to save him.
012749.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎤᏁᎳᎩ, ᎢᏓᏙᎴᎰᎯ, ᏥᎪᏃ ᎢᎳᏯ ᏓᎦᎷᏥ ᏓᏳᏍᏕᎸᎯᎵ.
012749.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; U-ne-la-gi, i-da-do-le-ho-hi, tsi-go-no I-la-ya da-ga-lu-tsi da-yu-s-de-lv-hi-li.

012750.english: Jesus, when he had cried again with a loud voice, yielded up the ghost.
012750.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᎤᏁᏨ ᏕᎤᏲᏎ ᎤᏓᏅᏙ.
012750.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no ta-li-ne a-s-da-ya u-ne-tsv de-u-yo-se u-da-nv-do.

012751.english: And, behold, the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom; and the earth did quake, and the rocks rent;
012751.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎠᏄᏬ ᎠᏰᏙᎳᏛ ᎤᏛᎾ-ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏚᎵᏍᏡᏰᎢ, ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏧᎴᏅᎮ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏭᏍᏆᏗᏍᏔᏁᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙᎯ ᎤᎵᏖᎸᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏅᏯ ᏚᎾᎵᏍᏡᏰᎢ.
012751.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo-no a-nu-wo a-ye-do-la-dv u-dv-na--di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi du-li-s-tlu-ye-i, ga-lv-la-di tsu-le-nv-he e-la-di wu-s-qua-di-s-ta-ne-i; a-le ga-do-hi u-li-te-lv-ne-i, a-le nv-ya du-na-li-s-tlu-ye-i.

012752.english: And the graves were opened; and many bodies of the saints which slept arose,
012752.cherokee: ᏓᏤᎵᏍᏛᏃ ᏚᎵᏍᏚᎢᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᎤᏂᎵᏅᏨᎯ ᎤᎾᏗᏓᏁᏎᎢ,
012752.phonetic: Da-tse-li-s-dv-no du-li-s-du-i-se-i, a-le u-ni-tsa-ti u-na-da-nv-ti u-ni-li-nv-tsv-hi u-na-di-da-ne-se-i,

012753.english: And came out of the graves after his resurrection, and went into the holy city, and appeared unto many.
012753.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏓᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᏚᏂᏄᎪᏤ ᏧᎴᏅᎯ ᏂᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎤᏂᏴᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏚᎾᏛᏁᎴᎢ.
012753.phonetic: A-le da-tse-li-s-dv du-ni-nu-go-tse tsu-le-nv-hi ni-ge-se-i, a-le ga-lv-quo-di-yu ga-du-hv u-ni-yv-le-i, a-le u-ni-tsa-te gv-ni-ge-sv ni-du-na-dv-ne-le-i.

012754.english: Now when the centurion, and they that were with him, watching Jesus, saw the earthquake, and those things that were done, they feared greatly, saying, Truly this was the Son of God.
012754.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏥᏌ ᎬᏩᏯᏫᏍᎩ, ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎦᏙᎯ ᎠᎵᏖᎸᎲᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏂᏙᎸᎢ, ᎤᏣᏖ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎨᏎ ᎯᎠ.
012754.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-da-ti-ni-do-hi a-le na-s-gi na i-tsu-la-ha Tsi-sa gv-wa-ya-wi-s-gi, u-na-do-le-ho-sv ga-do-hi a-li-te-lv-hv-s-gv-i, a-le na-s-gi nu-li-s-ta-ni-do-lv-i, u-tsa-te u-ni-s-ga-le-i, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi ge-se hi-a.

012755.english: And many women were there beholding afar off, which followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering unto him:
012755.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎡ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ Ꭸ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏙᏗᏂᎧᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏌ ᎨᎵᎵ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏛ ᎬᏩᏍᏕᎸᎯᏙᎸᎯ,
012755.phonetic: Na-hna-no a-ni-do-na-e u-ni-tsa-te a-ni-ge-yv ge i-yv-dv do-di-ni-ka-ne-i, na-s-gi Tsi-sa Ge-li-li nv-da-gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-dv gv-wa-s-de-lv-hi-do-lv-hi,

012756.english: Among which was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of Zebedee's children.
012756.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᎤᎾᏓᏑᏰ ᎺᎵ ᎹᎩᏕᎵ ᎡᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎺᎵ ᏥᎻ ᎠᎴ ᏦᏏ ᎤᏂᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᏤᏈᏗ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎤᏂᏥ.
012756.phonetic: Na-hna u-na-da-su-ye Me-li Ma-gi-de-li e-hi, a-le Me-li Tsi-mi a-le Tso-si u-ni-tsi, a-le Tse-qui-di tsu-we-tsi u-ni-tsi.

012757.english: When the even was come, there came a rich man of Arimathaea, named Joseph, who also himself was Jesus' disciple:
012757.cherokee: ᎤᏒᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᎤᎷᏤ ᎤᏪᎿᎭᎢ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎵᎹᏗᏱ ᎡᎯ ᏦᏩ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎨᏎᎢ;
012757.phonetic: U-sv-no nu-li-s-ta-nv u-lu-tse u-we-hna-i a-s-ga-ya A-li-ma-di-yi e-hi Tso-wa tsu-do-i-dv, na-s-quo na-s-gi Tsi-sa u-s-da-wa-di-do-hi ge-se-i;

012758.english: He went to Pilate, and begged the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded the body to be delivered.
012758.cherokee: ᏆᎴᏗᏱ ᎤᏪᏅᏎᎢ, ᏥᏌ ᎠᏰᎸᎢ ᏭᏔᏲᎴᎢ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏆᎴᏗ ᎤᏁᏤ ᎠᏰᎸᎢ ᏧᏂᏲᎯᏎᏗᏱ.
012758.phonetic: Qua-le-di-yi u-we-nv-se-i, Tsi-sa a-ye-lv-i wu-ta-yo-le-i.  Hna-quo-no Qua-le-di u-ne-tse a-ye-lv-i tsu-ni-yo-hi-se-di-yi.

012759.english: And when Joseph had taken the body, he wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
012759.cherokee: ᏦᏩᏃ ᎠᏰᎸ ᎤᏁᏒ ᎤᏪᏣᏄᎶᏔᏁ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᏄᏬ ᎤᏁᎬ;
012759.phonetic: Tso-wa-no a-ye-lv u-ne-sv u-we-tsa-nu-lo-ta-ne ga-da-ha ni-ge-sv-na a-nu-wo u-ne-gv;

012760.english: And laid it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn out in the rock: and he rolled a great stone to the door of the sepulchre, and departed.
012760.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏅᏁ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎢᏤ ᏗᏓᏂᏐᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲᎯ ᎤᏔᎴᏒᎢ; ᎡᏆᏃ ᏅᏯ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᏭᏪᏌᏆᎴᎸ ᎤᏓᏅᏎᎢ.
012760.phonetic: A-le u-nv-ne u-wa-sv u-tse-li i-tse di-da-ni-so-di-yi, na-s-gi u-yo-hi u-ta-le-sv-i; e-qua-no nv-ya ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi a-tse-li-s-dv wu-we-sa-qua-le-lv u-da-nv-se-i.

012761.english: And there was Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting over against the sepulchre.
012761.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ ᏗᏓᏂᏐᏗᏱ ᏚᏃᎴ ᎺᎵ ᎹᎩᏕᎵ ᎡᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏐᎢ ᎺᎵ.
012761.phonetic: Na-hna-no i-gv-yi-di-tlv di-da-ni-so-di-yi du-no-le Me-li Ma-gi-de-li e-hi a-le so-i Me-li.

012762.english: Now the next day, that followed the day of the preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees came together unto Pilate,
012762.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᎩᏨᏛ, ᎠᏛᏅᎢᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎦ ᎤᎶᏐᏅᎯ, ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᏕᎬᏩᎳᏫᏤ ᏆᎴᏗ,
012762.phonetic: A-le u-gi-tsv-dv, a-dv-nv-i-s-do-di-yi i-ga u-lo-so-nv-hi, nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi a-le A-ni-qua-li-si de-gv-wa-la-wi-tse Qua-le-di,

012763.english: Saying, Sir, we remember that that deceiver said, while he was yet alive, After three days I will rise again.
012763.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎣᎦᏅᏔ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᏓᎶᏄᎮᏍᎩ, ᎠᏏᏉ ᏥᎬᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏦᎢᏁᏉ ᎢᎦ ᏙᏛᎦᎴᏂ.
012763.phonetic: Hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, o-ga-nv-ta na-s-gi na a-da-lo-nu-he-s-gi, a-si-quo tsi-gv-nv-gi, hi-a tsi-nu-we-sv-gi; Tso-i-ne-quo i-ga do-dv-ga-le-ni.

012764.english: Command therefore that the sepulchre be made sure until the third day, lest his disciples come by night, and steal him away, and say unto the people, He is risen from the dead: so the last error shall be worse than the first.
012764.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎯᏁᎩ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᎢᏳᏅᏁᏗᏱ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛᎢ ᏦᎢᏁ ᎢᎦ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ, ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏰᏃ ᏒᏃᏱ ᏯᏂᎷᎩ ᏱᎬᏩᏃᏍᎩ, ᎯᎠᏃ ᏱᏂᏓᏂᏪᏏ ᏴᏫ; ᏧᏲᎱᏒ ᏙᎤᎴᏅ; ᎣᏂᏱᏃ ᎤᎾᏠᏄᎮᎸᎢ ᎤᏟ ᎤᏲᎢᏳ ᏱᎩ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ.
012764.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di hi-ne-gi a-s-da-ya i-yu-nv-ne-di-yi a-tse-li-s-dv-i tso-i-ne i-ga i-go-hi-dv, gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-ye-no sv-no-yi ya-ni-lu-gi yi-gv-wa-no-s-gi, hi-a-no yi-ni-da-ni-we-si yv-wi; Tsu-yo-hu-sv do-u-le-nv; o-ni-yi-no u-na-tlo-nu-he-lv-i u-tli u-yo-i-yu yi-gi e-s-ga-quo i-gv-yi-yi.

012765.english: Pilate said unto them, Ye have a watch: go your way, make it as sure as ye can.
012765.cherokee: ᏆᎴᏗ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᏕᏥᎧᎭ, ᎢᏤᎾ ᎾᏍᏓᏴ ᎢᎨᏨᏁᏗ ᎨᎡ ᏫᏂᏨᎦ.
012765.phonetic: Qua-le-di hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; A-ni-ya-wi-s-gi de-tsi-ka-ha, i-tse-na na-s-da-yv i-ge-tsv-ne-di ge-e wi-ni-tsv-ga.

012766.english: So they went, and made the sepulchre sure, sealing the stone, and setting a watch.
012766.cherokee: ᎰᏩᏃ ᎤᏁᏅᏎ ᎠᏍᏓᏱᏳ ᏫᏄᏅᏁᎴ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛᎢ, ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏔᏁ ᏅᏲᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᎧᏁ ᎠᏂᎦᏘᏗᏍᎩ.
012766.phonetic: Ho-wa-no u-ne-nv-se a-s-da-yi-yu wi-nu-nv-ne-le a-tse-li-s-dv-i, u-ni-ye-lv-ta-ne nv-yo-hi, a-le du-ni-ka-ne a-ni-ga-ti-di-s-gi.

012801.english: In the end of the sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week, came Mary Magdalene and the other Mary to see the sepulchre.
012801.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏉᏅᎯᏃ, ᎩᎳ ᎢᎦ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ ᎠᎴᏂᏍᎬ ᏑᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎺᎵ ᎹᎩᏕᎵ ᎡᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏐᎢ ᎺᎵ ᎤᎾᎦᏔᏂᎴ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛᎢ.
012801.phonetic: U-na-do-da-quo-nv-hi-no, gi-la i-ga ni-ga-li-s-di-s-ge-i a-le-ni-s-gv su-na-do-da-qua-s-di ge-sv Me-li Ma-gi-de-li e-hi a-le so-i Me-li u-na-ga-ta-ni-le a-tse-li-s-dv-i.

012802.english: And, behold, there was a great earthquake: for the angel of the Lord descended from heaven, and came and rolled back the stone from the door, and sat upon it.
012802.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎦᏙᎯ ᎤᎵᏖᎸᏁᎢ, ᏱᎰᏩᏰᏃ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏧᏠᎠᏎᎢ, ᎤᎷᏨᏃ ᏅᏯ ᎤᏪᏌᏆᎴᎴ ᎤᎲᏎ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎲᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᎩᎳᏁᎢ.
012802.phonetic: A-le gv-ni-yu-quo u-tsa-ti ga-do-hi u-li-te-lv-ne-i, Yi-ho-wa-ye-no u-tse-li di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi ga-lv-la-di tsu-tlo-a-se-i, u-lu-tsv-no nv-ya u-we-sa-qua-le-le u-hv-se ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi a-hv-i; a-le u-s-gi-la-ne-i.

012803.english: His countenance was like lightning, and his raiment white as snow:
012803.cherokee: ᏗᎧᏃᏗᏱ ᎠᎾᎦᎵᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎤᏄᏩᎥᏃ ᎥᏃᏥ ᏥᏄᏁᎩᏴ ᏄᏁᎩᏰᎢ.
012803.phonetic: Di-ka-no-di-yi a-na-ga-li-s-gi na-s-gi-ya ge-se-i, u-nu-wa-v-no v-no-tsi tsi-nu-ne-gi-yv nu-ne-gi-ye-i.

012804.english: And for fear of him the keepers did shake, and became as dead men.
012804.cherokee: ᎠᏂᎦᏘᏯᏃ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎲ ᏚᏂᎾᏪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏂᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏄᎾᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
012804.phonetic: A-ni-ga-ti-ya-no a-ni-s-ga-i-hv du-ni-na-we-i, a-le tsu-ni-yo-hu-sv-hi na-s-gi-ya nu-na-li-s-ta-ne-i.

012805.english: And the angel answered and said unto the women, Fear not ye: for I know that ye seek Jesus, which was crucified.
012805.cherokee: ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏂᎯ ᏱᏍᏗᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ, ᏥᎦᏔᎭᏰᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎠᎦᏛᏅᎯ ᎡᏍᏗᏲᎲᎢ.
012805.phonetic: Di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi-no u-ne-tsv hi-a ni-du-we-se-le a-ni-ge-yv; Tle-s-di ni-hi yi-s-di-s-ga-i-he-s-di, tsi-ga-ta-ha-ye-no Tsi-sa a-ga-dv-nv-hi e-s-di-yo-hv-i.

012806.english: He is not here: for he is risen, as he said. Come, see the place where the Lord lay.
012806.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᏂ ᏘᎦᎾ, ᏚᎴᏅᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏄᏪᏒᎢ; ᎡᏍᏕᎾ ᎡᏍᏓᎦᏔ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏥᏅᏅᎢ.
012806.phonetic: V-tla a-ni ti-ga-na, du-le-nv-ye-no na-s-gi-ya nu-we-sv-i; E-s-de-na e-s-da-ga-ta U-gv-wi-yu-hi a-tsi-nv-nv-i.

012807.english: And go quickly, and tell his disciples that he is risen from the dead; and, behold, he goeth before you into Galilee; there shall ye see him: lo, I have told you.
012807.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏍᏗᏳ ᎢᏍᏕᎾ ᏫᏗᏍᏗᏃᎲᏏ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏕᎤᎴᏅ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎨᎵᎵ ᏅᏓᏰᏏ, ᎾᎿᎭᏓᏰᏥᎪᎢ.  ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎢᏍᏛᏃᎲᏏ.
012807.phonetic: A-le u-li-s-di-yu i-s-de-na wi-di-s-di-no-hv-si gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi de-u-le-nv u-yo-hu-sv-i; a-le gv-ni-yu-quo i-gv-yi Ge-li-li nv-da-ye-si, na-hna da-ye-tsi-go-i.  Gv-ni-yu-quo i-s-dv-no-hv-si.

012808.english: And they departed quickly from the sepulchre with fear and great joy; and did run to bring his disciples word.
012808.cherokee: ᎤᎵᏍᏗᏳᏃ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏎᎢ, ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎮ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎠᎾᎵᎮᎵᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᏍᏆᎸᏔᏁ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏚᏂᏃᏁᎸᏎᎢ.
012808.phonetic: U-li-s-di-yu-no a-tse-li-s-dv u-na-da-nv-se-i, a-ni-s-ga-i-he a-le u-tsa-ti a-na-li-he-li-ge-i, a-le du-ni-s-qua-lv-ta-ne gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi du-ni-no-ne-lv-se-i.

012809.english: And as they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, All hail. And they came and held him by the feet, and worshipped him.
012809.cherokee: ᎠᎾᎢᏒᏃ ᏓᏂᏃᏁᎵᏒ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏥᏌ ᏚᎾᏠᏎᎢ; ᏙᎯᏱ ᏕᏓᏓᎪᏩᏛ, ᎤᏛᏁᎢ.  ᎤᏂᎷᏨᏃ ᏧᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏚᏂᏂᏴᎮᎢ, ᎡᎳᏗ ᏂᎬᏩᏛᏁᎴᎢ.
012809.phonetic: A-na-i-sv-no da-ni-no-ne-li-sv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi gv-ni-yu-quo Tsi-sa du-na-tlo-se-i; Do-hi-yi de-da-da-go-wa-dv, u-dv-ne-i.  U-ni-lu-tsv-no tsu-la-si-de-ni du-ni-ni-yv-he-i, e-la-di ni-gv-wa-dv-ne-le-i.

012810.english: Then said Jesus unto them, Be not afraid: go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.
012810.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏍᏗᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ; ᎢᏍᏕᎾ ᏫᏗᏍᏗᏃᎲᏏ ᎣᏣᎵᏅᏟ ᎨᎵᎵ ᏭᏂᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏓᎬᎩᎪᎯ.
012810.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Tle-s-di yi-s-di-s-ga-i-he-s-di; i-s-de-na wi-di-s-di-no-hv-si o-tsa-li-nv-tli Ge-li-li wu-ni-lo-hi-s-di-yi, na-hna-no da-gv-gi-go-hi.

012811.english: Now when they were going, behold, some of the watch came into the city, and shewed unto the chief priests all the things that were done.
012811.cherokee: ᎠᎾᎢᏒᏃ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎢᎦᏛ ᎤᏂᏯᏫᏒᎯ ᎤᏂᎷᏤ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᏚᏂᏃᏁᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏂᏙᎸᎢ.
012811.phonetic: A-na-i-sv-no gv-ni-yu-quo i-ga-dv u-ni-ya-wi-sv-hi u-ni-lu-tse ga-du-hv-i, a-le nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi du-ni-no-ne-le ni-ga-dv nu-li-s-ta-ni-do-lv-i.

012812.english: And when they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave large money unto the soldiers,
012812.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ ᏚᏂᎳᏫᏨ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᎠᏕᎸ ᏚᏂᏁᎴ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ,
012812.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no a-le di-ni-la-wi-gi du-ni-la-wi-tsv a-le u-ni-no-he-lv, u-tsa-ti a-de-lv du-ni-ne-le a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi,

012813.english: Saying, Say ye, His disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept.
012813.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎯᎠ ᏂᏥᏪᏒᎭ; ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏒᏃᏱ ᎤᏂᎷᏨᎩ, ᎣᏥᎵᏅ ᎬᏩᏃᏍᎩᏒᎩ.
012813.phonetic: Hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Hi-a ni-tsi-we-sv-ha; Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi sv-no-yi u-ni-lu-tsv-gi, o-tsi-li-nv gv-wa-no-s-gi-sv-gi.

012814.english: And if this come to the governor's ears, we will persuade him, and secure you.
012814.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏛᎦᏅ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏓᏲᏥᏍᏗᏰᏔᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏨᏍᏕᎸᎯ.
012814.phonetic: I-yu-no na-s-gi a-dv-ga-nv u-gv-wi-yu-hi, da-yo-tsi-s-di-ye-ta-ni, a-le da-tsv-s-de-lv-hi.

012815.english: So they took the money, and did as they were taught: and this saying is commonly reported among the Jews until this day.
012815.cherokee: ᏚᏂᎩᏒᏃ ᎠᏕᎸ, ᏂᎨᏥᏪᏎᎸ ᏄᎾᏛᏁᎴᎢ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎠᏏ ᎠᏂᏃᎮᎵᏙᎭ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ.
012815.phonetic: Du-ni-gi-sv-no a-de-lv, ni-ge-tsi-we-se-lv nu-na-dv-ne-le-i.  Na-s-gi-no hi-a ka-no-he-dv a-si a-ni-no-he-li-do-ha A-ni-tsu-si.

012816.english: Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, into a mountain where Jesus had appointed them.
012816.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏌᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎨᎵᎵ ᎤᏁᏅᏒᎩ ᏦᏓᎸ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎾᏎᎮᎸᎢ.
012816.phonetic: Hna-quo-no sa-du i-ya-ni-dv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi Ge-li-li u-ne-nv-sv-gi tso-da-lv Tsi-sa u-na-se-he-lv-i.

012817.english: And when they saw him, they worshipped him: but some doubted.
012817.cherokee: ᎬᏩᎪᎲᏃ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏂᎬᏩᏛᏁᎸᎩ.  ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᎦᏛ ᎤᎾᏜᏓᏏᏛᎡᎸᏉ.
012817.phonetic: Gv-wa-go-hv-no e-la-di ni-gv-wa-dv-ne-lv-gi.  A-se-no i-ga-dv u-na-dla-si-dv-e-lv-quo.

012818.english: And Jesus came and spake unto them, saying, All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth.
012818.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᎷᏨ ᏚᏬᏁᏔᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎥᎩᏁᎸ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏗᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎡᎶᎯ.
012818.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-lu-tsv du-wo-ne-ta-nv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; V-gi-ne-lv ni-ga-v di-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi ga-lv-la-di a-le e-lo-hi.

012819.english: Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:
012819.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏤᎾ ᏫᏗᏤᏲᎲᎦ ᎾᎦᏛ ᏄᎾᏓᎴᏒ ᏴᏫ, ᏕᏣᏬᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎢᏨᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏚᏙᎥ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᎴ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ,
012819.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-tse-na wi-di-tse-yo-hv-ga na-ga-dv nu-na-da-le-sv yv-wi, de-tsa-wo-s-ge-s-di i-tsv-di-s-ge-s-di du-do-v A-ga-yv-li-ge-i a-le U-we-tsi a-le Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do,

012820.english: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you always, even unto the end of the world. Amen.
012820.cherokee: ᏕᏤᏲᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏧᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎢᏨᏁᏤᎸᎢ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎢᏨᏰᎳᏗᏙᎭ ᏫᎬᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᎩ ᎡᎶᎯ.  ᎡᎺᏅ.
012820.phonetic: De-tse-yo-hv-s-ge-s-di tsu-ni-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di-yi ni-ga-v i-tsv-ne-tse-lv-i; gv-ni-yu-quo-no ni-go-hi-lv i-tsv-ye-la-di-do-ha wi-gv-li-s-qua-di-s-gi e-lo-hi.  E-me-nv.
